heart-failure.title: Insuficiência cardíaca
heart-failure.overview: A insuficiência cardíaca é uma condição crônica e progressiva na qual o músculo cardíaco é incapaz de bombear sangue suficiente para atender às necessidades do corpo em sangue e oxigênio. O coração tenta compensar pela dilatação, hipertrofia e aumento da frequência cardíaca, mas esses mecanismos eventualmente falham.
clinicalPearls: Pese o paciente diariamente no mesmo horário
Monitore a ingestão e a eliminação com precisão
Eduque sobre a dieta com baixo teor de sódio
A digoxina requer verificação do pulso antes da administração
Eleve a cabeceira da cama a 45 graus para ortopneia

hypertension.title: Hipertensão
hypertension.overview: A hipertensão é uma elevação persistente da pressão arterial sistêmica. É um fator de risco importante para doenças cardiovasculares, AVC e doença renal. A maioria dos pacientes é assintomática, daí seu apelido de 'assassino silencioso'.
clinicalPearls: Ensine a técnica correta de medição da PA
Os medicamentos devem ser tomados de forma consistente
Monitore os efeitos colaterais dos anti-hipertensivos
Incentive as modificações do estilo de vida

respiratory-assessment.title: Avaliação respiratória
respiratory-assessment.overview: A avaliação respiratória é uma competência fundamental de enfermagem que inclui inspeção, palpação, percussão e ausculta. Uma avaliação respiratória completa é essencial para identificar problemas respiratórios e intervir de forma apropriada.

copd-basics-rpn.title: Fundamentos da DPOC
copd-basics-rpn.overview: A doença pulmonar obstrutiva crônica (DPOC) é um grupo de doenças pulmonares progressivas que obstruem o fluxo de ar e dificultam a respiração. As duas formas principais são a bronquite crônica e o enfisema.

asthma-basics-rpn.title: Fundamentos da asma
asthma-basics-rpn.overview: A asma é uma doença inflamatória crônica das vias aéreas caracterizada por hiperreatividade brônquica, broncoespasmo, edema da mucosa e produção excessiva de muco.

diabetes-type-1.title: Diabetes tipo 1
diabetes-type-1.overview: O diabetes tipo 1 é uma doença autoimune na qual o sistema imunológico destrói as células beta produtoras de insulina do pâncreas. Isso resulta em uma deficiência absoluta de insulina, exigindo terapia com insulina por toda a vida.

diabetes-type-2.title: Diabetes tipo 2
diabetes-type-2.overview: O diabetes tipo 2 é um distúrbio metabólico caracterizado por resistência à insulina e deficiência relativa de insulina. É a forma mais comum de diabetes e está fortemente associado à obesidade e ao estilo de vida sedentário.

pneumonia-basics-rpn.title: Fundamentos da pneumonia
pneumonia-basics-rpn.overview: A pneumonia é uma infecção inflamatória dos pulmões que afeta principalmente os alvéolos. Pode ser causada por bactérias, vírus ou fungos e é classificada como adquirida na comunidade ou nosocomial.

sepsis-prioritization.title: Priorização da sepse
sepsis-prioritization.overview: A sepse é uma resposta sistêmica potencialmente fatal à infecção que pode levar à falência de órgãos e morte se não tratada rapidamente. O reconhecimento precoce e a intervenção rápida são essenciais.

iv-therapy-basics.title: Fundamentos da terapia IV
iv-therapy-basics.overview: A terapia intravenosa (IV) é a administração de fluidos, medicamentos ou nutrição diretamente na corrente sanguínea através de uma veia. É um dos procedimentos invasivos mais comuns no ambiente hospitalar.

mi-management-np.title: STEMI: Molecular & Pharmacology
mi-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de STEMI: Molecular & Pharmacology, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of premature CAD (male < 55, female < 65)
clinicalPearls: Door-to-Balloon time < 90 mins
Door-to-Needle time < 30 mins (if no PCI)
Beta-blockers reduce remodeling and arrhythmias (start within 24h)

shock-syndromes-np.title: Shock: Hemodynamic Monitoring
shock-syndromes-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Shock: Hemodynamic Monitoring, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Passive Leg Raise (PLR) is the best predictor of fluid responsiveness
Avoid fluid overload in Cardiogenic shock (worsens pulmonary edema)
Target MAP of 65 mmHg for end-organ perfusion

sepsis-mastery-np.title: Sepsis: Cytokine Storm & SOFA
sepsis-mastery-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sepsis: Cytokine Storm & SOFA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Start broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour (Surviving Sepsis)
30mL/kg crystalloid bolus for hypotension/lactate > 4
Reassess volume status frequently (dynamic measures preferred)

siadh-di-np.title: Sodium Disorders: Osmoregulation
siadh-di-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sodium Disorders: Osmoregulation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Small cell lung cancer (SIADH: ectopic ADH)
clinicalPearls: Osmotic Demyelination Syndrome (ODS) is the risk of correcting chronic hyponatremia too fast
Check Serum Osmolality to confirm hypotonicity first
Vaptans (Tolvaptan) block V2 receptors in SIADH

aaa-rupture-np.title: AAA: Pathogenesis & Management
aaa-rupture-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de AAA: Pathogenesis & Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Permissive hypotension (SBP 70-90) until control is achieved to prevent clot blowout
Endovascular Aneurysm Repair (EVAR) vs Open
Control pain/anxiety to reduce sympathetic surge

dka-hhns-np.title: DKA/HHS: Anion Gap & Osmolality
dka-hhns-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de DKA/HHS: Anion Gap & Osmolality, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Close the Anion Gap, don't just fix the glucose
Add Dextrose to fluids when Glucose < 200 to prevent hypoglycemia while clearing ketones
Cerebral edema risk in children if Osm drops too fast

increased-icp-np.title: ICP: Cerebral Perfusion Pressure
increased-icp-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de ICP: Cerebral Perfusion Pressure, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Target CPP > 60 mmHg
Hyperventilation (CO2 30-35) is a temporary bridge (vasoconstriction reduces ICP but risks ischemia)
Keep HOB 30 degrees, head midline

copd-exacerbation-np.title: COPD: Cellular Mechanisms
copd-exacerbation-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de COPD: Cellular Mechanisms, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Permissive Hypercapnia: Tolerate high CO2 if pH > 7.25
Avoid intubation if possible (difficult to wean)
Target SpO2 88-92% to maintain hypoxic drive

aki-management-np.title: AKI: RIFLE Criteria & Dialysis
aki-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de AKI: RIFLE Criteria & Dialysis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Sepsis (most common cause of AKI in ICU)
clinicalPearls: Indications for Dialysis (AEIOU): Acidosis, Electrolytes (K+), Intoxication, Overload, Uremia
FeNa < 1% = Pre-renal; FeNa > 2% = ATN
Avoid nephrotoxins (NSAIDs, ACEi, Contrast)

tumor-lysis-np.title: Tumor Lysis Syndrome: Uric Acid Crisis
tumor-lysis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tumor Lysis Syndrome: Uric Acid Crisis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Elevated pre-treatment LDH and uric acid
clinicalPearls: Aggressive hydration (3L/m2/day) is the cornerstone of prevention
Keep urine pH > 7.0 (alkalinization controversy)
Monitor Calcium x Phosphate product > 60 (Risk of calcification)

transfusion-reactions-np.title: Transfusion Reactions: Hemolytic vs Febrile
transfusion-reactions-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Transfusion Reactions: Hemolytic vs Febrile, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: STOP the transfusion immediately for any reaction
Hemolytic reaction causes AKI via hemoglobin precipitation in tubules
Anaphylactic reaction is due to IgA deficiency (anti-IgA antibodies)

wound-vac-np.title: Negative Pressure Wound Therapy
wound-vac-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Negative Pressure Wound Therapy, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Foam must be cut to fit exactly (don't overlap intact skin)
Maintain airtight seal (use drape)
Stop if frank bleeding occurs

hellp-syndrome-np.title: HELLP Syndrome: Hepatic Cascade
hellp-syndrome-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de HELLP Syndrome: Hepatic Cascade, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Delivery is the definitive treatment regardless of gestational age
Mississippi classification grades severity by platelet nadir
Monitor for DIC and hepatic rupture: life-threatening complications

amniotic-fluid-embolism-np.title: Amniotic Fluid Embolism: DIC Pathway
amniotic-fluid-embolism-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Amniotic Fluid Embolism: DIC Pathway, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Diagnosis of exclusion: no definitive diagnostic test exists
Immediate ACLS with left uterine displacement is critical
Perimortem cesarean delivery within 4-5 minutes if cardiac arrest occurs

eclampsia-np.title: Eclampsia: Endothelial Dysfunction
eclampsia-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Eclampsia: Endothelial Dysfunction, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Mag toxicity antidote is Calcium Gluconate 1g IV
sFlt-1/PlGF ratio is emerging as a predictive biomarker for preeclampsia
Seizure prophylaxis with MgSO4 continues 24-48 hours postpartum

obstetric-hemorrhage-np.title: Obstetric Hemorrhage: Massive Transfusion
obstetric-hemorrhage-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Obstetric Hemorrhage: Massive Transfusion, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Shock Index (HR/SBP) is more sensitive than vital signs alone in pregnancy
Bakri balloon provides uterine tamponade for atony
B-Lynch compression suture is a surgical option before hysterectomy
Peripartum hysterectomy is definitive for life-threatening hemorrhage

neonatal-rds-np.title: RDS: Surfactant Physiology
neonatal-rds-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RDS: Surfactant Physiology, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: INSURE technique: Intubate-Surfactant-Extubate to minimize ventilator exposure
LISA/MIST (Less Invasive Surfactant Administration) avoids intubation entirely
Target SpO2 90-95% to reduce ROP risk while avoiding hypoxia

neonatal-hie-np.title: HIE: Therapeutic Hypothermia
neonatal-hie-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de HIE: Therapeutic Hypothermia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Cooling must begin within 6 hours of birth for neuroprotective benefit
TOBY, CoolCap, and NICHD trials established evidence for therapeutic hypothermia
Rewarming must be gradual at 0.5°C per hour to prevent rebound seizures

persistent-pulm-htn-np.title: PPHN: Nitric Oxide Pathway
persistent-pulm-htn-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de PPHN: Nitric Oxide Pathway, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Avoid cold stress and acidosis: both significantly worsen pulmonary vascular resistance
Use gentle ventilation strategy with permissive hypercapnia to avoid lung injury
ECMO is indicated for refractory cases with Oxygenation Index (OI) > 40

neonatal-abstinence-np.title: NAS: Opioid Withdrawal Scoring
neonatal-abstinence-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NAS: Opioid Withdrawal Scoring, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Eat-Sleep-Console (ESC) model is replacing Finnegan scoring at many centers: focuses on function rather than individual symptoms
Rooming-in with mother significantly improves outcomes and reduces pharmacotherapy need
Breastfeeding is encouraged if mother is in a stable medication-assisted treatment program

central-line-np.title: Central Line Insertion & Bundle
central-line-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Central Line Insertion & Bundle, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Subclavian has lowest CLABSI but highest pneumothorax risk
Confirm tip position at cavoatrial junction on CXR
Change dressings every 7 days (transparent) or 2 days (gauze)
Use antimicrobial-impregnated catheters for high-risk patients

lumbar-puncture-np.title: Lumbar Puncture & CSF Analysis
lumbar-puncture-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lumbar Puncture & CSF Analysis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Absolute contraindication: increased ICP with mass lesion (CT first!)
Post-procedure: lie flat 4-6 hours, increase fluids, monitor for headache/neuro changes
Traumatic tap vs SAH: RBCs decrease from tube 1 to 4 in traumatic tap

abg-sampling-np.title: ABG Sampling & Interpretation
abg-sampling-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de ABG Sampling & Interpretation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Apply pressure for 5 minutes post-puncture (10 min if on anticoagulants)
Sample on ice and analyze within 30 minutes
Compensatory changes never overcorrect
Always correlate ABG with clinical picture

mechanical-vent-np.title: Mechanical Ventilation Management
mechanical-vent-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Mechanical Ventilation Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ARDSNet protocol (low Vt 6mL/kg, titrate PEEP/FiO2 table)
Daily spontaneous breathing trial (SBT) assessment for extubation readiness
RASS sedation scale (-5 to +4) target -2 to 0 for most patients
Rapid shallow breathing index (f/Vt <105 predicts successful extubation)

normal-sinus-rhythm.title: Normal Sinus Rhythm (NSR)
normal-sinus-rhythm.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Normal Sinus Rhythm (NSR), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Understanding NSR is the baseline for identifying all arrhythmias
Rate outside 60-100 bpm with normal morphology may indicate sinus bradycardia or sinus tachycardia
Every P wave should be followed by a QRS complex in 1:1 ratio
PR interval should be consistent at 0.12-0.20 seconds
QRS complex should be narrow at less than 0.12 seconds
clinicalPearls: Every ECG interpretation starts with: Is there a P wave before every QRS? Is the rhythm regular? What is the rate?
PR interval greater than 0.20 sec = first-degree heart block, even if otherwise normal
The 6-second method: count R waves in a 6-second strip and multiply by 10 for rate
A rhythm can look normal but still be clinically significant — always assess the patient

sinus-bradycardia.title: Sinus Bradycardia
sinus-bradycardia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sinus Bradycardia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inferior wall myocardial infarction (right coronary artery supplies SA node)
Beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker therapy
Vagal stimulation (vomiting, bearing down, carotid massage)
clinicalPearls: Symptomatic vs asymptomatic determines treatment — never treat the monitor, treat the patient
Athletes commonly have resting heart rates of 40-50 bpm and this is normal for them
Inferior MI commonly causes bradycardia because the right coronary artery supplies the SA node in 60% of people
Atropine will NOT work in transplanted hearts (no vagal innervation) — go directly to pacing or chronotropic agents
Cushing triad (bradycardia + hypertension + irregular respirations) = increased ICP emergency

sinus-tachycardia.title: Sinus Tachycardia
sinus-tachycardia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sinus Tachycardia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Fever (heart rate increases approximately 10 bpm per degree Celsius elevation)
clinicalPearls: Sinus tachycardia is a SYMPTOM, not a primary diagnosis — always find the cause
The heart rate increases approximately 10 bpm for every 1 degree Celsius rise in temperature
Persistent unexplained sinus tachycardia in a post-surgical patient should raise suspicion for PE, hemorrhage, or sepsis
Sinus tachycardia has gradual onset/offset; SVT starts and stops abruptly — this is a key differentiator
Never cardiovert sinus tachycardia — it will not work and delays treatment of the real problem

atrial-fibrillation-rn.title: Atrial Fibrillation (A-Fib)
atrial-fibrillation-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Atrial Fibrillation (A-Fib), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: A-fib is the most common sustained arrhythmia — you will see it constantly in clinical practice
Irregularly irregular = A-fib until proven otherwise
The biggest danger of A-fib is STROKE, not the rhythm itself — anticoagulation is critical
CHA2DS2-VASc score determines anticoagulation need: CHF, Hypertension, Age 75+, Diabetes, Stroke/TIA history, Vascular disease, Age 65-74, Sex category (female)
Holiday Heart: binge drinking can trigger A-fib in patients with no cardiac history
Never cardiovert A-fib of unknown duration without anticoagulation or TEE — risk of embolizing an atrial thrombus

atrial-flutter-rn.title: Atrial Flutter
atrial-flutter-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Atrial Flutter, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Often coexists with or converts to atrial fibrillation
clinicalPearls: If the ventricular rate is exactly 150 bpm, think atrial flutter with 2:1 block until proven otherwise
Sawtooth flutter waves are best seen in leads II, III, aVF — if you don't see them, try changing leads
Adenosine will NOT convert flutter to NSR, but it will briefly increase AV block to unmask flutter waves for diagnosis
Atrial flutter and A-fib often coexist — a patient may alternate between the two
Catheter ablation has a greater than 90% success rate for typical atrial flutter and is often considered first-line definitive therapy

svt-recognition.title: Supraventricular Tachycardia (SVT)
svt-recognition.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Supraventricular Tachycardia (SVT), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Young, otherwise healthy individuals (most common demographic)
clinicalPearls: SVT is abrupt onset/offset ('like a light switch'). Sinus tachycardia is gradual ('like a dimmer switch'). This history is diagnostic.
The modified Valsalva maneuver (bearing down for 15 seconds then lying flat with legs elevated) has the highest vagal success rate
Adenosine's half-life is less than 10 seconds — it MUST be given as the fastest IV push possible followed by immediate flush
If adenosine reveals atrial flutter or A-fib instead of converting, you've made the diagnosis — it's not SVT
Never give adenosine, diltiazem, or verapamil to wide-complex tachycardia that might be VT — these drugs can cause cardiovascular collapse

pvc-recognition.title: Premature Ventricular Contractions (PVCs)
pvc-recognition.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Premature Ventricular Contractions (PVCs), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The most important PVC-related emergency is R-on-T phenomenon — a PVC landing on the T wave (vulnerable period) can trigger VT or VF
Bigeminy = every other beat is a PVC; Trigeminy = every third beat is a PVC; Couplet = two consecutive PVCs
Three or more consecutive PVCs at a rate greater than 100 = ventricular tachycardia
Hypokalemia is the most common reversible cause of PVCs — always check K+ first
Multifocal PVCs (different QRS morphologies) are more concerning than unifocal PVCs because they indicate multiple irritable foci
Compensatory pause: the interval from the beat before the PVC to the beat after the PVC equals two normal R-R intervals

vtach-management.title: Ventricular Tachycardia (VT)
vtach-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ventricular Tachycardia (VT), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prior myocardial infarction (scar tissue creates re-entry circuits — most common cause)
Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
clinicalPearls: The SINGLE most important question when you see VT: Does the patient have a pulse? This determines everything.
Pulseless VT = defibrillation (unsynchronized). VT with pulse and unstable = synchronized cardioversion. The energy type differs.
Wide complex tachycardia should be treated as VT until proven otherwise — it is safer to treat SVT with aberrancy as VT than to miss real VT
AV dissociation on a 12-lead ECG is virtually diagnostic of VT over SVT with aberrancy
Amiodarone is mixed in D5W only — it precipitates in normal saline
Post-cardiac arrest: all survivors of VT/VF arrest should be evaluated for ICD (implantable cardioverter-defibrillator) placement

vfib-management.title: Ventricular Fibrillation (V-Fib)
vfib-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ventricular Fibrillation (V-Fib), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Commotio cordis (blunt chest trauma during vulnerable period)
clinicalPearls: V-fib = shock immediately. Every minute of delay reduces survival by 7-10%. Nothing else matters until you defibrillate.
V-fib is UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation (there is no organized rhythm to synchronize with)
High-quality CPR between shocks is as important as the shocks themselves — it maintains coronary perfusion
Fine V-fib may look like asystole — always confirm rhythm in 2 leads before calling asystole
The H's and T's of reversible causes: Hypovolemia, Hypoxia, Hydrogen ion (acidosis), Hypo/Hyperkalemia, Hypothermia, Tension pneumothorax, Tamponade, Toxins, Thrombosis (pulmonary/coronary)
Post-resuscitation: targeted temperature management (32-36°C for 24 hours) improves neurological outcomes

heart-block-complete.title: Third-Degree (Complete) Heart Block
heart-block-complete.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Third-Degree (Complete) Heart Block, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inferior MI (AV nodal block — often transient)
Anterior MI (infranodal block — usually permanent and worse prognosis)
Degenerative conduction disease (Lenegre disease, Lev disease)
Drug toxicity (digoxin, beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, amiodarone)
Congenital complete heart block (associated with maternal lupus antibodies)
clinicalPearls: Third-degree heart block = NO relationship between P waves and QRS. The PR interval varies randomly. This is different from first-degree (prolonged but consistent PR) and second-degree (some blocked P waves).
Narrow escape QRS = junctional escape (above the block), rate 40-60. Wide escape QRS = ventricular escape (below the block), rate 20-40. Ventricular escape is more dangerous.
Anterior MI causing complete heart block has a much worse prognosis than inferior MI because it indicates massive septal damage
Transcutaneous pacing is painful — the patient needs sedation and analgesia if conscious
Always verify MECHANICAL capture (palpable pulse) in addition to electrical capture (pacer spikes followed by QRS on monitor)
Atropine works on the AV node — if the block is below the AV node (infranodal), atropine will increase atrial rate but NOT improve ventricular conduction

torsades-management.title: Torsades de Pointes (TdP)
torsades-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Torsades de Pointes (TdP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Drug-induced QT prolongation (most common cause): antiarrhythmics (sotalol, procainamide, quinidine), antibiotics (fluoroquinolones, macrolides, azole antifungals), antipsychotics (haloperidol, ziprasidone), methadone, ondansetron
Concurrent use of multiple QT-prolonging drugs
clinicalPearls: TdP is the ONE ventricular arrhythmia where amiodarone is CONTRAINDICATED — it prolongs QT and will make TdP worse
Magnesium is the treatment for TdP even if the serum magnesium level is normal — it works by stabilizing the membrane regardless of levels
The short-long-short initiation sequence is classic: a PVC (short interval), compensatory pause (long interval), then another PVC during the long QT that triggers TdP
Always review the medication list when you see a prolonged QT — drug-induced QT prolongation is the most common cause of TdP
QTc greater than 500 msec is the danger zone — report immediately and hold QT-prolonging drugs
Overdrive pacing shortens the QT interval by not allowing the prolonged repolarization to occur

asystole-pea.title: Asystole and Pulseless Electrical Activity (PEA)
asystole-pea.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Asystole and Pulseless Electrical Activity (PEA), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prolonged cardiac arrest (V-fib degenerating to asystole)
The H's: Hypovolemia, Hypoxia, Hydrogen ion (acidosis), Hypo/Hyperkalemia, Hypothermia
The T's: Tension pneumothorax, Tamponade (cardiac), Toxins, Thrombosis (pulmonary or coronary)
clinicalPearls: Asystole and PEA are NON-SHOCKABLE. Defibrillation will not help and wastes time.
PEA is the rhythm that FOOLS you — the monitor looks normal but the patient has no pulse. ALWAYS check a pulse.
End-tidal CO2 (ETCO2) is the best indicator of CPR quality. Target greater than 10 mmHg. A sudden rise in ETCO2 during CPR may indicate return of spontaneous circulation.
The most common cause of PEA is hypovolemia — consider massive fluid bolus
Confirm asystole in 2 leads. Fine V-fib looks like asystole and IS shockable. A lead disconnect also shows a flat line.
Atropine is NO LONGER recommended for asystole/PEA (removed from ACLS guidelines)

head-to-toe-rpn.title: Head-to-Toe Assessment (RPN/LVN)
head-to-toe-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Head-to-Toe Assessment (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incomplete assessment leading to missed findings
Altered level of consciousness limiting subjective data
clinicalPearls: Always perform the assessment in the same systematic order to avoid missing findings: consistency prevents errors.
Compare bilaterally: Always compare one side to the other (lung sounds, pulses, pupils, strength, sensation). Asymmetry is often more significant than individual findings.
For abdominal assessment, remember: Inspect → Auscultate → Percuss → Palpate. Auscultate BEFORE palpation because touching the abdomen can alter bowel sounds.
Document using approved terminology: 'Lungs CTA bilaterally' (clear to auscultation), 'Abdomen NTND' (non-tender, non-distended), 'A&Ox4' (alert and oriented to person, place, time, situation).
Cluster assessments: Perform all interventions during one visit to minimize patient disruption and maximize rest periods. However, never rush an assessment: thoroughness prevents missed findings.

vital-signs-assessment.title: Vital Signs: Complete Assessment Guide
vital-signs-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Vital Signs: Complete Assessment Guide, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Count respirations without the patient knowing: if they are aware, they may consciously alter their breathing pattern. Count while appearing to take the pulse (keep fingers on wrist).

Orthostatic (postural) vital signs: Measure BP and HR lying → sitting → standing (wait 1-3 min between positions). A drop of ≥20 mmHg systolic, ≥10 mmHg diastolic, or HR increase ≥20 bpm is positive for orthostatic hypotension.
Widening pulse pressure (systolic rises, diastolic falls) with bradycardia = Cushing's triad: late sign of increased ICP. This is a medical emergency.
Never delegate the first assessment of vital signs to unlicensed personnel: the RPN/RN must establish the baseline.
Pain is subjective: the patient's self-report is the most reliable indicator. Use age-appropriate scales: NRS (0-10) for adults, Wong-Baker FACES for children, FLACC for nonverbal patients.

pain-assessment-rpn.title: Pain Assessment Scales (NRS, FACES, Wong-Baker)
pain-assessment-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pain Assessment Scales (NRS, FACES, Wong-Baker), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of substance abuse (may underreport or overreport)
clinicalPearls: The patient's self-report is always the GOLD STANDARD for pain assessment: believe the patient. Pain is subjective and cannot be objectively measured.
Reassess pain after every intervention: 30 min after IV medication, 60 min after oral medication, 15-20 min after repositioning or non-pharmacological intervention.

Multimodal analgesia is best practice: Combine non-opioid (acetaminophen, NSAIDs), opioid (if needed), and non-pharmacological methods (positioning, ice/heat, distraction, relaxation) for synergistic effect and reduced opioid requirements.
For nonverbal patients (intubated, cognitively impaired, neonates), use behavioral pain tools (FLACC, CPOT) rather than assuming no pain. Absence of self-report does NOT equal absence of pain.
Document: Pain intensity (using standardized scale), location, quality (sharp/dull/burning/aching), onset, duration, aggravating/alleviating factors, impact on function, and patient's pain goal.

gcs-assessment-rpn.title: Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS)
gcs-assessment-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Glasgow Coma Scale (GCS), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always report the TOTAL score AND individual components: E4V5M6 = GCS 15. The breakdown matters more than the total: a patient with E4V1M6 (GCS 11) has a very different clinical picture than E3V4M4 (also GCS 11).
A GCS drop of ≥2 points from baseline is clinically significant and requires IMMEDIATE reassessment and provider notification.
Decorticate (flexion) is BETTER than decerebrate (extension): both are ominous, but decerebrate indicates damage at a deeper (brainstem) level with worse prognosis.
For intubated patients, document verbal component as '1T' or 'VT' and calculate the score without the verbal component (best possible = E4VTM6).

Apply painful stimuli correctly: Trapezius squeeze (pinch the trapezius muscle) or central pressure (sternal rub) for central stimulation; nail bed pressure for peripheral stimulation. Peripheral stimulus tests spinal reflexes, not brain function.

braden-scale-rpn.title: Braden Scale for Pressure Injury Risk
braden-scale-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Braden Scale for Pressure Injury Risk, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Lower Braden Score = Higher risk. Remember: 'Low is Bad': a score of 9 is very high risk, while 23 is no risk.

Reposition every 2 hours: this is the single most important nursing intervention for pressure injury prevention. Use the 30-degree lateral position (not 90-degree) to avoid pressure on the trochanter.
Float heels off the bed completely: heels are the second most common site for pressure injuries (after sacrum). Use a pillow under the calves, NOT under the heels.
Assess nutritional status: Protein (albumin, prealbumin), calories, vitamin C, and zinc are essential for tissue integrity and wound healing. Malnutrition is a major modifiable risk factor.
Document Braden Scale score on admission and every shift (or per policy). Implement a care plan based on the score: interventions should escalate as the score decreases.

comprehensive-health-assessment.title: Comprehensive Health Assessment (RN Level)
comprehensive-health-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Comprehensive Health Assessment (RN Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cognitive impairment affecting health history accuracy
Multiple comorbidities with complex medication regimens
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS start with the patient's chief complaint: what brought them here? This guides the depth and focus of your assessment even within a comprehensive framework.

Use therapeutic communication during the health history: Open-ended questions first ('Tell me about your pain'), then focused/closed-ended questions to clarify ('Is it sharp or dull?'). Avoid leading questions.

Document pertinent negatives: what you specifically looked for and did NOT find. 'Denies chest pain, SOB, palpitations' is as important as documenting positive findings for building the clinical picture.

The assessment is not a one-time event: it is continuous. Initial comprehensive assessment → ongoing focused assessments → reassessment after every intervention. Clinical status changes require reassessment.

Cultural competence: Ask about health beliefs, traditional remedies, dietary practices, religious considerations, decision-making patterns, and communication preferences. Use professional interpreters for language barriers: NEVER family members for medical interpretation.

primary-survey-rn.title: Primary Survey (ABCDE) Assessment
primary-survey-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Primary Survey (ABCDE) Assessment, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Distracting injuries masking more critical problems
Unresponsive patient with no available history
Mass casualty situations with limited resources
clinicalPearls: ABCDE is SEQUENTIAL: you must fix 'A' before assessing 'B.' A patient with an obstructed airway will die before any other problem matters. Airway is ALWAYS first.
In trauma, assume c-spine injury until cleared: use jaw thrust (NOT head-tilt chin-lift) to open the airway. Maintain manual in-line stabilization during all airway maneuvers.

The most common preventable cause of death in trauma is uncontrolled hemorrhage. Control bleeding with direct pressure first, then tourniquet for extremity hemorrhage that is not controlled by direct pressure.
Reassess ABCDE after EVERY intervention: the primary survey is not a one-time assessment. The patient's condition can change rapidly, and previously stable elements can deteriorate.

The secondary survey (complete head-to-toe) is only performed AFTER the primary survey is complete and all life threats are addressed. Never get distracted by dramatic-looking but non-life-threatening injuries.

nih-stroke-scale.title: NIH Stroke Scale (NIHSS) Assessment
nih-stroke-scale.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NIH Stroke Scale (NIHSS) Assessment, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Posterior circulation stroke (may be missed by NIHSS)
Young patients with stroke risk factors
clinicalPearls: NIHSS scoring must be precise and consistent: even small score changes guide major treatment decisions (tPA, thrombectomy). Certification training is required.
Score what the patient DOES, not what you think they CAN do. If the patient doesn't attempt the task, score it as unable. Don't coach or encourage beyond standard prompts.
Motor drift assessment: Ask patient to hold arms extended (palms up) at 90° sitting or 45° supine for 10 seconds. ANY downward drift before 10 seconds is abnormal. Test each arm separately.

The NIHSS underestimates posterior circulation strokes (vertebrobasilar): a patient can have a devastating basilar artery occlusion with a relatively low NIHSS score because ataxia, vertigo, and cranial nerve deficits are not heavily weighted.

Always correlate NIHSS with imaging. A high NIHSS with a large vessel occlusion on CTA = candidate for mechanical thrombectomy (up to 24 hours in select patients with salvageable tissue on perfusion imaging).

sepsis-screening-rn.title: Sepsis Screening (qSOFA, SIRS Criteria)
sepsis-screening-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sepsis Screening (qSOFA, SIRS Criteria), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hour-1 Bundle is critical: Measure lactate, blood cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics, begin crystalloid 30 mL/kg if hypotensive or lactate ≥4. Every hour of delay increases mortality.
Blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics: but NEVER delay antibiotics waiting for cultures. If cultures cannot be obtained within minutes, give antibiotics and draw cultures when possible.

Lactate is the most important biomarker for sepsis severity and treatment response. Repeat lactate within 2-4 hours if initial value is elevated. Lactate clearance >10% in 6 hours is associated with improved survival.

Fluid responsiveness: Not all septic patients respond to fluids. Assess for fluid responsiveness using passive leg raise test, pulse pressure variation, or stroke volume variation BEFORE giving additional fluid boluses.

Sepsis can present atypically in elderly (hypothermia instead of fever, confusion without fever), neonates (temperature instability, poor feeding, lethargy), and immunocompromised patients (blunted inflammatory response). Maintain a HIGH index of suspicion in vulnerable populations.

comprehensive-hpi-np.title: Comprehensive History and Physical (NP Level)
comprehensive-hpi-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Comprehensive History and Physical (NP Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Complex presentations with multiple overlapping symptoms
Patient unable to provide reliable history
Language or cultural barriers limiting history quality
Atypical presentations in elderly or immunocompromised
clinicalPearls: The HPI is the most diagnostically valuable component: a thorough HPI correctly suggests the diagnosis 75-80% of the time before you even examine the patient.

Always ask: 'What do you think is going on?' and 'What are you most worried about?': the patient's illness experience and concerns are essential for patient-centered care and may reveal critical information.

Document your clinical reasoning: Why did you order this test? Why did you choose this treatment? What diagnosis are you ruling out? This protects you legally and demonstrates medical decision-making complexity.

Red flag rule: For every chief complaint, have a mental list of 'cannot miss' diagnoses. Chest pain? Rule out MI, PE, aortic dissection, tamponade. Headache? Rule out SAH, meningitis, mass lesion, temporal arteritis.

The assessment is where you demonstrate NP-level thinking: Don't just list problems: synthesize the data into a coherent clinical picture, generate a ranked differential, and articulate your reasoning for the working diagnosis and plan.

differential-diagnosis-np.title: Differential Diagnosis Formulation (NP Level)
differential-diagnosis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Differential Diagnosis Formulation (NP Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Premature closure (accepting diagnosis before considering alternatives)
Atypical presentations in elderly, pediatric, or immunocompromised patients
clinicalPearls: Always ask: 'What is the worst thing this could be?' before asking 'What is the most likely thing this is?' Missing a common diagnosis causes morbidity; missing a dangerous diagnosis causes mortality.
The problem representation is the single most important step: if you frame the case correctly, the differential generates itself. Practice summarizing cases in one sentence.

Cognitive debiasing strategies: Use diagnostic checklists, consider the opposite hypothesis, ask 'What else could this be?', perform a diagnostic timeout, and always revisit the differential when treatment isn't working.

Test ordering should be purposeful: Ask 'How will this result change my management?' before ordering any test. If the result won't change what you do, the test is not indicated (and wastes resources and may lead to false positives).
When the diagnosis is unclear, treat the most dangerous possibility while continuing the workup. You can always de-escalate treatment; you cannot undo a missed life-threatening diagnosis.

ecg-advanced-np.title: 12-Lead ECG: Advanced Interpretation (NP Level)
ecg-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de 12-Lead ECG: Advanced Interpretation (NP Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Systematic interpretation prevents missed findings: use the same 12-step method EVERY time. Speed comes with practice, not by skipping steps.
Always compare with prior ECGs when available: a new finding is more clinically significant than a chronic one. 'New LBBB + chest pain = STEMI until proven otherwise.'

Reciprocal changes CONFIRM a STEMI: if you see ST elevation in inferior leads (II, III, aVF), look for reciprocal ST depression in lateral leads (I, aVL). Reciprocal changes increase specificity for acute MI.

In posterior MI, the standard 12-lead may only show ST depression in V1-V3 (mirror image of posterior ST elevation). If suspected, obtain posterior leads (V7-V9) for direct confirmation. Always obtain right-sided leads (V4R) for inferior STEMI to assess RV involvement.

Drug-induced QT prolongation is one of the most common medication safety issues. Common offenders: Haloperidol, ondansetron, fluoroquinolones, macrolides, methadone, antipsychotics, Class IA/III antiarrhythmics. Check QTc before starting any of these and correct K+/Mg2+ first.

sofa-apache-np.title: SOFA and APACHE II Scoring (NP Level)
sofa-apache-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de SOFA and APACHE II Scoring (NP Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: ICU patients with multisystem organ dysfunction
clinicalPearls: SOFA is for TRENDING: calculate it daily to track organ dysfunction trajectory. A single value is less useful than the trend. Worsening SOFA despite treatment = re-evaluate the plan.
For sepsis diagnosis, it's the CHANGE in SOFA that matters, not the absolute value. Baseline SOFA is assumed to be 0 for patients without known pre-existing organ dysfunction.
APACHE II uses the WORST values in the first 24 hours of ICU admission: ensure all required labs and assessments are completed within this window for accurate scoring.

Use severity scores to facilitate goals-of-care discussions with families: 'Based on objective measures of organ function, your loved one's organs are showing signs of worsening despite maximum treatment...'

Neither SOFA nor APACHE II should be used in isolation to make withdrawal-of-care decisions. They are prognostic tools that inform discussion, not decision tools that replace clinical judgment and family values.

point-of-care-us-np.title: Point-of-Care Ultrasound (POCUS) Assessment
point-of-care-us-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Point-of-Care Ultrasound (POCUS) Assessment, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Procedure guidance needed (central line, paracentesis, thoracentesis)
clinicalPearls: POCUS answers YES/NO clinical questions at the bedside in seconds to minutes: it does not replace formal imaging but accelerates clinical decision-making in acute settings.

The eFAST exam can be completed in <3 minutes and detects free fluid, pericardial effusion, and pneumothorax: three potentially lethal conditions in trauma. Every NP in acute care should be proficient.

IVC assessment is a rapid, non-invasive surrogate for CVP/volume status. A flat IVC that collapses with inspiration = 'give fluid.' A plethoric IVC that doesn't collapse = 'the patient doesn't need more fluid (and may need diuresis).'
Lung ultrasound is MORE SENSITIVE than chest X-ray for detecting pneumothorax, pleural effusion, and pulmonary edema. A-lines = normal or pneumothorax (check for lung sliding). B-lines = wet lungs.

Credentialing: POCUS competency requires structured training, supervised scans, and ongoing quality assurance. Document your training, maintain a scan log, and follow your institution's POCUS credentialing requirements.

aaa-rupture.title: Ruptura do aneurisma da aorta abdominal
aaa-rupture.overview: Ruptura do aneurisma da aorta abdominal é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hourly urine output must be >30mL to ensure renal perfusion
Monitor peripheral pulses distal to graft post-op
Report sudden severe pain immediately - sign of graft leak

mi-management.title: Manejo do infarto do miocárdio
mi-management.overview: Manejo do infarto do miocárdio é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NPO until swallow screen (if stroke signs coexist)
Avoid NSAIDs - they increase mortality risk post-MI
Semi-Fowler's position to reduce heart workload

hf-advanced.title: Insuficiência cardíaca avançada
hf-advanced.overview: Insuficiência cardíaca avançada é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Daily weights are the most sensitive indicator of fluid status
Sodium restriction (<3g/day) is vital
Avoid NSAIDs as they cause fluid retention

dysrhythmias.title: Disritmias cardíacas
dysrhythmias.overview: Disritmias cardíacas é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: A-Fib: Priority is rate control and anticoagulation
VFib = Defib (Immediate)
Check a pulse for at least 5 but no more than 10 seconds

pe-recognition.title: Reconhecimento de embolia pulmonar
pe-recognition.overview: Reconhecimento de embolia pulmonar é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: DVT prophylaxis (Enoxaparin/SCDs) is the best prevention
Position: High-Fowler's immediately
CT Angiography is the gold standard for diagnosis

infective-endocarditis.title: Endocardite infectieuse
infective-endocarditis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Endocardite infectieuse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Blood cultures x3 from different sites before starting antibiotics
PICC line placement for long-term outpatient IV therapy
Dental prophylaxis required for patients with prosthetic valves
Monitor for embolic complications (stroke, splenic infarct, renal infarct)

peripheral-artery-disease.title: Maladie artérielle périphérique
peripheral-artery-disease.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie artérielle périphérique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Supervised walking program is the most important non-surgical intervention
Buerger's exercises improve collateral circulation
Position legs in DEPENDENT position (NOT elevated like venous disease)
Smoking cessation is critical - single most modifiable risk factor

aortic-dissection.title: Dissection aortique
aortic-dissection.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Dissection aortique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Control HR before BP (target HR <60 bpm)
Type A (ascending) = emergency surgery
BP must be checked in BOTH arms - unequal pressures are diagnostic
Morphine for pain management (also reduces sympathetic drive)

carotid-endarterectomy.title: Endartériectomie carotidienne
carotid-endarterectomy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Endartériectomie carotidienne, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Keep BP within 20% of baseline (too low = thrombosis, too high = hemorrhage)
Monitor for hyperperfusion syndrome (headache, seizures, focal deficits)
Keep emergency airway equipment at bedside (hematoma can compress trachea)
Assess cranial nerves frequently - injury may be temporary or permanent

cardiovascular-rpn.title: Cardiovasculaire – RPN
cardiovascular-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiovasculaire – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The coronary arteries fill during diastole, not systole: this is why tachycardia is dangerous in cardiac patients: less diastolic time = less coronary perfusion = more ischemia.
Heart failure is NOT a disease: it is a syndrome. Always look for the underlying cause: hypertension, CAD, valvular disease, cardiomyopathy.
Angina pain should resolve within 5 minutes of rest or nitroglycerin. Pain lasting >20 minutes suggests MI: activate emergency response.
Daily weights are the single most important monitoring tool for heart failure patients. Weight gain of >2 lbs/day or >5 lbs/week indicates fluid retention: report immediately.
Atypical MI presentations (no chest pain) are common in women, elderly, and diabetic patients. Look for unexplained fatigue, dyspnea, nausea, jaw/back pain.

cardiovascular-rn.title: Cardiovasculaire – IA
cardiovascular-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiovasculaire – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hemodynamic profile recognition: Cardiogenic shock = High PAWP + Low CO + High SVR. Hypovolemic shock = Low PAWP + Low CO + High SVR. Septic shock (warm) = Low PAWP + High CO + Low SVR. Learn these patterns: they guide treatment.

Post-MI complications by timeline: Hours 0-24: Dysrhythmias (most common cause of death before hospitalization). Days 1-3: Heart failure, cardiogenic shock. Days 3-5: Papillary muscle rupture, VSD. Days 5-14: Ventricular wall rupture (cardiac tamponade). Weeks 2-8: Dressler syndrome (pericarditis).

SvO2 is the 'canary in the coal mine' for tissue perfusion. A dropping SvO2 often precedes clinical signs of shock. Normal SvO2 (60-80%) means oxygen delivery is meeting demand. Below 60% means tissues are extracting more oxygen = delivery is inadequate.

The Starling curve explains why 'more fluid is not always better.' A failing heart operates on the flat or descending portion of the curve: additional volume increases preload but does NOT increase cardiac output, instead causing pulmonary edema.
Always check SVR before giving vasopressors. If SVR is already elevated (>1400), adding a vasopressor may worsen cardiac output by increasing afterload. Consider afterload reduction instead.

cardiovascular-np.title: Cardiovasculaire – IPA
cardiovascular-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiovasculaire – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Modifiable: hypertension, dyslipidemia, diabetes, obesity, smoking, sedentary lifestyle, excessive alcohol
Non-modifiable: age, sex, family history, genetic predisposition
Emerging: elevated hsCRP, elevated Lp(a), obstructive sleep apnea, chronic kidney disease
ASCVD 10-year risk calculation determines treatment intensity
clinicalPearls: The Four Pillars of HFrEF GDMT (ARNI + BB + MRA + SGLT2i) should be initiated and uptitrated to target doses as quickly as tolerated. Each pillar independently reduces mortality: getting all four on board is critical.
New LBBB + chest pain = STEMI equivalent. Do NOT wait for troponin to come back: activate the cath lab. The LBBB makes ST-segment analysis unreliable, so clinical presentation drives the decision.

BNP and NT-proBNP: BNP is cleared by neprilysin, so levels are UNRELIABLE in patients on ARNI (sacubitril inhibits neprilysin, causing BNP accumulation). Use NT-proBNP instead for monitoring patients on Entresto.

Heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF) has no proven mortality-reducing therapy except SGLT2 inhibitors (EMPEROR-Preserved trial). Treatment focuses on managing congestion, controlling comorbidities (hypertension, obesity, AFib), and exercise.

When initiating beta-blockers in heart failure, the patient may initially feel WORSE due to reduced contractility. Educate: 'The medication needs time to work: improvement in heart function occurs over weeks to months. Do not stop taking it because you feel worse initially.'

chf-basics.title: Bases de l'insuffisance cardiaque congestive
chf-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bases de l'insuffisance cardiaque congestive, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: LEFT-sided failure = LUNGS (pulmonary symptoms: dyspnea, crackles, orthopnea, PND). RIGHT-sided failure = REST of body (systemic symptoms: JVD, edema, hepatomegaly, ascites). Memory aid: Left = Lungs, Right = Rest.
Daily weights are MORE RELIABLE than I&O for assessing fluid status. Weigh at the same time each day, same scale, same clothing. 1 liter of fluid = 1 kilogram (2.2 lbs) of weight.

The most common cause of right-sided heart failure is LEFT-sided heart failure. Chronic left-sided failure increases pulmonary pressures, which the right ventricle must pump against, eventually leading to RV failure.

BNP is a 'rule-out' test for heart failure. BNP <100 = dyspnea is NOT from HF (look for other causes: COPD, PE, pneumonia). BNP >400 = dyspnea IS from HF. BNP 100-400 = indeterminate, need additional workup.

The 'HF Zone System' for patient education: Green Zone (all clear) = no symptoms, stable weight. Yellow Zone (caution) = weight gain >2 lbs/day, increased dyspnea, more pillows needed: call provider. Red Zone (emergency) = severe dyspnea at rest, unrelieved by sitting up, unable to sleep: call 911.

mi-acute.title: Infarctus aigu du myocarde
mi-acute.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infarctus aigu du myocarde, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Time is Myocardium: Every 30-minute delay in reperfusion increases mortality. Door-to-balloon <90 minutes for PCI. Door-to-needle <30 minutes for fibrinolytics. The first hour after symptom onset is the 'golden hour': most myocardium can be salvaged.

Right Ventricular MI: If inferior STEMI (ST-elevation in II, III, aVF), ALWAYS check right-sided ECG (V4R). RV infarct is preload-dependent: the failing RV needs volume to maintain output. AVOID nitroglycerin, morphine, and diuretics (all reduce preload). Treatment: IV fluid bolus to maintain preload.

Reperfusion dysrhythmias are a GOOD sign! When a coronary artery is reopened (by PCI or fibrinolytics), the reperfused myocardium often generates dysrhythmias (PVCs, AIVR, brief runs of VTach). These usually resolve spontaneously and indicate successful reperfusion.

Post-MI medications for EVERY patient (unless contraindicated): 'ABCDE': Aspirin/Antiplatelet, Beta-blocker, Cholesterol (statin: high-intensity), DAPT (dual antiplatelet if stent), ACEI/ARB (prevent remodeling).

Morphine in MI: Use with CAUTION. While it relieves pain, morphine may worsen outcomes in NSTEMI (increased mortality in some studies). Use ONLY if pain is unrelieved by nitroglycerin. Current guidelines have de-emphasized morphine: some institutions have moved to 'ONA-B' instead of 'MONA-B.'

hypertension-management.title: Prise en charge de l'hypertension
hypertension-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prise en charge de l'hypertension, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hypertension is the 'silent killer': most patients are asymptomatic until target organ damage occurs. Regular screening and compliance monitoring are essential.

First-line agents: ACEi/ARB, CCB, or thiazide diuretic. Initial drug selection based on patient characteristics: ACEi/ARB for diabetics, CKD, HF, post-MI. CCB or thiazide for elderly and Black patients. Combination therapy is often needed to reach target.

In hypertensive emergency, do NOT lower BP too fast! Dropping MAP >25% in the first hour risks cerebral, coronary, or renal ischemia. The autoregulatory curve has shifted rightward in chronic hypertension: the brain/kidneys need higher pressures to perfuse. Exception: Aortic dissection (SBP <120 within 20 minutes).
The white coat effect can cause falsely elevated readings. Confirm with ambulatory BP monitoring (ABPM) or home BP monitoring before diagnosing hypertension and starting medications.

Resistant hypertension (uncontrolled on 3+ drugs including diuretic): Screen for secondary causes (renal artery stenosis, primary aldosteronism, pheochromocytoma, OSA). Adding spironolactone as 4th agent is most effective per PATHWAY-2 trial.

cardiac-monitoring.title: Monitorização cardíaca
cardiac-monitoring.overview: Monitorização cardíaca é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Systematic rhythm interpretation: 1) Is there a pulse? (Pulseless = ACLS protocol) 2) Rate? (Tachy vs Brady) 3) Rhythm regular or irregular? 4) P-waves present? (One P per QRS?) 5) PR interval? 6) QRS width? This approach prevents missing critical findings.

Sinus tachycardia is NEVER treated with cardioversion. It is always a compensatory response: treat the underlying cause (fever, pain, hypovolemia, anxiety, PE, HF). Cardioverting sinus tach removes the heart's compensation and can cause cardiovascular collapse.

Artifact vs. real rhythm: Always check the patient first. If the monitor shows VFib but the patient is sitting up talking to you, it's artifact. Check lead placement, loose electrodes, patient movement, and electrical interference before treating a rhythm.

The most important rhythm in ACLS is the one you can identify as NOT normal. You don't need to name every rhythm perfectly: you need to recognize when something is wrong, assess for a pulse, and initiate appropriate treatment (CPR/defib for pulseless, medication/cardioversion for pulsed unstable, medications for pulsed stable).

Telemetry alarm fatigue is a leading safety concern. Ensure alarm parameters are individualized to each patient and respond to every alarm: even false alarms require acknowledgment. The one alarm you ignore may be the real event.

cardiac-rhythm-rn.title: Rythmes cardiaques – IA
cardiac-rhythm-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rythmes cardiaques – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Drug toxicity (digoxin, beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers)
clinicalPearls: Wide-complex tachycardia = VTach until proven otherwise. Treating VTach as SVT-with-aberrancy is far more dangerous than treating SVT as VTach. When in doubt, treat as VTach.
AFib with a ventricular rate of exactly 150? Think: Is this actually atrial FLUTTER with 2:1 block? Increase paper speed to 50 mm/sec or use vagal maneuvers/adenosine to unmask flutter waves.

Mobitz Type II is the dangerous second-degree block (risk of complete block → asystole). 'Longer, longer, longer, DROP = Wenckebach' (Type I: relatively benign). 'Some P's don't get through, same PR when they do = Mobitz II' (dangerous: needs pacing).
The 'Ugly Five': rhythms requiring immediate intervention: VFib (defib), Pulseless VTach (defib), Asystole (CPR + epi), PEA (CPR + epi + fix cause), and Torsades (Mg2+ + overdrive pacing).

When a patient with a pacemaker develops bradycardia, think failure to CAPTURE (spikes without complexes) or failure to PACE (no spikes when expected). Place a magnet over the pacemaker to switch to asynchronous mode (VOO/DOO): this tests the pacemaker's ability to fire and capture without being inhibited by sensing.

cardiac-auscultation-rn.title: Auscultation cardiaque – IA
cardiac-auscultation-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Auscultation cardiaque – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: BELL for low-pitched sounds (S3, S4, mitral stenosis rumble). DIAPHRAGM for high-pitched sounds (S1, S2, aortic regurgitation, ejection clicks). Remember: 'Bell is for Bass.'
New murmur after MI = EMERGENCY until proven otherwise. Acute mitral regurgitation (papillary muscle rupture) or VSD can cause rapid hemodynamic deterioration. Get an emergent echocardiogram.
An S3 in a 25-year-old athlete is normal. An S3 in a 65-year-old with dyspnea is heart failure until proven otherwise. Context determines whether a finding is normal or pathologic.
Diastolic murmurs are ALWAYS pathologic: there is no 'innocent diastolic murmur.' Any new diastolic murmur requires echocardiographic evaluation.

Auscultation positioning matters: Left lateral decubitus position brings the LV apex closer to the chest wall: best for hearing S3, S4, and mitral murmurs. Sitting up and leaning forward brings the aortic root closer: best for aortic regurgitation murmur.

cardiogenic-shock.title: Choc cardiogénique
cardiogenic-shock.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Choc cardiogénique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Cardiogenic shock hemodynamic profile: HIGH PAWP + LOW CI + HIGH SVR. This distinguishes it from hypovolemic shock (LOW PAWP + LOW CI + HIGH SVR) and septic shock (LOW PAWP + HIGH CI + LOW SVR).

The goal in cardiogenic shock is to break the vicious cycle: restore perfusion (vasopressors), improve contractility (inotropes), reduce afterload (to improve ejection), and revascularize (emergent PCI for MI). All simultaneously: this is not a sequential approach.

IABP timing is critical: INFLATE at the dicrotic notch (start of diastole) to augment coronary perfusion. DEFLATE just before systole to reduce afterload. Improper timing can worsen hemodynamics. Augmented diastolic pressure should be HIGHER than unassisted systolic pressure on the waveform.

Lactate clearance is the best indicator of treatment effectiveness in cardiogenic shock. A normalizing lactate means tissues are being adequately perfused. A rising lactate despite maximal therapy suggests treatment failure: escalate to mechanical circulatory support.

In RV infarction causing cardiogenic shock: The hemodynamic profile is different: elevated CVP with LOW or NORMAL PAWP (the RV cannot fill the LV). Treatment: Volume loading (the opposite of LV-mediated cardiogenic shock) + avoid preload reducers (nitroglycerin, morphine, diuretics) + inotropes.

pe-dvt.title: Pulmonary Embolism and Deep Vein Thrombosis
pe-dvt.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pulmonary Embolism and Deep Vein Thrombosis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Virchow's Triad is the framework for understanding VTE risk: Stasis + Endothelial injury + Hypercoagulability. Every hospitalized patient should be assessed for VTE prophylaxis: mechanical (SCDs, TED hose) and/or pharmacologic (LMWH, UFH).

D-dimer is a 'rule-out' test, not a 'rule-in' test. A negative D-dimer in a low-probability patient effectively excludes PE (>99% NPV). But an elevated D-dimer does NOT diagnose PE: it simply means further imaging (CT-PA) is needed. D-dimer is elevated in many conditions (infection, cancer, pregnancy, recent surgery, trauma).

The most common cause of death from PE is RIGHT VENTRICULAR FAILURE, not hypoxemia. The thin-walled RV cannot handle the acute increase in pulmonary vascular resistance. RV dilation → septal shift → decreased LV filling → decreased cardiac output → obstructive shock.

Post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS) affects 20-50% of DVT patients within 2 years. Chronic venous insufficiency causes pain, swelling, skin changes, and ulceration. Compression stockings (30-40 mmHg) were previously recommended for prevention but recent evidence (SOX trial) is equivocal.

If a patient has a cardiac arrest and PE is suspected, consider administering tPA during CPR. The 2020 AHA guidelines state that fibrinolytic therapy can be considered for cardiac arrest with suspected PE. Continue CPR for at least 60-90 minutes after fibrinolytic administration to allow drug effect.

peds-cardiac-basics.title: Pediatric Cardiac Principles
peds-cardiac-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pediatric Cardiac Principles, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of congenital heart disease
clinicalPearls: Acyanotic defects (VSD, ASD) = left-to-right shunt = increased pulmonary blood flow
Cyanotic defects (Tetralogy of Fallot) = right-to-left shunt = decreased pulmonary blood flow
Knee-chest position increases systemic vascular resistance and helps resolve Tet spells

cardiac-assessment-ecg.title: Évaluation cardiaque et ECG
cardiac-assessment-ecg.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation cardiaque et ECG, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Rate calculation: 300 divided by number of large boxes between R-R intervals
ST elevation in two contiguous leads = STEMI until proven otherwise
Always correlate ECG changes with patient clinical presentation

cardiac-cycle-hemodynamics.title: Cycle cardiaque et hémodynamique
cardiac-cycle-hemodynamics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cycle cardiaque et hémodynamique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Cardiac Output = Heart Rate x Stroke Volume (CO = HR x SV)
MAP = (SBP + 2 x DBP) / 3; target >65 for adequate organ perfusion
Preload = volume (Frank-Starling mechanism); Afterload = resistance; Contractility = force

conduction-system.title: Système de conduction cardiaque
conduction-system.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Système de conduction cardiaque, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: VFib = NO pulse, NO organized rhythm = immediate defibrillation
Atrial fibrillation without anticoagulation = stroke risk (CHA2DS2-VASc score)
Peaked T waves on ECG suggest hyperkalemia; flattened T waves suggest hypokalemia

pad-claudication.title: Peripheral Artery Disease and Claudication
pad-claudication.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Peripheral Artery Disease and Claudication, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Arterial ulcers: painful, pale base, well-defined borders, found on toes/feet
Venous ulcers: minimal pain, ruddy base, irregular borders, found on medial malleolus
Never elevate legs above heart level in arterial disease (worsens ischemia)

cardioversion-defib.title: Cardioversion et défibrillation
cardioversion-defib.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardioversion et défibrillation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Ventricular fibrillation or pulseless VT (for defibrillation)
clinicalPearls: Defibrillation = unsynchronized = for VFib/pulseless VT only
Cardioversion = synchronized = for unstable tachycardias with a pulse
After cardioversion, re-check that sync mode is ON before each subsequent shock (many defibrillators auto-reset to unsync after each shock)

raynauds-phenomenon-rpn.title: Phénomène de Raynaud – RPN
raynauds-phenomenon-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Phénomène de Raynaud – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Teach patients to wear layered clothing and insulated gloves
Secondary Raynaud's is associated with scleroderma and may indicate more serious autoimmune disease
Smoking cessation is critical as nicotine causes vasoconstriction

buergers-disease-rpn.title: Maladie de Buerger – RPN
