buergers-disease-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie de Buerger – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Male sex (historically, though female incidence rising)
Southeast Asian or Middle Eastern descent
clinicalPearls: Complete smoking cessation is the ONLY way to stop disease progression
Even one cigarette can trigger disease flare
Buerger's differs from PAD: affects young smokers, involves both arteries and veins

venous-insufficiency-rpn.title: Insuffisance veineuse – RPN
venous-insufficiency-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insuffisance veineuse – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Venous ulcers: medial malleolus, shallow, ruddy base, irregular borders, minimal pain
Compression therapy is the cornerstone of treatment; never use on arterial disease (check ABI first)
Elevate legs ABOVE heart level, not just on a footstool

varicose-veins-rpn.title: Varices – RPN
varicose-veins-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Varices – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Compression stockings should be applied in the morning before standing
Walking improves calf muscle pump action and venous return
Varicose veins alone are mostly cosmetic, but can progress to venous insufficiency

endocarditis-basics-rpn.title: Bases de l'endocardite – RPN
endocarditis-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bases de l'endocardite – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics (at least 3 sets from different sites)
Prophylactic antibiotics before dental procedures for high-risk patients
IV drug users most commonly affect the TRICUSPID valve

rheumatic-fever-rpn.title: Rhumatisme articulaire aigu – RPN
rheumatic-fever-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rhumatisme articulaire aigu – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Jones Criteria: major = carditis, polyarthritis, chorea, erythema marginatum, subcutaneous nodules
Rheumatic fever does NOT occur without prior strep infection
Mitral stenosis is the most common long-term valvular consequence

kawasaki-disease-rpn.title: Maladie de Kawasaki – RPN
kawasaki-disease-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie de Kawasaki – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Aspirin is used in Kawasaki disease despite the general pediatric rule against aspirin (Reye syndrome risk)
Coronary artery aneurysm is the most dangerous complication
Desquamation of fingers/toes occurs in the subacute phase (week 2-3)

cardiomyopathy-basics-rpn.title: Cardiomyopathie – RPN
cardiomyopathy-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiomyopathie – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: HCM: leading cause of sudden cardiac death in young athletes
Dilated CM: most common type; 'big floppy heart' with low EF
In HCM, avoid dehydration, Valsalva, and strenuous exercise (increases outflow obstruction)

shock-types-recognition-rpn.title: Types de choc – RPN
shock-types-recognition-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Types de choc – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Warm shock (distributive) = warm, flushed skin; Cold shock (all others) = cool, clammy skin
Tachycardia is the earliest compensatory sign; hypotension is a LATE sign
Lactate >4 mmol/L indicates severe tissue hypoperfusion

chest-pain-differential-rpn.title: Douleur thoracique – RPN
chest-pain-differential-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Douleur thoracique – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Cardiac chest pain: pressure/squeezing, radiates to jaw/arm, associated with diaphoresis and nausea
Aortic dissection: sudden tearing pain radiating to back; do NOT give anticoagulants
Women, elderly, and diabetics may present atypically (fatigue, nausea, dyspnea without classic pain)

dic-basics.title: Coagulation intravasculaire disséminée
dic-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Coagulation intravasculaire disséminée, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Obstetric complications (placental abruption, amniotic fluid embolism)
clinicalPearls: DIC = 'Death Is Coming' - high mortality; treat the underlying cause
Lab triad: low platelets + low fibrinogen + elevated D-dimer
Bleeding AND clotting occur simultaneously - unique to DIC

marfan-syndrome-rpn.title: Syndrome de Marfan – RPN
marfan-syndrome-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome de Marfan – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Aortic dissection is the leading cause of death in Marfan syndrome
Avoid isometric exercises and contact sports (increases aortic wall stress)
Lens subluxation is upward in Marfan (downward in homocystinuria)

endocarditis-infection-rpn.title: Endocardite infectieuse – RPN
endocarditis-infection-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Endocardite infectieuse – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Janeway lesions = painless (immune complex deposits); Osler nodes = painful (septic emboli)
Subacute endocarditis (Strep viridans) = dental origin; Acute (Staph aureus) = IV drug use
Antibiotic therapy typically requires 4-6 weeks of IV administration

toxic-shock-syndrome-rpn.title: Syndrome du choc toxique – RPN
toxic-shock-syndrome-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome du choc toxique – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Remove the source (tampon, packing) IMMEDIATELY - this is the priority
Teach patients to change tampons every 4-8 hours and use lowest absorbency needed
Rash desquamation (peeling palms/soles) occurs 1-2 weeks after onset and is diagnostic

focused-cardiac-assessment.title: Évaluation cardiaque ciblée
focused-cardiac-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation cardiaque ciblée, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Auscultation landmarks: Aortic (2nd ICS right), Pulmonic (2nd ICS left), Erb's point (3rd ICS left), Tricuspid (4th ICS left), Mitral (5th ICS MCL)
S3 = heart failure (volume overload); S4 = stiff ventricle (HTN, HCM)
Always assess JVD with patient at 45-degree angle

heart-sounds-rpn.title: Bruits cardiaques – RPN
heart-sounds-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bruits cardiaques – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: All diastolic murmurs are pathologic until proven otherwise
Murmurs graded I-VI: I = barely audible; VI = heard without stethoscope
Use bell for S3/S4; diaphragm for murmurs and S1/S2

dvt-management.title: Prise en charge de la TVP
dvt-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prise en charge de la TVP, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Factor V Leiden or other thrombophilia
clinicalPearls: NEVER massage the affected leg (risk of embolization)
Heparin and warfarin overlap for at least 5 days and until INR 2-3
Teach patients on warfarin to maintain consistent vitamin K intake, not eliminate it

neurogenic-shock.title: Choc neurogénique
neurogenic-shock.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Choc neurogénique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Neurogenic shock = bradycardia + hypotension + warm skin (opposite of hypovolemic shock)
Do NOT over-resuscitate with fluids (heart cannot compensate with tachycardia)
Differentiate from spinal shock (loss of reflexes below injury) which is a neurological, not hemodynamic, event

obstructive-shock.title: Choc obstructif
obstructive-shock.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Choc obstructif, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Obstructive shock requires identifying and removing the mechanical barrier - fluids alone will not resolve it
Beck's triad = cardiac tamponade; tracheal deviation = tension pneumothorax
PEA arrest: think obstructive causes (H's and T's) - tamponade, tension pneumo, thromboembolism

hemodynamic-assessment-rn.title: Évaluation hémodynamique – IA
hemodynamic-assessment-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation hémodynamique – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Phlebostatic axis = 4th ICS at mid-axillary line; always level here for accurate readings
Read hemodynamic pressures at end-expiration (eliminates intrathoracic pressure effects)
High PAWP + Low CO = cardiogenic shock; Low PAWP + Low CO = hypovolemic shock

peripheral-vascular-rn.title: Peripheral Vascular (RN)
peripheral-vascular-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Peripheral Vascular (RN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Arterial ulcers: ELEVATE is wrong (keep legs level/dependent); Venous ulcers: ELEVATE above heart
ABI interpretation: >0.9 normal; 0.5-0.9 claudication; <0.5 critical ischemia
6 P's of acute arterial occlusion: Pain, Pallor, Pulselessness, Paresthesia, Paralysis, Poikilothermia

hf-advanced-np.title: Insuffisance cardiaque avancée – IPA
hf-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insuffisance cardiaque avancée – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Four pillars of GDMT for HFrEF: ARNI (or ACEi/ARB), beta-blocker, MRA, SGLT2i
Warm and wet = vasodilator + diuretic; Cold and wet = inotrope + diuretic; Cold and dry = fluids cautiously
Iron deficiency (even without anemia) worsens HF - order IV iron if ferritin <100

afib-management-np.title: Prise en charge de la FA – IPA
afib-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prise en charge de la FA – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: CHA2DS2-VASc: CHF=1, HTN=1, Age≥75=2, DM=1, Stroke/TIA=2, Vascular dz=1, Age 65-74=1, Sex(female)=1
DOACs preferred over warfarin for non-valvular AFib (no INR monitoring, fewer drug interactions)
Rate control target: resting HR <110 bpm (lenient) or <80 bpm (strict) based on symptoms

acs-management-np.title: Prise en charge du SCA – IPA
acs-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prise en charge du SCA – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of premature CAD (<55 male, <65 female)
clinicalPearls: Door-to-balloon time <90 minutes for primary PCI; door-to-needle <30 minutes for fibrinolysis
STEMI: ST elevation ≥1mm in 2+ contiguous leads (≥2mm in V1-V3)
Post-ACS discharge: DAPT + statin + beta-blocker + ACEi/ARB (evidence-based secondary prevention)

cardiac-arrest-acls-np.title: Arrêt cardiaque SVCA – IPA
cardiac-arrest-acls-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Arrêt cardiaque SVCA – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Coronary artery disease and prior MI
clinicalPearls: H's and T's: Hypovolemia, Hypoxia, Hydrogen ion (acidosis), Hypo/Hyperkalemia, Hypothermia | Tension pneumothorax, Tamponade, Toxins, Thrombosis (PE/MI)
Minimize interruptions in chest compressions; pulse checks every 2 minutes only
Post-ROSC: targeted temperature management (32-36°C for 24h) improves neurological outcomes

hfpef-np.title: ICFEp – IPA
hfpef-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de ICFEp – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: HFpEF is now more common than HFrEF; SGLT2 inhibitors are the first proven therapy
Traditional HFrEF drugs (ACEi, beta-blockers) have NOT shown mortality benefit in HFpEF
Obesity-HFpEF phenotype responds well to weight loss and GLP-1 agonists

valvular-disease-np.title: Maladie valvulaire – IPA
valvular-disease-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie valvulaire – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Aortic stenosis triad: syncope, angina, heart failure (once symptoms appear, prognosis is poor without intervention)
DOACs are CONTRAINDICATED in mechanical heart valves - only warfarin is approved
Severe aortic stenosis: avoid nitrates and vasodilators (cannot increase cardiac output to compensate)

pvd-advanced-np.title: Maladie vasculaire périphérique – IPA
pvd-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie vasculaire périphérique – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: PAD is a coronary artery disease equivalent - treat aggressively for cardiovascular risk
Critical limb ischemia = vascular emergency; revascularization within 2 weeks to prevent amputation
Diabetic patients may have falsely normal ABI due to calcified vessels; use toe pressures instead

takotsubo-cardiomyopathy-np.title: Cardiomyopathie de Takotsubo – IPA
takotsubo-cardiomyopathy-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiomyopathie de Takotsubo – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Takotsubo mimics STEMI; coronary angiography shows clean coronaries
Troponin is disproportionately low compared to the degree of wall motion abnormality
Full recovery of EF typically occurs within 1-4 weeks; in-hospital mortality is 1-2%

infective-endocarditis-advanced-np.title: Endocardite infectieuse avancée – IPA
infective-endocarditis-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Endocardite infectieuse avancée – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Modified Duke Criteria: 2 major, 1 major + 3 minor, or 5 minor = definite IE
Surgical indications: HF, uncontrolled infection, vegetation >10mm with embolic event, prosthetic valve IE with dehiscence
New AV block in IE = perivalvular abscess until proven otherwise (requires urgent surgery)

marfan-cardiac-np.title: Marfan cardiaque – IPA
marfan-cardiac-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Marfan cardiaque – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: FBN1 mutations associated with severe phenotype
clinicalPearls: Prophylactic aortic root replacement at ≥5.0 cm (or ≥4.5 cm with family history of dissection)
Pregnancy in Marfan: high risk if root >4.0 cm; mandatory preconception echo and close monitoring
Screen all first-degree relatives - autosomal dominant with variable expressivity

constrictive-pericarditis-np.title: Péricardite constrictive – IPA
constrictive-pericarditis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Péricardite constrictive – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Constrictive pericarditis vs restrictive cardiomyopathy: BNP low in constriction, high in restriction
Kussmaul sign (JVD rising with inspiration) is characteristic of constrictive pericarditis
Pericardiectomy is the definitive treatment but carries 5-10% operative mortality

cardiac-tamponade-mgmt-np.title: Tamponnade cardiaque – IPA
cardiac-tamponade-mgmt-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tamponnade cardiaque – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Beck's triad is present in only ~30% of tamponade cases; bedside echo is the diagnostic gold standard
NEVER give diuretics or vasodilators in tamponade (reduces preload, worsens output)
Electrical alternans (beat-to-beat QRS amplitude variation) is highly specific for large pericardial effusion with tamponade

stemi-nstemi-algorithm-np.title: Algorithme STEMI/NSTEMI – IPA
stemi-nstemi-algorithm-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Algorithme STEMI/NSTEMI – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: STEMI equivalents: new LBBB, Wellens syndrome (deep T-wave inversions in V2-V3), posterior MI (ST depression V1-V3)
High-risk NSTEMI features: ongoing pain, dynamic ECG changes, hemodynamic instability, elevated troponin, GRACE >140
Post-MI: DAPT for 12 months (minimum), then aspirin lifelong

heart-transplant-rejection-np.title: Rejet de greffe cardiaque – IPA
heart-transplant-rejection-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rejet de greffe cardiaque – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Medication non-adherence (most common cause of late rejection)
clinicalPearls: Triple immunosuppression: calcineurin inhibitor + antimetabolite + corticosteroid
Transplanted hearts are denervated: no chest pain with ischemia; dyspnea may be the only anginal equivalent
Cardiac allograft vasculopathy (chronic rejection) is the leading cause of late graft failure

pulmonary-hypertension-np.title: Hypertension pulmonaire – IPA
pulmonary-hypertension-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hypertension pulmonaire – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Congenital heart disease with Eisenmenger syndrome
Left heart disease (most common cause: Group 2 PH)
clinicalPearls: Group 2 PH (left heart disease) is the most common cause; treating the underlying left heart disease is the priority
PAH-specific drugs (PDE5i, ERA, prostacyclins) are ONLY for Group 1 PAH; harmful in Group 2
CTEPH (Group 4) is potentially curable with pulmonary thromboendarterectomy

cerebral-venous-sinus-thrombosis-np.title: Thrombose veineuse cérébrale – IPA
cerebral-venous-sinus-thrombosis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Thrombose veineuse cérébrale – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: CVST is unique: anticoagulate even with intracranial hemorrhage (the hemorrhage is from venous congestion)
Young women on OCPs with headache and papilledema: think CVST
Seizures are more common in CVST than in arterial stroke

portal-hypertension-np.title: Hypertension portale – IPA
portal-hypertension-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hypertension portale – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cirrhosis (most common cause: alcohol, HCV, NASH)
Right heart failure causing hepatic congestion
clinicalPearls: HVPG ≥10 mmHg = clinically significant portal hypertension (risk of varices and ascites)
Stop NSBBs in refractory ascites, SBP, or hepatorenal syndrome (can worsen hypotension)
Diagnostic paracentesis: PMN >250/mm3 = spontaneous bacterial peritonitis (treat with ceftriaxone)

advanced-cardiac-auscultation-np.title: Auscultation cardiaque avancée – IPA
advanced-cardiac-auscultation-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Auscultation cardiaque avancée – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Valsalva and standing INCREASE HCM murmur (decreased preload worsens obstruction)
Squatting DECREASES HCM murmur (increased preload and afterload reduce obstruction)
All diastolic murmurs and any new murmur in an older adult require echocardiographic evaluation

hemodynamic-monitoring-np.title: Surveillance hémodynamique – IPA
hemodynamic-monitoring-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Surveillance hémodynamique – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hemodynamic profiles: Cardiogenic = high wedge + low CO + high SVR; Septic = low-normal wedge + high CO + low SVR
SvO2 <60% = tissues are extracting more oxygen than normal (inadequate delivery or increased demand)
Fluid responsiveness: assess with passive leg raise or pulse pressure variation, not just CVP

cardiac-output-np.title: Débit cardiaque – IPA
cardiac-output-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Débit cardiaque – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: CO = HR x SV; normal CO 4-8 L/min; normal CI 2.5-4.0 L/min/m2
Always assess fluid responsiveness before giving volume (passive leg raise, pulse pressure variation)
Inotropes increase contractility but also increase myocardial oxygen demand (except levosimendan)

vascular-assessment-np.title: Évaluation vasculaire – IPA
vascular-assessment-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation vasculaire – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: PAD is a cardiovascular risk equivalent - treat with same intensity as known CAD
ABI interpretation: >1.3 = non-compressible; 0.9-1.3 = normal; 0.5-0.89 = claudication; <0.5 = critical ischemia
Always check ABI before applying compression therapy for venous disease

polymyalgia-rheumatica-np.title: Polymyalgia Rheumatica (NP)
polymyalgia-rheumatica-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Polymyalgia Rheumatica (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: PMR responds dramatically to low-dose prednisone (12.5-25mg) within 1-3 days; lack of response = reconsider diagnosis
Always screen for GCA: new headache, jaw claudication, or vision changes = emergent high-dose steroids
CK is NORMAL in PMR (elevated CK suggests myositis, not PMR)

scleroderma-systemic-sclerosis-np.title: Scleroderma/Systemic Sclerosis (NP)
scleroderma-systemic-sclerosis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Scleroderma/Systemic Sclerosis (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: CREST syndrome (limited SSc): Calcinosis, Raynaud's, Esophageal dysmotility, Sclerodactyly, Telangiectasia
Scleroderma renal crisis: MUST use ACEi (captopril); ARBs are NOT equivalent and should not be substituted
Annual PFT with DLCO is essential for early ILD detection before symptoms develop

marfan-syndrome-np.title: Syndrome de Marfan – IPA
marfan-syndrome-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome de Marfan – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of aortic events <50 years
clinicalPearls: Surgical thresholds: ≥5.0 cm general, ≥4.5 cm with family history of dissection or rapid growth >0.5 cm/yr
Pregnancy risk: aortic root >4.0 cm = high risk for dissection; requires shared decision-making
Ectopia lentis is UP in Marfan (DOWN in homocystinuria) - classic board pearl

intra-aortic-balloon-pump-np.title: Ballon intra-aortique – IPA
intra-aortic-balloon-pump-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ballon intra-aortique – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Mechanical complications of MI (VSD, papillary muscle rupture)
Bridge to cardiac surgery or transplant
clinicalPearls: IABP inflates in DIASTOLE (coronary perfusion) and deflates before SYSTOLE (afterload reduction)
Contraindicated in aortic regurgitation (inflation worsens regurgitation) and aortic dissection
Monitor the catheterized limb's pulses, color, temperature, and sensation every hour - limb ischemia is the most common complication

pacemaker-care.title: Pacemakers: Insertion, Types, and Nursing Care
pacemaker-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pacemakers: Insertion, Types, and Nursing Care, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Symptomatic bradycardia (sick sinus syndrome, heart block)
Advanced AV block (second-degree Type II, third-degree)
clinicalPearls: Post-insertion: Immobilize the arm on the pacemaker side for 24-48 hours (prevents lead displacement). No lifting >10 lbs on the affected side for 4-6 weeks.
Monitor ECG continuously post-insertion: verify capture (spike + complex), sensing (appropriate inhibition), and correct rate. Report failure to capture or failure to sense immediately.

Pacemaker syndrome occurs when AV synchrony is lost (ventricular pacing without atrial contribution: loss of 'atrial kick' reduces cardiac output by 15-25%). Symptoms: fatigue, dizziness, hypotension, pulsatile sensation in neck (cannon A waves from atria contracting against closed AV valves).

Electromagnetic interference (EMI): MRI is now conditionally safe with MRI-conditional pacemakers (must verify model). Avoid holding cell phone directly over the generator. Electrocautery during surgery can inhibit pacing: have magnet available. Metal detectors at airports are safe but may trigger the alarm.

Teach patient to check pulse daily and report a rate below the programmed base rate. Carry pacemaker ID card at all times. Follow up for interrogation every 6-12 months to check battery, lead impedance, and sensing/capture thresholds.

Transcutaneous pacing (external): Place pads anterior-posterior. Set rate 60-80 bpm, increase milliamps until electrical and mechanical capture (palpable pulse). This is PAINFUL: sedate the conscious patient.

hypertensive-emergency-np.title: Urgence hypertensive – IPA
hypertensive-emergency-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Urgence hypertensive – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Medication non-adherence - most common precipitant of hypertensive emergency
Chronic kidney disease - impaired sodium excretion and RAAS dysregulation
Renovascular disease - renal artery stenosis driving excessive renin secretion
Sympathomimetic drug use (cocaine, amphetamines) - direct catecholamine excess
Abrupt clonidine withdrawal - rebound sympathetic hyperactivity
clinicalPearls: Never use sublingual nifedipine - causes unpredictable precipitous BP drops leading to stroke and MI
In aortic dissection, beta-blocker BEFORE vasodilator to prevent reflex tachycardia that increases aortic shear stress
Hypertensive urgency (elevated BP without end-organ damage) does NOT require IV therapy - restart/adjust oral medications and follow up in 24-72 hours

vte-prophylaxis-np.title: Prophylaxie de la TEV – IPA
vte-prophylaxis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prophylaxie de la TEV – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Major orthopedic surgery (hip/knee replacement) - highest risk category, VTE rate 40-60% without prophylaxis
Active malignancy - tumor cells express tissue factor and cancer procoagulant (direct Factor X activator)
Prior VTE - recurrence rate 30% within 10 years
Prolonged immobilization >72 hours - venous stasis in soleal sinuses and valve pockets
Obesity (BMI >30) - chronic low-grade inflammation and impaired fibrinolysis
Inherited thrombophilias (Factor V Leiden, prothrombin G20210A mutation) - resistance to activated Protein C or excess prothrombin
Estrogen therapy/pregnancy - upregulates hepatic synthesis of Factors II, VII, VIII, X and fibrinogen
clinicalPearls: Caprini score ≥5 = high risk: pharmacological prophylaxis is mandatory unless contraindicated. Each additional point increases VTE risk exponentially.
LMWH is renally cleared - always check CrCl before prescribing. CrCl <30 mL/min: switch to UFH 5000 units SQ every 8-12 hours.

HIT is a clinical diagnosis confirmed by the 4T score: Thrombocytopenia (>50% drop), Timing (5-14 days), Thrombosis, and no oTher cause. Stop all heparin products immediately and start argatroban or bivalirudin.

brugada-syndrome-np.title: Syndrome de Brugada – IPA
brugada-syndrome-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome de Brugada – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: SCN5A mutation carriers (autosomal dominant with variable penetrance, 20-30% of cases)
Male sex - 8:1 male predominance due to higher testosterone-mediated Ito density in male epicardium
Southeast Asian descent - highest prevalence (1 in 1000 in Thailand, leading cause of sudden death in young men)
Family history of sudden cardiac death before age 45
Febrile illness - fever augments sodium channel dysfunction and can unmask Type 1 ECG pattern
Medications that block sodium channels (Class IA/IC antiarrhythmics, tricyclic antidepressants, lithium, cocaine)
Vagal predominance - symptoms occur at rest or during sleep when vagal tone is highest
clinicalPearls: Brugada Type 1 ECG can be unmasked by fever, sodium channel blockers, vagotonic states, and hypokalemia - always check ECG during febrile illness in known carriers
ICD is the ONLY proven therapy to prevent sudden cardiac death in Brugada - no antiarrhythmic drug has shown mortality benefit in RCTs
Brugada ECG mimickers: right bundle branch block, early repolarization, anterior STEMI - always correlate with clinical context and consider V1-V3 lead repositioning

long-qt-syndrome-np.title: Syndrome du QT long – IPA
long-qt-syndrome-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome du QT long – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Congenital LQTS gene mutations - LQT1 (KCNQ1, 40-55%), LQT2 (KCNH2/hERG, 30-45%), LQT3 (SCN5A, 5-10%)

QTc-prolonging medications - antiarrhythmics (sotalol, dofetilide, amiodarone), antibiotics (fluoroquinolones, azithromycin, TMP-SMX), antipsychotics (haloperidol, ziprasidone), antiemetics (ondansetron, droperidol)
Hypokalemia - reduces IKr current by altering channel rectification properties
Hypomagnesemia - impairs repolarizing K+ channel function and enhances L-type Ca2+ current
Female sex - 2:1 female predominance for drug-induced LQTS due to lower baseline IKr density (estrogen effect)
Bradycardia - slower heart rates prolong repolarization and increase EAD susceptibility (pause-dependent TdP)
Structural heart disease with reduced ejection fraction - heterogeneous repolarization substrate
clinicalPearls: QTc >500 ms is the critical threshold - risk of TdP increases exponentially. Discontinue offending drugs, correct electrolytes, and consider overdrive pacing.
Torsades treatment is NOT the same as monomorphic VT: do NOT give amiodarone or procainamide (both prolong QT further). First-line: IV magnesium. Second-line: overdrive pacing or isoproterenol.
Genotype-trigger correlations: LQT1 = exercise (especially swimming), LQT2 = auditory stimuli (alarm clocks, phones), LQT3 = sleep/rest. Counsel patients on genotype-specific avoidance strategies.

tetralogy-of-fallot.title: Tétralogie de Fallot
tetralogy-of-fallot.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tétralogie de Fallot, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Maternal rubella infection in first trimester
Family history of congenital heart disease
Fetal exposure to phenytoin, retinoic acid, or other teratogens
clinicalPearls: Knee-chest position is the FIRST nursing action during a Tet spell  -  it increases systemic vascular resistance, which reduces right-to-left shunting and forces blood through the pulmonary valve
Squatting in older, unrepaired children is the self-taught equivalent of knee-chest  -  children instinctively assume this position to relieve cyanosis
Boot-shaped heart on CXR is classic: concavity at the pulmonary artery segment with upturned apex from RV hypertrophy
Do NOT administer digoxin during Tet spells  -  increased contractility worsens infundibular obstruction

who-see-first-adult.title: Who Do You See First: Adult Medical-Surgical
who-see-first-adult.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Who Do You See First: Adult Medical-Surgical, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: New-onset vital sign changes (tachycardia, hypotension, tachypnea, fever, desaturation)
Altered level of consciousness or new confusion
Acute chest pain, dyspnea, or hemodynamic instability
Patients returning from procedures requiring immediate assessment
Fall risk patients reporting dizziness or near-falls
Patients with new lab critical values requiring action
Diabetic patients with blood glucose extremes (<70 or >400 mg/dL)
clinicalPearls: The word 'NEW' is the most important word in prioritization  -  new symptoms always take priority over chronic stable conditions
Acute changes > Chronic conditions. Unexpected findings > Expected findings. Physiological needs > Psychosocial needs
Never choose an answer that delays assessment of an acutely ill patient for a task that can wait or be delegated
On exams, if a patient is 'pale, diaphoretic, and anxious,' this is describing hemodynamic instability  -  see this patient FIRST
A patient who 'doesn't look right' to an experienced nurse often IS deteriorating  -  trust clinical judgment and assess immediately
Post-surgical patients with sudden onset symptoms (pain, dyspnea, bleeding) are ALWAYS a priority over stable patients

who-see-first-pediatric.title: Who Do You See First: Pediatric Scenarios
who-see-first-pediatric.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Who Do You See First: Pediatric Scenarios, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Neonates and infants (<12 months)  -  highest vulnerability due to immature immune and physiological systems
Premature infants  -  increased risk of apnea, respiratory failure, and sepsis
Children with congenital heart defects  -  at risk for heart failure, cyanotic spells, and arrhythmias
Immunocompromised children (chemotherapy, transplant)  -  fever may be the only sign of life-threatening infection
Children with recent surgical procedures  -  higher risk of airway and respiratory complications
Toddlers and preschoolers  -  high risk for foreign body aspiration and accidental poisoning
Adolescents  -  risk of self-harm, substance use, and eating disorders presenting as medical complaints
clinicalPearls: In pediatrics, respiratory failure is the #1 cause of cardiac arrest  -  NOT a cardiac event. A child in respiratory distress is ALWAYS your priority
Tachycardia is the FIRST sign of shock in children. Hypotension is a LATE sign indicating decompensated shock and imminent arrest
A quiet, lethargic child who was previously active is MORE concerning than a screaming, crying child  -  crying requires energy and an intact airway
Never examine the throat of a child with suspected epiglottitis  -  this can cause complete airway obstruction. Keep the child calm, in a position of comfort (usually sitting upright on parent's lap)
Fever in a neonate (<28 days) is ALWAYS an emergency  -  assume sepsis until proven otherwise, even if the baby 'looks fine'
Trust parental intuition: 'My child isn't acting right' is a clinically significant finding that warrants thorough assessment

delegation-traps-exam.title: Delegation Traps: Common Exam Pitfalls
delegation-traps-exam.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Delegation Traps: Common Exam Pitfalls, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to understand scope of practice boundaries (RN vs LPN vs UAP)
Delegating assessment, evaluation, or nursing judgment to non-licensed personnel
Delegating tasks for unstable or unpredictable patients to UAP
Insufficient communication when delegating (vague instructions, no parameters for reporting)
Failing to supervise or follow up on delegated tasks
Assuming competency without verification (float pool staff, new hires, agency nurses)
Over-delegating: assigning too many tasks to one person, compromising safety
Under-delegating: performing all tasks yourself instead of appropriately using team members
clinicalPearls: UAP can COLLECT data (vital signs, I&O) but cannot INTERPRET or ASSESS data. The distinction is critical for exam questions
The most experienced UAP still cannot perform tasks outside UAP scope  -  experience does not expand legal scope of practice
When in doubt on an exam, choose the answer that keeps assessment, evaluation, and nursing judgment with the RN
Delegation is a TWO-WAY accountability: the RN is accountable for the delegation decision; the delegate is accountable for performing the task correctly
'Stable and predictable' are the key words for delegation eligibility  -  if either word doesn't apply, the RN should perform the task
On exams, watch for the answer that says 'do it yourself' when delegation IS appropriate  -  appropriate delegation is part of effective nursing practice

unexpected-vs-expected.title: Unexpected vs Expected Findings: Clinical Red Flags
unexpected-vs-expected.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Unexpected vs Expected Findings: Clinical Red Flags, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to establish baseline assessment data for comparison
Insufficient knowledge of expected post-procedure recovery timelines
Normalizing abnormal findings in patients with chronic conditions
Anchoring bias: focusing on the initial assessment and missing subsequent changes
Handoff communication gaps: not receiving complete information about the patient's trajectory
Alarm fatigue: desensitization to monitor alarms leading to delayed response
Cognitive overload: managing too many patients simultaneously, missing subtle changes
clinicalPearls: On exams, 'Which finding requires the nurse to notify the provider?' = the UNEXPECTED finding that deviates from normal recovery
The timeline matters: low-grade fever on post-op day 1 is expected (Wind/atelectasis). Fever on day 5-7 is unexpected (Wound infection, DVT)
Any SUDDEN onset of symptoms (pain, dyspnea, confusion, bleeding) is almost always unexpected and requires immediate action
Trust the data: compare current assessment to baseline. If something changed, investigate  -  even if the patient 'looks fine'
Expected does NOT mean unimportant  -  expected findings still require documentation, monitoring, and appropriate nursing interventions
When an exam gives you four findings and asks which one to report, three will be expected and one will be the clinical red flag

acute-deterioration-recognition.title: Acute Deterioration Recognition & Rapid Response
acute-deterioration-recognition.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Acute Deterioration Recognition & Rapid Response, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Admission from emergency department with acute illness
Chronic comorbidities (COPD, heart failure, renal disease, diabetes, immunosuppression)
Advanced age with limited physiological reserve
Recent discharge from ICU (within 48 hours)  -  highest risk of re-deterioration
History of previous cardiac arrest or ICU admission during this hospitalization
Sepsis or suspected infection with systemic inflammatory response
Patients receiving opioids or sedatives  -  risk of respiratory depression
clinicalPearls: 60-80% of in-hospital cardiac arrests are preceded by identifiable warning signs 6-24 hours before the event
Tachycardia is often the EARLIEST sign of deterioration  -  a rising heart rate trend is more important than any single reading
You do NOT need to diagnose the problem to activate the Rapid Response Team. 'I'm concerned about my patient' is sufficient
Respiratory rate is the most frequently abnormal vital sign before cardiac arrest and the most commonly missed or inaccurately recorded vital sign
Never reassure yourself by a normal blood pressure alone  -  patients compensate until they can't, and then they crash rapidly
If something feels wrong, ACT. The cost of a false alarm is infinitely less than the cost of failing to rescue

medication-safety-prioritization.title: Medication Safety: Prioritization & Error Prevention
medication-safety-prioritization.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Medication Safety: Prioritization & Error Prevention, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Polypharmacy (5+ medications)  -  increases risk of drug interactions and errors
High-alert medications: insulin, anticoagulants, opioids, concentrated electrolytes
Pediatric and geriatric patients  -  weight-based dosing errors, altered pharmacokinetics
Renal or hepatic impairment  -  requires dose adjustments that may be overlooked
Transitions of care (admission, transfer, discharge)  -  medication reconciliation gaps
Verbal or telephone orders  -  high risk of miscommunication
Interruptions during medication preparation and administration
clinicalPearls: The #1 rule of medication safety: If something doesn't seem right, STOP and CLARIFY before administering
NEVER document medication administration before giving the medication  -  always document AFTER
Independent double-checks for high-alert medications mean two nurses INDEPENDENTLY verify  -  not just co-signing
If a patient says 'That doesn't look like my usual pill'  -  STOP and verify. Patients often know their medications better than we expect
Medication errors are SYSTEM failures, not individual failures. Report all errors to improve the system. A culture of punishment leads to underreporting
The most dangerous medication errors involve the 'wrong dose' of the 'right drug'  -  always double-check dosing calculations

postop-complication-recognition.title: Post-Operative Complication Recognition
postop-complication-recognition.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Post-Operative Complication Recognition, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prolonged surgical time (>3 hours increases all complication risks)
Abdominal or thoracic surgery (highest risk for respiratory complications)
Obesity (BMI >30): increased risk of wound complications, DVT, and respiratory issues
Diabetes mellitus: delayed healing, increased infection risk, glucose instability
Current smoking: impaired wound healing, bronchospasm, atelectasis
Immunosuppression (steroids, chemotherapy, HIV): increased infection susceptibility
clinicalPearls: Post-op fever mnemonic: Wind (Day 1-2), Water (Day 3-5), Wound (Day 5-7), Walking (Day 5-7), Wonder drugs (anytime)
Atelectasis is the #1 most common post-op complication  -  prevention with incentive spirometry and early ambulation is essential
If evisceration occurs: NEVER push organs back in. Cover with sterile saline-moistened gauze, position supine with knees bent, NPO, call surgeon STAT
The 6 P's of neurovascular compromise: Pain (out of proportion), Pallor, Pulselessness, Paresthesia, Paralysis, Poikilothermia (cool extremity)
Sudden dyspnea + chest pain + tachycardia on post-op day 5-7 = PE until proven otherwise
Malignant hyperthermia is a genetic condition  -  always ask about family history of anesthesia complications

pediatric-vs-adult-priorities.title: Pediatric vs Adult Prioritization Differences
pediatric-vs-adult-priorities.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pediatric vs Adult Prioritization Differences, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to use age-appropriate vital sign ranges (applying adult parameters to children)
Underestimating the significance of tachycardia in pediatric patients
Assuming hypotension is required for shock diagnosis in children
Using adult medication doses without weight-based calculations
Missing subtle behavioral cues in pre-verbal children (lethargy, poor feeding, decreased play)
Delayed recognition of respiratory failure as a precursor to cardiac arrest
Inadequate fluid resuscitation (not using weight-based bolus calculations)
clinicalPearls: Children are NOT small adults  -  their physiology, pharmacology, and clinical presentations are fundamentally different
In adults, cardiac arrest is usually cardiac in origin. In children, it's almost always respiratory  -  AIRWAY and BREATHING are paramount
Tachycardia = first sign of shock in children. Hypotension = LATE sign meaning decompensation has occurred
A quiet, lethargic child is MORE concerning than a screaming child  -  crying requires energy and an intact cardiorespiratory system
1 mm of airway edema reduces a pediatric airway by 44% vs 19% in adults  -  small changes have huge impact
Parental report of 'something is different about my child' is a clinically significant finding that warrants immediate assessment

first-action-logic.title: First Action Logic: What to Do Before Calling the Provider
first-action-logic.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de First Action Logic: What to Do Before Calling the Provider, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Calling the provider without completing an assessment first (unprepared communication)
Delaying independent nursing actions while waiting for provider callback
Failing to act within nursing scope during emergencies (e.g., waiting for an order to apply O2)
Not knowing which actions require orders vs which are independent nursing interventions
Over-reliance on provider direction for routine nursing decisions
Incomplete data collection before provider notification (missing vital signs, assessment findings)
Calling at inappropriate times or through wrong channels (non-urgent pages for non-urgent findings)
clinicalPearls: On exams, 'Notify the healthcare provider' is rarely the FIRST action  -  assess and intervene within scope FIRST
The exception: if the question states the nurse has already assessed and the findings require medical intervention beyond nursing scope
In emergencies, you have standing authority to: maintain airway, apply O2, initiate CPR, apply pressure to bleeding, position for safety
SBAR is the gold standard for provider communication: Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation
A nurse who calls a provider and says 'I don't know what's wrong but the patient looks bad' has NOT prepared adequately
Always end your SBAR with a recommendation  -  this demonstrates clinical judgment and often expedites appropriate orders

assignment-making-scenarios.title: Assignment Making: Matching Patient Acuity to Staff
assignment-making-scenarios.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Assignment Making: Matching Patient Acuity to Staff, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Float or agency nurses unfamiliar with unit-specific patient populations and protocols
New graduate nurses in orientation or early independent practice
High patient-to-nurse ratios compromising safe care delivery
Multiple admissions, discharges, and transfers (ADT) creating unpredictable workload shifts
Patients requiring specialized skills (ventilator management, CRRT, complex wound care) assigned to nurses without demonstrated competency
Inadequate staffing mix (too few RNs for the acuity of patients on the unit)
Failure to account for non-direct care responsibilities (charge duties, precepting, orientation)
clinicalPearls: Assignment making is about COMPETENCY, not just convenience. The goal is to match patient needs to staff capabilities
A float nurse should NEVER receive the most complex patients on the unit  -  they lack unit-specific knowledge and familiarity with protocols
New graduates need stable, predictable patients  -  not complex ones 'to learn from.' Learning occurs with appropriate support, not trial by fire
LPNs cannot perform initial assessments, create care plans, or administer IV push medications. Assignment must reflect these scope limitations
Continuity of care improves outcomes  -  assign the same nurse to the same patient across shifts whenever possible
When an exam question asks 'Which patient should be assigned to the float nurse?'  -  the answer is always the most STABLE patient

abcs-life-threats.title: ABCs & Immediate Life Threat Prioritization
abcs-life-threats.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de ABCs & Immediate Life Threat Prioritization, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to assess airway patency before addressing other complaints
Distraction by dramatic but non-life-threatening findings (e.g., open fracture)
Treating pain before ensuring adequate oxygenation and ventilation
Addressing psychosocial concerns before stabilizing physiological parameters
Failure to recognize early signs of airway compromise (stridor, accessory muscle use)
Delayed recognition of obstructive airway emergencies (anaphylaxis, epiglottitis)
Ignoring subtle signs of circulatory compromise (delayed capillary refill, mottling)
Over-focusing on single vital sign abnormalities without assessing the full ABC picture
clinicalPearls: If the patient can speak in complete sentences, the airway is patent and breathing is sufficient for the moment
In trauma, assume cervical spine injury until cleared - use jaw thrust instead of head-tilt chin-lift
Bleeding that you can see is less dangerous than bleeding you cannot see (internal hemorrhage)
On NCLEX: when multiple patients need attention, always address the ABC problem first

unstable-vs-stable.title: Unstable vs Stable: Prioritization Framework
unstable-vs-stable.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Unstable vs Stable: Prioritization Framework, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to recognize 'new onset' vs 'chronic/expected' presentation
Assuming that stable vital signs mean the patient is clinically stable
Normalizing abnormal findings because the patient 'always looks like that'
Failure to trend vital signs and recognize deteriorating patterns
Over-reliance on technology without clinical bedside assessment
Cognitive anchoring to the initial diagnosis without considering new developments
Failure to reassess after interventions to determine effectiveness
Delaying assessment of patients who 'look fine' but have concerning vital signs
clinicalPearls: The word 'NEW' is the most important word in clinical prioritization - new symptoms always take priority
A patient with 'normal' vital signs can still be unstable if those values represent a significant change from their baseline
Expected pain after surgery is stable; new-onset sharp chest pain after surgery is unstable (possible PE)
When in doubt, assess the patient at the bedside - no amount of data review replaces a hands-on evaluation

who-to-see-first.title: Who to See First: Clinical Decision Framework
who-to-see-first.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Who to See First: Clinical Decision Framework, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Seeing patients in room order rather than acuity order
Addressing the most vocal or demanding patient first instead of the most critical
Confusing urgency with importance (a patient demanding pain medication vs. a silent patient with declining SpO2)
Failure to delegate appropriate tasks to free time for high-acuity patients
Not reassessing priorities after new information becomes available
Anchoring on initial morning assessment without accounting for acute changes
Inadequate shift handoff leading to missed clinical concerns
clinicalPearls: The sickest patient is not always the loudest - a patient who is too sick to call for help is more critical than one who is yelling for pain medication
On NCLEX, when all patients seem equally urgent, apply ABCs to differentiate
Expected findings for a diagnosis are never the priority over unexpected/new findings in another patient
When delegating to UAP, you free yourself to focus on the patients who require RN-level assessment and judgment

delegation-rules-scope.title: Delegation Rules & Scope of Practice
delegation-rules-scope.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Delegation Rules & Scope of Practice, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Delegating assessment, evaluation, or clinical judgment to UAPs
Failing to verify the delegate's competency for the specific task
Delegating to an overwhelmed staff member without checking their workload
Assuming that RPN/LPN scope is identical to RN scope
Failing to provide clear, complete instructions and expected reporting parameters
Not following up after delegation to evaluate task completion and patient outcome
Delegating unstable or unpredictable patient care to inappropriate personnel
Delegating tasks that are excluded from the delegate's scope by regulation
clinicalPearls: The RN ALWAYS retains accountability for delegated tasks - you can delegate the task but never the responsibility
Assessment is NEVER delegated - UAPs collect data (vital signs), but the RN interprets and assesses
When in doubt about scope, check: Can the delegate legally do this? Do they have the competency? Is the patient stable enough?
On NCLEX, if the question involves an unstable patient, the answer is almost always 'the RN does it' - not delegated

sbar-escalation.title: SBAR Escalation: When & How to Escalate
sbar-escalation.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de SBAR Escalation: When & How to Escalate, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Hesitation to contact physicians due to intimidation or hierarchical culture
Failure to articulate clinical concern clearly and concisely
Presenting data without clinical interpretation or recommendation
Accepting a physician response that does not adequately address the concern
Failure to use the chain of command when initial escalation is inadequate
Normalizing abnormal findings or dismissing nursing intuition
Delayed escalation due to 'wanting to collect more data first'
Poor documentation of escalation attempts and provider responses
clinicalPearls: If you are worried enough to consider escalating, escalate. Trust your clinical judgment.
Always include your ASSESSMENT in SBAR - the provider needs your clinical interpretation, not just numbers
Document every provider communication: time, name, what was discussed, response received, and orders given
Using the chain of command is a professional obligation when patient safety is at risk - never let hierarchy prevent escalation

post-op-prioritization.title: Post-Op Complications: Prioritization
post-op-prioritization.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Post-Op Complications: Prioritization, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Advanced age (decreased physiological reserve and healing capacity)
Obesity (increased risk of wound complications, DVT, and respiratory issues)
Smoking history (impaired wound healing, increased respiratory complications)
Diabetes mellitus (delayed healing, increased infection risk)
Prolonged surgical time (increased risk of all complications)
clinicalPearls: Atelectasis is the #1 most common post-op complication - incentive spirometry and early ambulation are the best prevention
Post-op fever timeline: Wind (atelectasis, day 1-2), Water (UTI, day 3-5), Wound (infection, day 5-7), Walking (DVT/PE, day 5-7), Wonder drugs (drug fever, any time)
If a wound eviscerates: do NOT push organs back in. Cover with sterile saline-soaked gauze, position supine with knees bent, NPO, call surgeon STAT
Post-op day 5-7 is the highest risk period for pulmonary embolism from DVT dislodgment

psoriasis.title: Psoriasis
psoriasis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Psoriasis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of psoriasis (genetic predisposition, HLA-Cw6)
Streptococcal throat infection (trigger for guttate psoriasis)
clinicalPearls: Auspitz sign (pinpoint bleeding when scales are removed) is characteristic of psoriasis
Psoriasis is a lifelong condition requiring long-term management and flare prevention strategies
Screen for psoriatic arthritis, cardiovascular disease, and depression as common comorbidities

eczema-atopic-dermatitis.title: Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis)
eczema-atopic-dermatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of atopy (asthma, allergic rhinitis, eczema)
Environmental allergens (dust mites, pet dander, pollen)
clinicalPearls: Emollients are the cornerstone of treatment, applied liberally and frequently, especially within 3 minutes after bathing
The itch-scratch cycle worsens the condition; controlling pruritus is essential to prevent lichenification
Monitor for eczema herpeticum (widespread herpes simplex superinfection) which requires urgent antiviral treatment

contact-dermatitis.title: Contact Dermatitis
contact-dermatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Contact Dermatitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prior sensitization to allergens (nickel, latex, fragrances)
Frequent hand washing or wet work
Exposure to poison ivy, oak, or sumac
Use of fragranced personal care products
clinicalPearls: Identification and avoidance of the offending agent is the most important management step
Linear vesicular streaks on the skin are classic for plant-derived allergic contact dermatitis (poison ivy)
Patch testing is the gold standard for identifying specific allergens in allergic contact dermatitis

cellulitis.title: Cellulitis
cellulitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cellulitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Skin break or wound (surgical site, insect bite, abrasion)
Peripheral vascular disease or venous insufficiency
Tinea pedis (athlete's foot - portal of entry)
clinicalPearls: Mark borders of erythema with a skin marker to objectively track progression or resolution
Elevate the affected extremity to reduce edema and promote venous return
Treat underlying tinea pedis or skin breaks to prevent recurrence, as these serve as portals of entry

herpes-zoster-shingles.title: Herpes Zoster (Shingles)
herpes-zoster-shingles.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Herpes Zoster (Shingles), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age greater than 50 years (declining cellular immunity)
clinicalPearls: Antivirals must be started within 72 hours of rash onset to be most effective in reducing severity and postherpetic neuralgia risk
Vesicles on the tip of the nose (Hutchinson sign) indicate nasociliary nerve involvement and risk of herpes zoster ophthalmicus, requiring urgent ophthalmology referral
Shingrix (recombinant zoster vaccine) is recommended for adults 50 years and older, including those with prior shingles episode

acne-vulgaris.title: Acne Vulgaris
acne-vulgaris.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Acne Vulgaris, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Occlusive headbands, helmets, or chin straps
clinicalPearls: Topical retinoids are the foundation of acne treatment and should be used consistently as maintenance therapy
Isotretinoin is the only medication that targets all four acne pathogenic factors but requires strict pregnancy prevention (iPLEDGE program)
Improvement takes 6-8 weeks; educate patients to continue treatment and avoid picking lesions to prevent scarring

melanoma.title: Melanoma
melanoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Melanoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of severe sunburns, especially in childhood
Fair skin, light eyes, red or blonde hair (Fitzpatrick types I-II)
Excessive UV exposure (sunlight or tanning beds)
Large number of moles (>50 common nevi)
clinicalPearls: ABCDE criteria: Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation, Diameter >6 mm, Evolving - use for early melanoma detection
Breslow depth (tumor thickness) is the most important prognostic factor for survival
Educate all patients on monthly self-skin examinations and annual dermatologic screening, especially high-risk individuals

basal-cell-carcinoma.title: Basal Cell Carcinoma
basal-cell-carcinoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Basal Cell Carcinoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic cumulative UV radiation exposure (occupational or recreational)
Fair skin, light eyes, and red or blonde hair (Fitzpatrick skin types I-II)
History of blistering sunburns, especially in childhood
Prior radiation therapy to the affected area
Basal cell nevus syndrome (Gorlin syndrome - autosomal dominant)
Male sex and age over 50 years
clinicalPearls: BCC is the most common cancer in humans but has the lowest metastatic potential - local tissue destruction is the primary concern if left untreated
The classic presentation is a pearly papule with telangiectasias on the face that bleeds easily and fails to heal - patients often present saying they have a pimple that will not go away
Patients with one BCC have a 50% chance of developing another within 5 years - lifelong surveillance and sun protection are essential

squamous-cell-carcinoma.title: Squamous Cell Carcinoma
squamous-cell-carcinoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Squamous Cell Carcinoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Immunosuppression (organ transplant recipients at highest risk)
Fair skin and history of sunburns
Chronic wounds, scars, or areas of chronic inflammation (Marjolin ulcer)
Tobacco use (especially for lip and oral SCC)
Exposure to arsenic or industrial carcinogens
Human papillomavirus infection (genital and periungual SCC)
clinicalPearls: SCC has a higher metastatic potential than BCC - regional lymph node assessment is part of the clinical evaluation, especially for high-risk lesions
Actinic keratoses are the precursors to SCC - treating them with cryotherapy or topical 5-fluorouracil is cancer prevention
Organ transplant recipients on chronic immunosuppression develop SCC at 65-250 times the general population rate - they require skin examinations every 3-6 months

stevens-johnson-syndrome.title: Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS)
stevens-johnson-syndrome.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Medication exposure (sulfonamides, anticonvulsants, allopurinol, NSAIDs, antibiotics)
HLA-B*1502 genotype (carbamazepine-associated SJS in Southeast Asian descent)
clinicalPearls: Immediate discontinuation of the causative medication is the single most important intervention to improve outcomes
SJS involves less than 10% TBSA epidermal detachment; 10-30% is SJS/TEN overlap; greater than 30% is TEN
Mucous membrane involvement (oral, ocular, genital) occurs in over 90% of cases and requires specialized multidisciplinary care

toxic-epidermal-necrolysis.title: Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN)
toxic-epidermal-necrolysis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Medication exposure (same high-risk drugs as SJS)
clinicalPearls: TEN is a dermatologic emergency with 25-35% mortality, requiring burn unit level care
Gentle handling is critical, as mechanical forces cause further epidermal detachment; use lift sheets and minimize skin contact
All medications the patient has started within the past 1-8 weeks should be considered potential causative agents and discontinued if possible

urticaria.title: Urticaria (Hives)
urticaria.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Urticaria (Hives), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Individual wheals lasting longer than 24 hours or leaving bruising should raise suspicion for urticarial vasculitis, not simple urticaria
Always assess for angioedema and anaphylaxis when urticaria is present, as these represent life-threatening progression
Patients with angioedema history or anaphylaxis risk should carry two epinephrine auto-injectors at all times

scabies.title: Scabies
scabies.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Scabies, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Close prolonged skin-to-skin contact with infested individual
Crowded living conditions (shelters, dormitories, long-term care facilities)
Healthcare workers in contact with infested patients
clinicalPearls: Pruritus may persist for 2-4 weeks after successful treatment due to ongoing hypersensitivity reaction to dead mites and debris; this does not indicate treatment failure
All household members and close contacts must be treated simultaneously to prevent re-infestation cycle
Crusted (Norwegian) scabies in immunocompromised patients is extremely contagious due to massive mite burden and requires airborne plus contact precautions

impetigo.title: Impetigo
impetigo.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Impetigo, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Strict hand hygiene is the most important measure to prevent spread. Wash hands thoroughly after touching lesions.
Keep child's fingernails trimmed short to prevent scratching and autoinoculation to other body areas.
Do NOT share towels, washcloths, clothing, or personal items. Launder linens and clothing in hot water separately.
Child may return to school/daycare 24 hours after starting antibiotic treatment and lesions are no longer weeping.

pemphigus-vulgaris.title: Pemphigus Vulgaris
pemphigus-vulgaris.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pemphigus Vulgaris, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Oral mucosal erosions are typically the first manifestation and may precede skin blistering by weeks to months
Positive Nikolsky sign (skin shearing with gentle lateral pressure) is characteristic but also seen in other blistering disorders (SJS/TEN, bullous pemphigoid)
Anti-desmoglein antibody titers correlate with disease activity and can be used to monitor treatment response

potassium-imbalance-rpn.title: Hyperkalemia vs Hypokalemia  -  ECG Changes & Cellular Physiology
potassium-imbalance-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hyperkalemia vs Hypokalemia  -  ECG Changes & Cellular Physiology, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HYPERKALEMIA: Acute kidney injury or chronic kidney disease (impaired renal excretion)
HYPERKALEMIA: ACE inhibitors and ARBs (reduce aldosterone-mediated K⁺ secretion)
HYPERKALEMIA: Massive tissue destruction (crush injury, rhabdomyolysis, burns, tumor lysis syndrome)
HYPERKALEMIA: Metabolic acidosis (H⁺ shifts into cells, K⁺ shifts out  -  transcellular shift)
