HYPERKALEMIA: Excessive potassium supplementation or salt substitutes (KCl-based)
HYPERKALEMIA: Addison's disease (aldosterone deficiency impairs K⁺ excretion)
HYPERKALEMIA: Hemolyzed blood sample (pseudohyperkalemia  -  always verify before treating)
HYPOKALEMIA: Loop diuretics (furosemide) and thiazide diuretics (hydrochlorothiazide)
HYPOKALEMIA: Prolonged vomiting, nasogastric suction, or diarrhea
HYPOKALEMIA: Metabolic alkalosis (H⁺ shifts out of cells, K⁺ shifts in)
HYPOKALEMIA: Excessive insulin administration (drives K⁺ intracellularly)
HYPOKALEMIA: Cushing syndrome or exogenous corticosteroids (aldosterone effect)
HYPOKALEMIA: Inadequate dietary intake (anorexia, alcoholism, malnutrition)
HYPOKALEMIA: Renal tubular acidosis (types I and II cause potassium wasting)
HYPOKALEMIA: Beta-2 agonist use (albuterol shifts K⁺ into cells)
clinicalPearls: Potassium is the most dangerous electrolyte to replace IV  -  never bolus push, always on a pump, always verify urine output first
Peaked T waves are the EARLIEST ECG change in hyperkalemia  -  if you see them, the K⁺ is already dangerously high
The U wave is pathognomonic for hypokalemia  -  a small positive deflection after the T wave that represents delayed ventricular repolarization
Digoxin + hypokalemia = lethal combination  -  low K⁺ allows digoxin to bind more cardiac Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase sites, increasing toxicity risk even at therapeutic digoxin levels
Calcium gluconate does NOT lower potassium  -  it stabilizes the heart. Always pair with a K⁺-lowering strategy (insulin/dextrose, dialysis, or cation binders)
Hypokalemia will NOT correct until hypomagnesemia is corrected  -  always check and replete magnesium concurrently
Acid-base status directly affects K⁺ distribution: acidosis pushes K⁺ OUT of cells (hyperkalemia), alkalosis pushes K⁺ INTO cells (hypokalemia)
Hemolyzed blood samples cause falsely elevated K⁺ (pseudohyperkalemia)  -  if the level is unexpected, redraw and verify before treating
In hyperkalemia, the ECG progression is: peaked T → prolonged PR → lost P wave → widened QRS → sine wave → V-fib/asystole
Administer oral KCl with food and a full glass of water  -  it can cause esophageal and gastric ulceration if taken on an empty stomach

hyperkalemia-emergency-np.title: Hyperkalemia Emergency: ECG Progression & Advanced Treatment
hyperkalemia-emergency-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hyperkalemia Emergency: ECG Progression & Advanced Treatment, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: End-stage renal disease (most common cause of severe hyperkalemia)
Tumor lysis syndrome (massive intracellular K⁺ release from lysed tumor cells)
Massive hemolysis (transfusion reactions, autoimmune hemolytic anemia)
Medication combinations: ACE inhibitor + K⁺-sparing diuretic + NSAID (triple whammy)
Succinylcholine administration in susceptible patients (burns, crush, denervation injuries)
Diabetic ketoacidosis (acidosis-driven transcellular shift despite total body K⁺ depletion)
clinicalPearls: The ECG progression of hyperkalemia follows a predictable sequence: peaked T → prolonged PR → absent P → wide QRS → sine wave → arrest. Each stage correlates with increasing K⁺ and worsening conduction delay.
Calcium gluconate is ALWAYS the first intervention for hyperkalemia with ECG changes  -  it works within minutes to stabilize the heart and buys time for definitive K⁺-lowering therapy
All K⁺-shifting therapies (insulin, bicarb, albuterol) are TEMPORARY  -  potassium eventually shifts back out of cells. Must combine with removal strategies (dialysis, binders, diuretics)
The triple whammy: ACE inhibitor + potassium-sparing diuretic + NSAID is a common medication combination that causes life-threatening hyperkalemia. Always review the medication list.

In DKA, serum K⁺ may appear normal or elevated on admission due to acidosis-driven shift OUT of cells, but total body K⁺ is severely depleted. Once insulin and fluids are started, K⁺ drops rapidly  -  monitor extremely closely.
Atrial tissue is more sensitive to hyperkalemia than ventricular tissue  -  this is why P waves disappear before QRS widening
Never use calcium gluconate as the sole treatment  -  it does not change serum K⁺ and its protective effect wears off in 30-60 minutes

shock-syndromes.title: Shock States (RN Mastery)
shock-syndromes.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Shock States (RN Mastery), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Septic Shock: Start antibiotics within 1 hour
Hypovolemic Shock: Fluid bolus is the priority
Cardiogenic Shock: Morphine/Nitros to reduce heart workload

sepsis-mastery.title: Sepsis & SIRS Recognition
sepsis-mastery.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sepsis & SIRS Recognition, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Lactate level is a key marker of tissue hypoxia
Blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics
Source control is vital

burn-management.title: Burn Injury & Resuscitation
burn-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Burn Injury & Resuscitation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Airway is the #1 priority (check for soot/singed hairs)
First 24 hours focus on fluid resuscitation
High risk for infection and hypothermia

compartment-syndrome.title: Compartment Syndrome
compartment-syndrome.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Compartment Syndrome, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Pain with PASSIVE STRETCH is the earliest and most reliable sign of compartment syndrome - do not wait for pulselessness
The 6 P's develop in order: Pain → Pressure → Paresthesia → Paralysis → Pallor → Pulselessness - by the time you lose pulses, tissue death has occurred
NEVER elevate the limb above heart level - this reduces arterial inflow and worsens ischemia; keep at heart level

malignant-hyperthermia.title: Malignant Hyperthermia
malignant-hyperthermia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Malignant Hyperthermia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: STOP triggering anesthetic agent IMMEDIATELY and hyperventilate with 100% O2
Call for help - this requires multiple providers simultaneously
Dantrolene MUST be stocked wherever general anesthesia is administered
Genetic testing available: caffeine-halothane contracture test
FAMILY HISTORY is critical - autosomal dominant inheritance
Cool patient aggressively: iced IV fluids, ice packs to axilla/groin/neck

anaphylaxis.title: Anaphylaxis Management
anaphylaxis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Anaphylaxis Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Known allergies (food, insect stings, medications, latex)
History of atopy (asthma, eczema, allergic rhinitis)
clinicalPearls: ABCs first - secure airway, support breathing and circulation
Epinephrine is the ONLY first-line treatment - do NOT delay for antihistamines
Biphasic reaction possible: monitor patient for 4-6 hours after initial resolution
EpiPen teaching: remove safety cap, press firmly into outer thigh, hold 10 seconds
Identify and strictly avoid triggers going forward
Patient should wear medical alert bracelet and carry EpiPen at all times

siadh-di.title: SIADH et diabète insipide
siadh-di.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de SIADH et diabète insipide, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: SIADH: CNS disorders, lung cancer (SCLC), medications (SSRIs, carbamazepine), post-surgical state
DI: Head trauma, pituitary surgery, brain tumors, lithium therapy
clinicalPearls: SIADH: Fluid restriction is the priority
DI: Fluid replacement is the priority
Monitor sodium levels every 4-6 hours

hypothyroidism-basics.title: Hypothyroïdie
hypothyroidism-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hypothyroïdie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Monitor TSH levels regularly
Takes 4-6 weeks for dose adjustments to show full effect
Lifelong treatment required
Teach signs of hyperthyroidism (overreplacement): tachycardia, tremor, weight loss, heat intolerance

adrenal-insufficiency.title: Insuffisance surrénalienne
adrenal-insufficiency.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insuffisance surrénalienne, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Addisonian crisis is LIFE-THREATENING: N/V, severe hypotension, shock - treat with IV hydrocortisone STAT and aggressive NS bolus
Patient should wear medical alert bracelet at all times
DOUBLE dose of hydrocortisone during minor illness, TRIPLE for major stress/surgery
NEVER stop corticosteroids abruptly - must taper to prevent crisis

thyroidectomy.title: Thyroïdectomie
thyroidectomy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Thyroïdectomie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Support neck when repositioning (avoid hyperextension of surgical site)
Semi-Fowler's position to reduce edema and promote drainage
Check POSTERIOR dressing - blood pools behind the neck
Calcium gluconate AND tracheostomy set at bedside
Assess voice quality every 1-2 hours (hoarseness may indicate nerve damage)
Report ANY breathing difficulty, stridor, or neck swelling IMMEDIATELY

siadh-basics-rpn.title: SIADH – RPN
siadh-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de SIADH – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC)  -  ectopic ADH production (most common malignant cause)
CNS disorders: head trauma, stroke, subarachnoid hemorrhage, meningitis, encephalitis
Pulmonary conditions: pneumonia, tuberculosis, positive-pressure ventilation
Medications: SSRIs, carbamazepine, cyclophosphamide, vincristine, opioids, NSAIDs
Post-surgical state (pain, anesthesia, and stress stimulate ADH release)
Elderly patients (multiple medication use and age-related ADH sensitivity)
clinicalPearls: FLUID RESTRICTION is the cornerstone of SIADH treatment  -  not sodium supplementation

Osmotic Demyelination Syndrome (ODS, formerly Central Pontine Myelinolysis) occurs when chronic hyponatremia is corrected too rapidly: rapid water loss from brain cells strips myelin sheaths, causing irreversible pontine damage with dysarthria, dysphagia, and quadriparesis
Remember: SIADH = Dilute serum (low osmolality) + Concentrated urine (high osmolality) + Euvolemia  -  this triad is the diagnostic hallmark
SIADH is a diagnosis of exclusion: must rule out hypothyroidism, adrenal insufficiency, and diuretic use before diagnosing

acromegaly-basics-rpn.title: Acromégalie – RPN
acromegaly-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Acromégalie – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Ectopic GHRH secretion from neuroendocrine tumors (rare)
clinicalPearls: Ring size and shoe size increasing over years is a classic patient complaint  -  compare old photographs to document progressive facial changes
Cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death in acromegaly  -  screen aggressively for hypertension, cardiomyopathy, and diabetes

Distinguish from GIGANTISM: same GH excess pathology, but gigantism occurs BEFORE epiphyseal closure (childhood) causing excessive linear growth; acromegaly occurs AFTER closure (adults) causing acral and soft tissue enlargement
Post-transsphenoidal surgery, watch for clear nasal drainage (CSF rhinorrhea)  -  have patient lean forward and test fluid for glucose (CSF glucose is high)

prolactinoma-basics.title: Prolactinome
prolactinoma-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prolactinome, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex (more commonly diagnosed in reproductive-age women)
Medications that block dopamine: antipsychotics, metoclopramide, risperidone
Chest wall irritation (herpes zoster, surgical scars)
clinicalPearls: Prolactinomas are the ONLY pituitary adenomas where MEDICATION is first-line treatment  -  all other pituitary adenomas typically require surgery first
Prolactin level roughly correlates with tumor size: PRL >200 ng/mL almost always indicates a macroprolactinoma (not just stalk effect)

The 'stalk effect' or 'hook effect': any mass compressing the pituitary stalk interrupts dopamine delivery and mildly elevates prolactin (<100 ng/mL). A giant adenoma with only mildly elevated prolactin is likely a NON-functioning adenoma causing stalk compression, NOT a prolactinoma
Dopamine agonists can shrink prolactinomas dramatically  -  monitor for CSF rhinorrhea as large suprasellar tumors shrink away from the skull base

thyrotoxicosis-basics.title: Thyrotoxicose
thyrotoxicosis-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Thyrotoxicose, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Untreated or undertreated Graves' disease (most common underlying cause)
Infection or sepsis in a hyperthyroid patient (most common precipitant)
Surgery (especially thyroid surgery without adequate preoperative preparation)
DKA or other acute metabolic derangements
clinicalPearls: Medication ORDER matters in thyroid storm: PTU first → wait 1 hour → THEN iodide. Giving iodide before PTU provides raw material for MORE hormone synthesis (Jod-Basedow phenomenon)

NEVER give aspirin for fever in thyrotoxicosis  -  aspirin displaces T4 from thyroid-binding globulin, dramatically increasing FREE (active) T4 levels and worsening the crisis. Use acetaminophen only.
Thyroid storm has a Burch-Wartofsky Point Scale ≥45 (scores temperature, CNS effects, GI-hepatic dysfunction, heart rate, heart failure, atrial fibrillation, and precipitating event)
Graves' disease is the most common cause of thyrotoxicosis overall  -  look for the triad of hyperthyroidism + exophthalmos + pretibial myxedema

np-testbank-advanced-assessment.title: NP Advanced Assessment Test Bank
np-testbank-advanced-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NP Advanced Assessment Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incomplete history-taking leading to missed diagnoses
Failure to perform age-appropriate screening examinations
Over-reliance on diagnostic testing without thorough physical examination
Cognitive biases: anchoring, premature closure, confirmation bias
Inadequate social determinants of health assessment
Failure to assess medication adherence and polypharmacy
Missing red flag symptoms requiring urgent referral
Incomplete family history for genetic risk stratification
clinicalPearls: Always assess for red flags in headache: thunderclap onset, worst headache of life, fever with neck stiffness, new onset after age 50, focal neurological deficits, papilledema
The SAAG (serum-ascites albumin gradient) ≥1.1 g/dL indicates portal hypertension; <1.1 suggests peritoneal malignancy, TB, or nephrotic syndrome
BNP <100 pg/mL effectively rules out heart failure; BNP >400 strongly suggests decompensated HF; values between 100-400 require clinical correlation
In acute coronary syndrome, troponin elevation with a rise-and-fall pattern distinguishes acute MI from chronic troponin elevation seen in CKD or stable HF
The Wells score for PE: >6 points = high probability, proceed directly to CTPA; ≤4 points = low probability, obtain D-dimer first
A normal D-dimer has >95% negative predictive value for PE/DVT; however, it is elevated in pregnancy, malignancy, infection, surgery, and advanced age, limiting specificity
Orthostatic hypotension is defined as a drop in systolic BP ≥20 mmHg or diastolic BP ≥10 mmHg within 3 minutes of standing; assess in all patients with dizziness or syncope
The CHA2DS2-VASc score determines anticoagulation need in atrial fibrillation: score ≥2 in males or ≥3 in females warrants anticoagulation (DOAC preferred over warfarin)

np-testbank-prescribing.title: NP Prescribing & Pharmacotherapeutics Test Bank
np-testbank-prescribing.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NP Prescribing & Pharmacotherapeutics Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Polypharmacy (≥5 medications) increasing drug interaction risk
Renal impairment requiring dose adjustments for renally cleared drugs
Hepatic impairment affecting drug metabolism (CYP450 system)
Genetic polymorphisms in CYP2D6, CYP2C19, CYP2C9 altering drug metabolism
Extremes of age: neonates (immature metabolism) and elderly (declining organ function)
Pregnancy and lactation requiring careful risk-benefit analysis
Non-adherence due to cost, complexity, or side effects
Herbal supplement interactions (St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4, reducing drug levels)
clinicalPearls: CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole, erythromycin, grapefruit juice, ritonavir) increase levels of most statins, CCBs, benzodiazepines, and many other drugs; always check for interactions
CYP2D6 poor metabolizers cannot convert codeine to morphine, making it ineffective for pain; ultra-rapid metabolizers produce excessive morphine, risking fatal respiratory depression
When switching from an ACE inhibitor to sacubitril/valsartan (Entresto), a 36-hour washout period is mandatory to prevent life-threatening angioedema from dual neprilysin and ACE inhibition
Fluoroquinolones carry FDA black box warnings for tendon rupture, peripheral neuropathy, CNS effects, and aortic dissection; reserve for infections without safer alternatives
Methotrexate requires folate supplementation (folic acid 1mg daily) to reduce toxicity; monitor CBC, hepatic panel, and renal function; teratogenic (Category X) requiring contraception

The Beers Criteria identifies medications to avoid in elderly patients: benzodiazepines (fall risk), first-generation antihistamines (anticholinergic), long-acting sulfonylureas (hypoglycemia), and NSAIDs (GI/renal risk)
PTU is preferred over methimazole in first trimester pregnancy (methimazole associated with aplasia cutis and choanal atresia); switch to methimazole in second/third trimester
Warfarin is dosed based on INR response, not fixed dosing; genetic testing for CYP2C9 and VKORC1 polymorphisms can guide initial dosing

np-testbank-differential-diagnosis.title: NP Differential Diagnosis Test Bank
np-testbank-differential-diagnosis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NP Differential Diagnosis Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cognitive biases leading to diagnostic error (anchoring, premature closure, availability heuristic)
Incomplete history-taking missing critical red flags
Atypical disease presentations in elderly, immunocompromised, or diabetic patients
Low index of suspicion for rare but life-threatening conditions
Failure to consider demographic-specific risks (age, sex, race, occupation)
Communication barriers (language, health literacy, cultural differences)
Time pressure in clinical settings reducing thorough evaluation
Over-reliance on technology and testing without clinical correlation
clinicalPearls: The 'Big 6' must-not-miss diagnoses for acute chest pain: MI, aortic dissection, PE, tension pneumothorax, cardiac tamponade, and esophageal rupture — all require rapid identification to prevent death

Aortic dissection is the great mimicker: it can present as MI (coronary ostia involvement), stroke (carotid involvement), acute limb ischemia (iliac involvement), or renal failure (renal artery involvement)

A negative D-dimer (with appropriate pretest probability per PERC or Wells) effectively rules out PE/DVT; however, D-dimer loses utility in hospitalized patients, post-surgical patients, and those with active malignancy
In acute monoarticular arthritis, always perform arthrocentesis to rule out septic arthritis before attributing to gout or pseudogout; septic arthritis is a surgical emergency
Troponin elevation is NOT specific for MI: other causes include PE, myocarditis, heart failure, renal failure, sepsis, and demand ischemia — clinical context determines the diagnosis

Temporal arteritis (giant cell arteritis) in patients >50 with new headache, jaw claudication, visual changes, or elevated ESR >50 requires immediate empiric corticosteroids pending temporal artery biopsy to prevent irreversible blindness

The HEART score (History, ECG, Age, Risk factors, Troponin) stratifies chest pain patients: 0-3 = low risk (safe for discharge with follow-up), 4-6 = moderate (observation), 7-10 = high risk (admission and intervention)
Ectopic pregnancy must be on the differential for any reproductive-age female presenting with abdominal/pelvic pain — always obtain a beta-hCG before imaging

np-testbank-emergency-management.title: NP Emergency & Complex Management Test Bank
np-testbank-emergency-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de NP Emergency & Complex Management Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Delayed recognition of sepsis leading to increased mortality (each hour delay in antibiotics increases mortality 7.6%)
Missed STEMI due to atypical presentation (elderly, women, diabetics may lack typical chest pain)
Inappropriate insulin administration in DKA with hypokalemia (<3.3 mEq/L) risking cardiac arrest
Rapid correction of chronic hyponatremia causing osmotic demyelination syndrome
Failure to recognize and treat tension pneumothorax (clinical diagnosis, do not delay for imaging)
Missed massive PE in patients with unexplained hemodynamic instability
Inadequate fluid resuscitation volume in septic shock (minimum 30 mL/kg crystalloid)
Over-reliance on fever for infection identification (immunocompromised patients may be afebrile with severe infection)
clinicalPearls: In septic shock, every hour delay in antibiotic administration increases mortality by approximately 7.6% — administer broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour of recognition, even before culture results
Never start insulin in DKA until potassium is confirmed >3.3 mEq/L — insulin drives potassium intracellularly and can cause fatal hypokalemia and cardiac arrest

Chronic hyponatremia correction must not exceed 8-10 mEq/L in 24 hours to prevent osmotic demyelination syndrome (central pontine myelinolysis) — if overcorrected, administer desmopressin (DDAVP) to re-lower sodium
Tension pneumothorax is a CLINICAL diagnosis — do not delay treatment for imaging; perform needle decompression at the 2nd intercostal space, midclavicular line, followed by chest tube placement
In massive PE with hemodynamic instability, systemic thrombolysis with alteplase (100mg IV over 2 hours) is indicated even with relative contraindications when the patient is in extremis

The treatment sequence for thyroid storm is critical: beta-blocker FIRST (symptom control), then PTU (blocks synthesis AND peripheral conversion), then iodine solution ONE HOUR after PTU (blocks hormone release, but if given before PTU, provides substrate for more hormone), then corticosteroids (blocks T4-to-T3 conversion and treats relative adrenal insufficiency)

In right ventricular infarction (inferior STEMI with right-sided ST elevation in V4R), avoid nitroglycerin and diuretics as they reduce preload, worsening RV output; administer IV fluids to maintain preload

Calcium gluconate in hyperkalemia stabilizes the cardiac membrane but does NOT lower potassium — always follow with potassium-shifting agents (insulin/dextrose, albuterol) and elimination therapies (kayexalate, dialysis)

rn-testbank-cardiovascular.title: RN Cardiovascular Test Bank
rn-testbank-cardiovascular.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Cardiovascular Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of premature CAD (men < 55, women < 65)
clinicalPearls: Door-to-balloon time for STEMI should be < 90 minutes; every minute of delay increases myocardial necrosis
Right ventricular infarction (inferior MI with ST elevation in V4R) contraindicates nitroglycerin and diuretics—these patients are preload-dependent
Troponin begins rising 3-6 hours post-MI; a negative result at presentation does not rule out MI—repeat at 3-6 hours
New-onset atrial fibrillation requires rate control first, then rhythm control consideration; anticoagulate if CHA2DS2-VASc ≥ 2
Hypomagnesemia and hypokalemia together predispose to torsades de pointes—always check and replace both simultaneously
Cardiac tamponade requires emergent pericardiocentesis; do NOT administer diuretics as these patients need volume
Post-CABG patients: monitor for mediastinal shift, graft occlusion (ECG changes), and sternal wound infection
Sacubitril/valsartan (Entresto) requires 36-hour washout from ACE inhibitors to prevent life-threatening angioedema

rn-testbank-respiratory.title: RN Respiratory Test Bank
rn-testbank-respiratory.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Respiratory Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ARDS Berlin criteria: mild PaO2/FiO2 200-300, moderate 100-200, severe < 100 on PEEP ≥ 5 cmH2O
Lung-protective ventilation: tidal volume 6-8 mL/kg ideal body weight, plateau pressure < 30 cmH2O to prevent VILI
Post-pneumonectomy: NEVER position on the non-operative side—this causes mediastinal shift compressing the remaining lung
Chest tube drainage > 100-200 mL/hr is excessive and suggests hemorrhage—report immediately and prepare for surgical intervention
RSBI (RR/TV) > 105 predicts weaning failure; resume ventilatory support and address contributing factors
For PE: Wells score > 6 = high probability → proceed directly to CTPA; skip D-dimer testing
Tension pneumothorax is a clinical diagnosis requiring emergent needle decompression at the 2nd intercostal space, midclavicular line—do not wait for imaging
ABG interpretation: pH determines acidosis vs alkalosis; PaCO2 is the respiratory component; HCO3 is the metabolic component

rn-testbank-neuro.title: RN Neurological Test Bank
rn-testbank-neuro.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Neurological Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Hypercoagulable states (Factor V Leiden, antiphospholipid syndrome)
clinicalPearls: Time is brain: each minute of untreated large-vessel stroke destroys ~1.9 million neurons; door-to-needle time for tPA should be < 60 minutes
CPP = MAP - ICP; maintain CPP 60-70 mmHg. Interventions to raise MAP or lower ICP are equally valid approaches
Cushing triad (hypertension, bradycardia, abnormal respirations) is a LATE sign of increased ICP indicating brainstem herniation—do not wait for this to intervene
Autonomic dysreflexia in SCI above T6: sit patient upright immediately, identify and remove trigger (full bladder is most common), treat hypertension if BP remains dangerously elevated
Vital capacity < 20 mL/kg in Guillain-Barré syndrome indicates impending respiratory failure—prepare for elective intubation rather than emergent
Status epilepticus protocol: benzodiazepine first (lorazepam IV) → second-line agent (fosphenytoin, valproic acid, levetiracetam) → refractory treatment (propofol, midazolam drip)
After tPA administration: no anticoagulants, antiplatelets, or invasive procedures for 24 hours. Perform neuro checks every 15 minutes x 2 hours, then every 30 minutes x 6 hours
Diabetes insipidus post-pituitary surgery: massive dilute urine (> 200 mL/hr), specific gravity < 1.005, rising serum sodium and osmolality. Treat with desmopressin (DDAVP).

rn-testbank-critical-care.title: RN Critical Care Test Bank
rn-testbank-critical-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Critical Care Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic organ dysfunction (heart failure, COPD, CKD, cirrhosis)
clinicalPearls: Surviving Sepsis Campaign hour-1 bundle: measure lactate, blood cultures, broad-spectrum antibiotics, 30 mL/kg crystalloid for hypotension or lactate ≥ 4, and vasopressors for MAP < 65 after fluids
Lactate clearance > 10% in 2-6 hours is a positive prognostic sign; persistently elevated lactate indicates ongoing tissue hypoperfusion
DIC treatment: treat the underlying cause first. Replace consumed factors with cryoprecipitate (fibrinogen < 100), FFP (prolonged PT/INR), and platelets (< 20,000 or < 50,000 with active bleeding)
Propofol infusion syndrome (PRIS) is fatal if not recognized early: unexplained metabolic acidosis + rhabdomyolysis + hyperkalemia + hyperlipidemia → discontinue propofol immediately
Adequate urine output in adults is ≥ 0.5 mL/kg/hr. In a 70 kg patient, minimum acceptable output is 35 mL/hr
Ranson criteria for acute pancreatitis: score ≥ 3 indicates severe disease; score ≥ 6 predicts > 50% mortality
Compartment syndrome: pain out of proportion to injury + pain with passive stretch is the earliest sign. Absent pulses is a LATE finding—fasciotomy needed before pulse loss
CRRT filter clotting: adjust anticoagulation protocol (regional citrate is preferred over heparin in most centers). Monitor ionized calcium with citrate anticoagulation.

rn-testbank-pharmacology.title: RN Pharmacology Test Bank
rn-testbank-pharmacology.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Pharmacology Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Extremes of age (neonates and elderly have altered pharmacokinetics)
Low body weight or malnutrition (altered volume of distribution)
Pregnancy and lactation (altered physiology, fetal considerations)
History of adverse drug reactions or allergies
clinicalPearls: Narrow therapeutic index drugs requiring level monitoring: digoxin (0.5-2.0), lithium (0.6-1.2), phenytoin (10-20), theophylline (10-20), vancomycin (trough 15-20 for serious infections), aminoglycosides (trough < 2)
Red Man Syndrome from vancomycin is a RATE-RELATED histamine reaction, NOT a true allergy. Slow the infusion; do not document as allergy
Amiodarone requires monitoring of lungs (PFTs), thyroid (TFTs), liver (LFTs), and eyes (ophthalmologic exam)—mnemonic: 'Amio affects the BODY from lungs to eyes'
Never give nitroglycerin to a patient who has taken a PDE5 inhibitor (sildenafil/tadalafil) within 24-48 hours—risk of fatal hypotension
When reversing opioid toxicity with naloxone, titrate to adequate respirations (RR > 12), not to complete pain reversal—prevents acute withdrawal and maintains analgesia
ACE inhibitors must be stopped 36 hours before starting sacubitril/valsartan to prevent angioedema. ACE inhibitor cough is caused by bradykinin accumulation—switch to ARB if intolerable
Phenytoin (Dilantin) toxicity progression: nystagmus at 20 mcg/mL → ataxia at 30 mcg/mL → lethargy/confusion at 40 mcg/mL → seizures paradoxically at > 50 mcg/mL
Carbamazepine requires baseline CBC due to risk of aplastic anemia and agranulocytosis. Teach patients to report fever, sore throat, easy bruising, and oral ulcers.

rn-testbank-maternal-child.title: RN Maternal-Child Test Bank
rn-testbank-maternal-child.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de RN Maternal-Child Test Bank, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of preeclampsia in previous pregnancies
Substance use during pregnancy (alcohol, tobacco, illicit drugs)
clinicalPearls: Magnesium sulfate toxicity progression: loss of DTRs at 7-10 mEq/L → respiratory depression at 10-12 mEq/L → cardiac arrest at > 15 mEq/L. Antidote is calcium gluconate 1 g IV push

Cord prolapse management: elevate the presenting part (gloved hand pushing upward), place patient in Trendelenburg or knee-chest position, administer oxygen, keep cord moist with warm saline, prepare for emergent cesarean

Late decelerations indicate uteroplacental insufficiency—nadir occurs AFTER contraction peak. Interventions: left lateral positioning, IV fluid bolus, stop oxytocin, administer oxygen, notify provider
Congenital diaphragmatic hernia triad: scaphoid abdomen, respiratory distress, bowel sounds in chest. Do NOT bag-mask ventilate (forces air into stomach, worsening thoracic compression)
Postpartum hemorrhage: uterine atony is the #1 cause (4 T's: Tone, Trauma, Tissue, Thrombin). Fundal massage is the first intervention; administer uterotonics as ordered
APGAR scoring at 1 and 5 minutes: Appearance (color), Pulse, Grimace (reflex), Activity (muscle tone), Respirations. Score 7-10 is normal; < 7 requires intervention
Gestational diabetes screening: 1-hour glucose challenge test (50 g) at 24-28 weeks; if ≥ 140 mg/dL, proceed to 3-hour glucose tolerance test (100 g)
Preeclampsia with severe features: BP ≥ 160/110, platelets < 100,000, liver enzymes 2x normal, creatinine > 1.1, pulmonary edema, cerebral/visual symptoms. Delivery is the definitive treatment.

nursing-process-adpie.title: Nursing Process (ADPIE) and Clinical Reasoning
nursing-process-adpie.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Nursing Process (ADPIE) and Clinical Reasoning, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incomplete or rushed assessment leading to missed findings
Cognitive biases (anchoring, confirmation bias) in clinical reasoning
Interruptions during medication administration or critical tasks
Failure to prioritize problems using clinical frameworks
Lack of integration between subjective and objective data
clinicalPearls: Assessment is the most critical step - everything else depends on accurate data collection
Never skip reassessment after an intervention - this closes the nursing process loop
Prioritize nursing diagnoses using ABCs and Maslow's hierarchy: physiological needs first
Clinical reasoning improves with deliberate practice and reflective thinking

vital-signs-red-flags.title: Vital Signs: Red Flags & Rapid Response Triggers
vital-signs-red-flags.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Vital Signs: Red Flags & Rapid Response Triggers, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to trend vital signs over time (looking at single readings in isolation)
Not reassessing vital signs after interventions or status changes
Using incorrect measurement techniques (wrong cuff size, incorrect positioning)
Ignoring subtle changes from patient's individual baseline
Delayed recognition of compensatory mechanisms masking true severity
Failure to correlate vital signs with clinical presentation
Not recognizing that 'normal' vital signs can be abnormal for certain patients
Alarm fatigue leading to delayed response to monitoring alerts
clinicalPearls: Tachypnea (RR > 24) is the single most predictive vital sign for clinical deterioration
A 'normal' blood pressure does not mean the patient is stable - tachycardia may be compensating
Always compare current vital signs to the patient's BASELINE, not just textbook normals
The combination of tachycardia + tachypnea + altered mental status = emergency until proven otherwise

medication-administration-safety.title: Medication Administration: Rights & Safety Checks
medication-administration-safety.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Medication Administration: Rights & Safety Checks, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Interruptions during medication preparation and administration
Failure to verify patient identity using two identifiers
Calculation errors with weight-based or concentration-dependent dosing
Bypassing barcode scanning or other safety technologies
Inadequate knowledge of medication actions, interactions, and contraindications
Transcription errors from verbal or telephone orders
Failure to assess allergies before administration
clinicalPearls: The three checks: verify medication label when pulling from storage, when preparing, and at the bedside before administration
If a patient says 'That doesn't look like my usual pill,' STOP and verify - patients know their medications
Never crush enteric-coated or extended-release tablets - it destroys the protective coating and can cause toxicity
Insulin pens are single-patient use devices - NEVER share between patients even with a new needle

infection-prevention-ppe.title: Infection Prevention & PPE Standards
infection-prevention-ppe.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infection Prevention & PPE Standards, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inadequate hand hygiene (the single most important infection prevention measure)
Improper donning or doffing of PPE leading to self-contamination
Failure to identify patients requiring isolation precautions
Reuse of single-use equipment or failure to properly disinfect reusable equipment
Indwelling devices (urinary catheters, central lines, endotracheal tubes)
Immunocompromised patients (chemotherapy, HIV, organ transplant recipients)
Antibiotic overuse leading to resistant organism development
Breaks in sterile technique during invasive procedures
clinicalPearls: Hand hygiene is the single most effective measure to prevent healthcare-associated infections
For C. difficile: use SOAP AND WATER only - alcohol-based sanitizers do not kill bacterial spores
Airborne precautions require a fit-tested N95 respirator, not a surgical mask
PPE doffing is the highest risk moment for self-contamination - follow the sequence carefully

documentation-sbar-dar.title: Clinical Documentation: SBAR, DAR & Legal Safety
documentation-sbar-dar.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Clinical Documentation: SBAR, DAR & Legal Safety, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Late or incomplete documentation creating gaps in the medical record
Using subjective or judgmental language (e.g., 'patient was noncompliant')
Documenting care that was not actually provided
Altering or adding to records after-the-fact without proper late entry notation
Using prohibited abbreviations that can be misinterpreted
Failure to document patient education and understanding
Incomplete handoff communication leading to information gaps
Documenting assessments without documenting follow-up actions taken
clinicalPearls: If it wasn't documented, it wasn't done - legally, undocumented care is equivalent to no care
Always document the time you notified a physician, the physician's name, what was communicated, and orders received
Never use 'patient refused' without documenting education provided, risks explained, and patient's understanding
SBAR communication reduces errors by 30% during handoffs - use it consistently for every provider notification

fluid-balance-assessment.title: Fluid Balance: I&O, Dehydration & Overload
fluid-balance-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Fluid Balance: I&O, Dehydration & Overload, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Elderly patients with decreased thirst mechanism and renal concentrating ability
Patients on diuretics, especially loop diuretics (furosemide)
Heart failure patients with impaired cardiac output and renal perfusion
Post-surgical patients with third-spacing and insensible losses
Patients with vomiting, diarrhea, nasogastric suction, or wound drainage
Burns patients with massive fluid shifts and evaporative losses
Patients receiving IV fluids without adequate intake/output monitoring
Chronic kidney disease patients with impaired fluid regulation
clinicalPearls: Daily weights are more accurate than I&O for assessing fluid status - 1 kg weight change = approximately 1 liter of fluid
Third-spacing creates a paradox: the patient is edematous but intravascularly depleted and may need IV fluids despite appearing 'overloaded'
Elderly patients may not exhibit typical signs of dehydration (poor skin turgor is less reliable due to decreased skin elasticity)
Frothy pink sputum is a late sign of pulmonary edema requiring emergency intervention (oxygen, diuretics, positioning)

gi-bleed.title: Hemorragia gastrointestinal
gi-bleed.overview: Hemorragia gastrointestinal é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NPO is mandatory for suspected obstruction
NG tube for decompression
Monitor for signs of peritonitis

acute-abdomen.title: Abdomen aigu
acute-abdomen.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Abdomen aigu, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NPO immediately for surgery
No heat pads for Appendicitis (risk of rupture)
Semi-Fowler's position aids drainage if perforated

ibs-basics.title: Syndrome de l'intestin irritable
ibs-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome de l'intestin irritable, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of physical or sexual abuse
clinicalPearls: Food diary to identify triggers
Stress management is key component
FODMAP diet may help reduce symptoms
IBS does NOT cause weight loss or bleeding - these are red flags for other diagnoses

peptic-ulcer.title: Peptic Ulcer Disease
peptic-ulcer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Peptic Ulcer Disease, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: EGD (endoscopy) for definitive diagnosis and biopsy
Discontinue NSAIDs immediately
H. pylori testing: urea breath test or stool antigen
Coffee-ground emesis = upper GI bleeding (report immediately)
NPO if perforation suspected - prepare for surgical consultation

ulcerative-colitis.title: Colite ulcéreuse
ulcerative-colitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Colite ulcéreuse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of inflammatory bowel disease
Age 15-30 years (peak onset) or 50-70 years (second peak)
Non-smoking status (paradoxically, smoking appears protective in UC)
Psychological stress (may trigger flares, not a cause)
clinicalPearls: UC involves continuous inflammation starting at the rectum; skip lesions suggest Crohn's disease
Toxic megacolon is a surgical emergency: stop anticholinergics and opioids, which can precipitate dilation
Colorectal cancer risk increases after 8-10 years of disease; surveillance colonoscopy is essential
pANCA is associated with UC; ASCA is associated with Crohn's disease

cholecystectomy.title: Cholécystectomie
cholecystectomy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cholécystectomie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex (4 F's: Fat, Forty, Fertile, Female)
clinicalPearls: Low-fat diet initially as body adjusts to continuous bile flow
Report clay-colored stools immediately (indicates bile duct obstruction)
T-tube care: keep below gallbladder level, measure and document drainage
Clamp T-tube before meals as ordered to aid fat digestion

cholecystectomy-rpn.title: Cholécystectomie – RPN
cholecystectomy-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cholécystectomie – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Rapid weight loss or crash dieting
clinicalPearls: Monitor T-tube: keep below gallbladder level, document color and amount of drainage every shift
Report clay-colored stools immediately  -  indicates bile is not reaching the intestine
Early ambulation is key: prevents DVT, promotes bowel function, and reduces shoulder pain from CO2
Low-fat diet initially  -  body needs time to adjust to continuous bile flow without gallbladder storage

cholecystectomy-np.title: Cholécystectomie – IPA
cholecystectomy-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cholécystectomie – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Acute cholecystitis with severe inflammation (difficult cholecystectomy)
Anatomic variants of biliary tree (short cystic duct, aberrant right hepatic duct)
Mirizzi syndrome (gallstone impacted in cystic duct compressing CBD)
Cholangitis (Charcot triad: fever, jaundice, RUQ pain)
clinicalPearls: Strasberg classification is essential for communicating bile duct injury severity  -  Type A (leak) is most common and most treatable; Type E (main duct transection) requires surgical reconstruction
Postcholecystectomy diarrhea is often bile acid malabsorption  -  trial of cholestyramine before meals is both diagnostic and therapeutic
ERCP carries 5-10% risk of pancreatitis  -  use MRCP first for diagnosis, reserve ERCP for therapeutic intervention
Critical view of safety (CVS) during laparoscopic cholecystectomy is the most important step to prevent bile duct injury  -  never clip or cut structures until CVS is achieved

ercp-egd.title: CPRE et EGD
ercp-egd.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de CPRE et EGD, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of GERD or dysphagia (for EGD)
clinicalPearls: NPO for 6-8 hours before procedure
Check gag reflex BEFORE allowing oral intake post-EGD
Monitor for pancreatitis after ERCP (check amylase/lipase)
ERCP carries 5-10% risk of post-procedure pancreatitis

dumping-syndrome.title: Syndrome de chasse
dumping-syndrome.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndrome de chasse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Small frequent meals (6 per day) instead of 3 large meals
Avoid simple sugars and fluids WITH meals (increases osmotic load)
Lie down for 30 minutes after eating to slow gastric transit
High protein, high fat, LOW carbohydrate diet
Separate liquids from solids by at least 30 minutes

bariatric-surgery.title: Bariatric Surgery
bariatric-surgery.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bariatric Surgery, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: BMI ≥ 40 or BMI ≥ 35 with obesity-related comorbidities
Failed conservative weight loss measures (diet, exercise, pharmacotherapy)
Type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, or hypertension related to obesity
History of binge eating disorder or uncontrolled psychiatric illness
Age extremes (adolescents and older adults carry higher surgical risk)
Prior abdominal surgeries increasing adhesion risk
clinicalPearls: Tachycardia is the earliest sign of anastomotic leak; report heart rate > 120 immediately
Dumping syndrome is managed through diet modification, not medication; teach clients to avoid concentrated sweets
Clients must separate liquids from solids by at least 30 minutes to prevent dumping
Weight regain is common 2-5 years postoperatively; ongoing behavioral support is essential
RYGB alters drug absorption; report to provider when new medications are ordered

cholecystitis.title: Cholecystitis
cholecystitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cholecystitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex (estrogen increases cholesterol secretion into bile)
Obesity and rapid weight loss (increases bile cholesterol saturation)
Oral contraceptive or hormone replacement therapy use
Diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, and metabolic syndrome
clinicalPearls: Avoid morphine in cholecystitis; it may cause sphincter of Oddi spasm and worsen pain
Murphy's sign differentiates cholecystitis from other RUQ pathology
Post-cholecystectomy clients may experience transient diarrhea due to continuous bile flow into the duodenum
Charcot's triad (fever, jaundice, RUQ pain) indicates ascending cholangitis; a medical emergency

cirrhosis.title: Cirrhosis
cirrhosis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cirrhosis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic alcohol use disorder (most common cause in Western countries)
Chronic hepatitis B or C infection
Primary biliary cholangitis or primary sclerosing cholangitis
Hemochromatosis (iron overload) or Wilson's disease (copper overload)
Prolonged exposure to hepatotoxic drugs (methotrexate, isoniazid, amiodarone)
clinicalPearls: AST:ALT ratio > 2:1 suggests alcoholic liver disease
Spironolactone and furosemide are given in a 100:40 mg ratio for ascites management
Asterixis (liver flap) is a hallmark finding of hepatic encephalopathy
Restrict dietary protein only during acute encephalopathy episodes; chronic restriction leads to muscle wasting
Thrombocytopenia in cirrhosis results from splenic sequestration, not bone marrow failure

portal-hypertension.title: Portal Hypertension
portal-hypertension.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Portal Hypertension, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cirrhosis (most common cause in developed countries)
Chronic hepatitis B or C infection
Portal vein thrombosis or Budd-Chiari syndrome
Right-sided heart failure causing hepatic congestion
clinicalPearls: HVPG ≥ 10 mmHg defines clinically significant portal hypertension; ≥ 12 mmHg increases variceal bleeding risk
Non-selective beta-blockers reduce portal pressure by 20-25% and are first-line for variceal prophylaxis
TIPS is reserved for refractory cases; it can worsen hepatic encephalopathy by shunting portal blood past the liver
All clients with cirrhosis should undergo screening endoscopy for varices at diagnosis

ascites.title: Ascites
ascites.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ascites, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cirrhosis with portal hypertension (most common cause)
clinicalPearls: SAAG ≥ 1.1 g/dL = portal hypertension-related ascites (95% accuracy)
Always perform diagnostic paracentesis on new-onset ascites and in any hospitalized client with ascites to rule out SBP
Weight loss goal: 0.5 kg/day without edema, 1 kg/day with edema; faster diuresis risks hepatorenal syndrome
Post-paracentesis albumin (6-8 g/L) is only required when > 5 L is removed

esophageal-varices.title: Esophageal Varices
esophageal-varices.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Esophageal Varices, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Large variceal size (> 5 mm) with red wale marks on endoscopy
Child-Pugh class B or C (decompensated cirrhosis)
Active alcohol use in clients with alcoholic cirrhosis
Thrombocytopenia (< 100,000/mm³) as marker of severe portal hypertension
History of prior variceal bleeding (60-70% rebleeding risk within 1 year)
clinicalPearls: Keep scissors at the bedside with balloon tamponade; if the tube migrates, cut and remove immediately to prevent airway obstruction
Target hemoglobin 7-8 g/dL during resuscitation; over-transfusion increases portal pressure and rebleeding risk
Blood in the GI tract is a protein load that increases ammonia production; monitor for hepatic encephalopathy
All clients surviving variceal bleeding should be started on secondary prophylaxis (beta-blocker + banding)

hepatitis.title: Hepatitis
hepatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hepatitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HAV/HEV: travel to endemic areas, contaminated food/water, poor sanitation
HBV: unprotected sexual contact, IV drug use, perinatal exposure, healthcare workers
HCV: IV drug use (most common in US), blood transfusion before 1992, needle-stick injury
HDV: only occurs with concurrent HBV infection
Men who have sex with men (HAV, HBV)
clinicalPearls: HBsAg positive = current HBV infection; anti-HBs positive = immunity (vaccine or resolved infection)
HCV is the most common blood-borne infection in the US; screen all adults born 1945-1965
HAV and HEV do not cause chronic hepatitis; HBV, HCV, and HDV can become chronic
DAA therapy for HCV can cause HBV reactivation; always screen for HBV before starting treatment
Fulminant hepatic failure is more common with HBV (especially HBV-HDV co-infection) than HCV

pancreatitis.title: Pancreatitis
pancreatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pancreatitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic alcohol use (most common cause of chronic pancreatitis)
Medications: azathioprine, valproic acid, thiazides, estrogens
clinicalPearls: Lipase is more specific than amylase for pancreatitis; amylase can be elevated in many non-pancreatic conditions
Grey Turner sign and Cullen sign indicate hemorrhagic pancreatitis; a severe, life-threatening presentation
Hypocalcemia occurs because calcium binds to fatty acids in areas of fat necrosis (saponification)
Early enteral nutrition is preferred over TPN; maintains gut barrier integrity and reduces infectious complications
Ranson score ≥ 3 at 48 hours = severe pancreatitis with significantly increased mortality

ibs.title: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
ibs.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age < 50 years (onset typically in young adulthood)
History of anxiety, depression, or psychological trauma
Visceral hypersensitivity and altered gut motility
Dietary triggers: high-FODMAP foods, caffeine, alcohol
History of physical or sexual abuse (central sensitization)
clinicalPearls: IBS is a diagnosis of exclusion; alarm features (weight loss, rectal bleeding, nocturnal symptoms, anemia) warrant further workup
Low-FODMAP diet should be guided by a dietitian and is not meant to be a permanent restriction
Nocturnal symptoms suggest organic disease (IBD, cancer), not IBS
Fecal calprotectin is a useful non-invasive marker to differentiate IBS from inflammatory bowel disease

crohns-disease.title: Crohn's Disease
crohns-disease.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Crohn's Disease, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of IBD (first-degree relatives have 5-20× increased risk)
Smoking (doubles the risk of Crohn's and worsens disease course; opposite of UC)
Western diet high in processed foods and refined sugars
History of appendectomy (may increase risk; opposite of UC)
clinicalPearls: Smoking worsens Crohn's but paradoxically appears protective in UC; always counsel Crohn's clients on cessation
Terminal ileum involvement causes B12 and bile salt malabsorption; assess for megaloblastic anemia and steatorrhea
Non-caseating granulomas on biopsy are characteristic of Crohn's but not required for diagnosis
Crohn's is NOT cured by surgery; post-surgical recurrence at anastomotic sites is common
ASCA positive + pANCA negative favors Crohn's; pANCA positive + ASCA negative favors UC

diverticulitis.title: Diverticulitis
diverticulitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Diverticulitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age > 50 years (diverticula prevalence increases with age)
clinicalPearls: Never perform colonoscopy during acute diverticulitis; wait 6-8 weeks to rule out colorectal cancer
Left lower quadrant pain is classic; right-sided diverticulitis occurs more in Asian populations
Metronidazole + ciprofloxacin covers both aerobic and anaerobic organisms in diverticulitis
Nuts, seeds, and popcorn do NOT cause diverticulitis; this is a debunked myth

hemorrhoids.title: Hemorrhoids
hemorrhoids.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hemorrhoids, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic constipation and prolonged straining during defecation
Low-fiber diet with inadequate fluid intake
Pregnancy (increased pelvic venous pressure, hormonal changes)
Portal hypertension (causes rectal varices, not true hemorrhoids, but often coexists)
clinicalPearls: Internal hemorrhoids are painless because they are above the dentate line (visceral innervation); external hemorrhoids are painful (somatic innervation)
Rubber band ligation must be placed ABOVE the dentate line to avoid pain; placement below causes severe pain
Urinary retention is the most common complication after hemorrhoidectomy; monitor voiding
All rectal bleeding warrants investigation for colorectal cancer, especially in clients > 45 years

antacids.title: Antacids
antacids.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Antacids, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Overuse or chronic self-medication without provider guidance
Concurrent use of medications with pH-dependent absorption
Renal insufficiency (magnesium and aluminum accumulation risk)
Hypercalcemia or history of kidney stones (calcium carbonate)
Elderly clients (increased risk of constipation with aluminum-based antacids)
clinicalPearls: Aluminum = constipation; Magnesium = diarrhea; combine them to balance GI effects
Separate antacids from all other oral medications by at least 1-2 hours to prevent drug interactions
Aluminum hydroxide binds phosphorus; used therapeutically in CKD hyperphosphatemia
Calcium carbonate causes acid rebound; not recommended for long-term use in GERD

h2-receptor-antagonists.title: H2-Receptor Antagonists
h2-receptor-antagonists.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de H2-Receptor Antagonists, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cimetidine use with concurrent medications metabolized by CYP450 (warfarin, phenytoin, theophylline)
Renal impairment requiring dose adjustment (all H2RAs are renally cleared)
Elderly clients (increased risk of CNS effects: confusion, dizziness)
clinicalPearls: Famotidine is preferred over cimetidine due to fewer drug interactions and no anti-androgenic effects
H2RAs are less potent than PPIs because they block only one of three acid-stimulating pathways
Tolerance develops with chronic H2RA use; this does not occur with PPIs
Ranitidine (Zantac) was withdrawn from many markets due to NDMA carcinogen contamination concerns

proton-pump-inhibitors.title: Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs)
proton-pump-inhibitors.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Proton Pump Inhibitors (PPIs), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Concurrent clopidogrel use; omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19-mediated activation of clopidogrel
Renal impairment (increased risk of interstitial nephritis)
clinicalPearls: PPIs must be taken 30-60 minutes before meals; the pump must be actively secreting acid for the drug to work
Omeprazole reduces clopidogrel effectiveness; use pantoprazole instead in cardiac clients
Taper PPIs gradually after long-term use to prevent rebound acid hypersecretion
PPIs are more potent than H2RAs because they block the final common pathway of all acid secretion

antiemetics.title: Antiemetics
antiemetics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Antiemetics, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: QT prolongation risk with ondansetron (especially at high IV doses)
Extrapyramidal symptoms with metoclopramide (especially in young women and elderly)
Tissue necrosis with promethazine IV extravasation
Excessive sedation with promethazine and first-generation antihistamines
Concurrent use of CNS depressants enhances sedation risk
clinicalPearls: Ondansetron does not cause sedation or EPS; it is the safest first-line antiemetic for most indications
Promethazine IV extravasation can cause tissue necrosis; always verify IV patency; IM route preferred
Metoclopramide is both an antiemetic AND a prokinetic; useful in gastroparesis but limit to < 12 weeks
Treat acute EPS from metoclopramide or promethazine with IV diphenhydramine (Benadryl)

laxatives.title: Laxatives
laxatives.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Laxatives, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic stimulant laxative use leading to cathartic colon and dependence
Inadequate fluid intake with bulk-forming laxatives (impaction/obstruction risk)
Electrolyte imbalances: hypokalemia (stimulant laxatives), hypermagnesemia (Mg-containing osmotic laxatives)
Bowel obstruction or undiagnosed abdominal pain (all laxatives contraindicated)
Elderly clients at higher risk of dehydration and electrolyte disturbances
clinicalPearls: Bulk-forming laxatives MUST be taken with adequate fluid; can cause esophageal or intestinal obstruction if taken dry
Stool softeners are preventive agents; they are not effective for treating existing constipation
Stimulant laxatives should not be used long-term; can lead to cathartic colon and laxative dependence
Never give laxatives to clients with undiagnosed abdominal pain; may mask surgical abdomen

antidiarrheals.title: Antidiarrheals
antidiarrheals.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Antidiarrheals, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Loperamide misuse/overdose (may cause QT prolongation, cardiac arrhythmias, and death at supratherapeutic doses)
Use in infectious diarrhea with fever/bloody stools (risk of toxic megacolon)
Salicylate sensitivity or aspirin allergy (bismuth subsalicylate)
Concurrent anticoagulant therapy (bismuth subsalicylate has salicylate effects)
Children under 2 years (loperamide) or children/teens with viral illness (bismuth; Reye syndrome risk)
Renal impairment (salicylate accumulation with bismuth subsalicylate)
clinicalPearls: Never give loperamide for bloody diarrhea or suspected C. difficile; slowing motility traps toxins and worsens disease
Bismuth subsalicylate = salicylate; avoid in aspirin allergy, children with viral illness, and concurrent anticoagulation
Rehydration is always the priority in diarrheal illness; antidiarrheals address symptoms, not the cause
Loperamide overdose is a growing concern; high doses cause fatal cardiac arrhythmias (QT prolongation)

hepatic-encephalopathy-meds.title: Hepatic Encephalopathy Medications
hepatic-encephalopathy-meds.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hepatic Encephalopathy Medications, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cirrhosis with portosystemic shunting (most common setting)
GI bleeding (blood protein digestion increases ammonia production)
Constipation (increased ammonia absorption from prolonged colonic contact)
Hypokalemia and metabolic alkalosis (favor NH₃ form that crosses blood-brain barrier)
High dietary protein intake (excessive amino acid deamination)
Sedatives, opioids, and benzodiazepines (decrease CNS function, worsen HE)
clinicalPearls: Lactulose titration goal: 2-3 soft stools/day; excessive diarrhea causes dehydration and hypokalemia, which worsen HE
Alkalosis promotes HE because NH₃ (crosses blood-brain barrier) predominates over NH₄⁺ in alkaline pH
GI bleeding is a major HE precipitant; blood in the GI tract is a massive protein/ammonia load
Serum ammonia levels correlate poorly with clinical severity; always treat based on clinical assessment
Do NOT chronically restrict dietary protein; this causes sarcopenia and worsens outcomes in cirrhosis

viral-hepatitis.title: Viral Hepatitis Overview
viral-hepatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Viral Hepatitis Overview, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: IV drug use (shared needles - Hep B, C)
Travel to endemic areas (Hep A, E)
clinicalPearls: Hep A: fecal-oral, acute only, VACCINE available; Hep B: bloodborne, can become chronic, VACCINE available; Hep C: bloodborne, most common chronic form, NO vaccine, NOW CURABLE with DAAs
ALT is the most specific liver enzyme - 'L for Liver'; ALT > AST suggests viral hepatitis; AST > ALT (2:1 ratio) suggests alcoholic hepatitis
Infants born to HBsAg-positive mothers must receive BOTH hepatitis B vaccine AND hepatitis B immune globulin (HBIG) within 12 hours of birth

hepatitis-c.title: Hepatitis C
hepatitis-c.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hepatitis C, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: IV drug use (most common route in North America)
Birth to HCV-infected mother (5% vertical transmission)
clinicalPearls: Hepatitis C is now CURABLE - DAAs achieve >95% sustained virologic response (SVR); cure is defined as undetectable HCV RNA 12 weeks after treatment completion
Anti-HCV antibody positive does NOT distinguish active from resolved infection - must confirm with HCV RNA viral load; antibody remains positive for life even after cure
There is NO vaccine for hepatitis C due to the virus's rapid mutation rate - prevention relies entirely on avoiding blood-to-blood contact

chronic-hepatitis.title: Chronic Hepatitis
chronic-hepatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Chronic Hepatitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic hepatitis B infection (5-10% of adults, 90% of infected neonates)
Chronic hepatitis C infection (75-85% of acute infections)
Autoimmune hepatitis (female predominance, associated with other autoimmune diseases)
clinicalPearls: Chronic hepatitis is often silent for years - 'the liver suffers in silence'; many patients are diagnosed incidentally through abnormal liver enzymes
NEVER abruptly stop HBV antivirals - withdrawal can trigger a severe hepatitis flare due to immune reconstitution that can lead to liver failure
All patients with chronic hepatitis B or C AND cirrhosis need HCC screening with ultrasound and alpha-fetoprotein every 6 months for life

biliary-atresia-rpn.title: Biliary Atresia (RPN Basics)
biliary-atresia-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Biliary Atresia (RPN Basics), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Newborn with jaundice persisting beyond 2 weeks of life
Premature birth or low birth weight
Associated congenital anomalies (polysplenia, situs inversus, cardiac defects)
clinicalPearls: Any jaundice persisting beyond 2 weeks of life requires a fractionated bilirubin to rule out biliary atresia
Clay-colored stools + dark urine + persistent jaundice = suspect biliary atresia until proven otherwise
The Kasai procedure is most successful when performed before 60 days of life; early recognition is critical
Biliary atresia is the #1 indication for pediatric liver transplantation

biliary-atresia.title: Biliary Atresia
biliary-atresia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Biliary Atresia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Newborn with persistent conjugated hyperbilirubinemia beyond 2 weeks of life
Female sex (slightly higher incidence in perinatal form)
Possible viral triggers: reovirus, rotavirus, CMV exposure in perinatal period
Associated congenital anomalies (polysplenia, situs inversus, cardiac defects) in embryonic form
No clear genetic inheritance pattern in most cases
clinicalPearls: Conjugated (direct) bilirubin > 1 mg/dL in a newborn is NEVER normal and demands immediate investigation
The 'golden window' for Kasai surgery is before 60 days of life; every day of delay worsens outcomes
GGT is the most sensitive liver enzyme for biliary atresia and is markedly elevated compared to other neonatal cholestatic conditions
Even successful Kasai is not curative; 70-80% of patients eventually need liver transplantation
Cholangitis post-Kasai presents as fever + worsening jaundice + acholic stools; requires immediate IV antibiotics

biliary-atresia-np.title: Biliary Atresia (Advanced NP)
biliary-atresia-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Biliary Atresia (Advanced NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Perinatal viral exposure (reovirus, rotavirus, CMV) triggering immune-mediated bile duct destruction
Dysregulated Th1/Th17 immune response with Treg insufficiency
Biliary atresia splenic malformation (BASM) syndrome with laterality defects (embryonic form)
Possible genetic susceptibility loci identified on chromosomes 2q37.3 and 10q24.2 (GPC1 and ADD3 genes)
Aberrant Notch and Hedgehog signaling during biliary morphogenesis (embryonic form)
No clear Mendelian inheritance pattern; likely multifactorial etiology
clinicalPearls: The two-hit hypothesis (viral trigger → autoimmune bile duct destruction) explains why biliary atresia presents postnatally even though bile ducts form by week 12 of gestation
GGT is the single most useful biochemical marker: markedly elevated in biliary atresia, normal or mildly elevated in most other neonatal cholestatic conditions
Post-Kasai bilirubin clearance to < 2 mg/dL within 3 months is the strongest predictor of native liver survival
Liver transplant for biliary atresia has > 90% five-year patient survival; living-donor left lateral segmentectomy is preferred for size matching
PTLD risk is highest in the first year post-transplant; EBV-seronegative recipients of EBV-seropositive grafts are at highest risk

eosinophilic-esophagitis-rpn.title: Eosinophilic Esophagitis (RPN Basics)
eosinophilic-esophagitis-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Eosinophilic Esophagitis (RPN Basics), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
