riskFactors: History of atopic conditions: asthma, eczema, allergic rhinitis, food allergies
Family history of EoE or atopic diseases
Age: can occur at any age but most commonly diagnosed in children and young adults
clinicalPearls: EoE should be suspected in any client with recurrent dysphagia, food impaction, or heartburn not responding to standard GERD treatment
Food impaction requiring emergency removal is often the event that leads to EoE diagnosis
The 'six food elimination diet' removes milk, wheat, eggs, soy, nuts, and fish/shellfish; foods are reintroduced one at a time
Topical corticosteroids for EoE are swallowed, not inhaled; this is a common point of client confusion

eosinophilic-esophagitis.title: Eosinophilic Esophagitis
eosinophilic-esophagitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Eosinophilic Esophagitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Atopic triad: concurrent asthma, eczema, or allergic rhinitis (50-80% of EoE patients)
IgE-mediated food allergies (though EoE itself is primarily non-IgE mediated)
Family history of EoE (sibling recurrence risk 40-fold higher than general population)
Early antibiotic exposure, cesarean delivery, and formula feeding (microbiome disruption hypothesis)
clinicalPearls: Diagnostic threshold is ≥ 15 eosinophils/HPF on esophageal biopsy; always biopsy both proximal and distal esophagus (at least 6 biopsies total)
Milk and wheat are the two most common trigger foods, accounting for the majority of EoE cases; a 2-food elimination diet is now the preferred initial approach
EoE is a chronic disease; stopping treatment leads to relapse in most patients within 3-6 months
Esophageal perforation risk during dilation is low (< 1%) when using the 'rule of three' (no more than 3 mm dilation per session)
Dupilumab is the first biologic therapy for EoE and represents a paradigm shift for refractory cases

eosinophilic-esophagitis-np.title: Eosinophilic Esophagitis (Advanced NP)
eosinophilic-esophagitis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Eosinophilic Esophagitis (Advanced NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Genetic susceptibility: TSLP gene polymorphisms (5q22), CAPN14 variants (2p23), eotaxin-3 (CCL26) gene variants
Epithelial barrier dysfunction: loss of desmoglein-1, filaggrin, involucrin expression
Atopic comorbidities: 50-80% of EoE patients have concurrent asthma, eczema, rhinitis, or IgE-mediated food allergy
Male sex (3:1 ratio; estrogen may be protective via downregulation of eotaxin-3)
Environmental factors: early antibiotic exposure, acid suppression in infancy, cesarean delivery (microbiome disruption)
Family history: sibling recurrence risk 40-80 fold higher than general population; 50% heritability in twin studies
clinicalPearls: Eotaxin-3 (CCL26) is the most upregulated gene in EoE tissue (53-fold increase); it is the critical eosinophil chemoattractant driven by IL-13-STAT6 signaling
EndoFLIP distensibility measurement better predicts food impaction risk than eosinophil count or endoscopic appearance alone
Anti-IL-5 therapies reduce eosinophil counts but show inconsistent symptom improvement, suggesting fibrotic remodeling is the primary symptom driver in established disease
The CAPN14-desmoglein-1 axis creates a positive feedback loop: IL-13 → calpain-14 activation → desmoglein-1 cleavage → barrier dysfunction → allergen penetration → more IL-13
Maintenance therapy is essential; EoE is a chronic disease with > 80% relapse rate within months of discontinuing treatment

meckel-diverticulum-rpn.title: Meckel's Diverticulum (RPN Basics)
meckel-diverticulum-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Meckel's Diverticulum (RPN Basics), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Congenital anomaly present from birth (failure of vitelline duct regression)
Male sex (symptomatic cases are 2-3 times more common in males)
Age under 2 years (most common age for symptomatic presentation)
Presence of ectopic gastric tissue within the diverticulum (increases bleeding risk)
clinicalPearls: The 'Rule of 2s' helps remember key features of Meckel's diverticulum: 2% of population, 2 inches long, 2 feet from ileocecal valve, presents before age 2, 2 types of ectopic tissue
Painless rectal bleeding in a child under 2 should raise suspicion for Meckel's diverticulum
The Meckel's scan (technetium-99m) detects ectopic gastric tissue in the diverticulum, which is the cause of bleeding
Most Meckel's diverticula are asymptomatic and found incidentally; only symptomatic cases require surgery

meckel-diverticulum.title: Meckel's Diverticulum
meckel-diverticulum.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Meckel's Diverticulum, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Congenital: failure of vitelline duct regression by week 7 of gestation
Male sex: symptomatic presentations are 2-3 times more common in males
Age under 2 years: peak age for hemorrhagic presentation
Presence of ectopic gastric tissue (highest risk for bleeding)
Persistent fibrous band to umbilicus (risk for volvulus and obstruction)
clinicalPearls: Meckel's diverticulum is a TRUE diverticulum (all intestinal wall layers) on the antimesenteric border, unlike colonic diverticula which are pseudodiverticula
Painless rectal bleeding in a child under 2 = Meckel's diverticulum until proven otherwise
The Meckel's scan detects ectopic GASTRIC tissue, not the diverticulum itself; a negative scan does not rule out Meckel's (diverticulum without gastric tissue will be missed)
Meckel's diverticulitis is clinically indistinguishable from appendicitis; it is often diagnosed intraoperatively when a normal appendix is found
The lifetime risk of complications from an asymptomatic Meckel's diverticulum is approximately 4-6%, decreasing with age

meckel-diverticulum-np.title: Meckel's Diverticulum (Advanced NP)
meckel-diverticulum-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Meckel's Diverticulum (Advanced NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Embryologic: failure of BMP/Wnt-mediated apoptotic regression of the vitelline duct by week 7 of gestation
Heterotopic gastric tissue: present in 50-60% of symptomatic cases; contains functional parietal cells causing peptic ulceration
Male sex: symptomatic presentations 2-3 times more frequent (possibly related to hormonal influences on acid secretion)
Persistent fibrous band or mesodiverticular band: creates axis for volvulus and internal herniation
Broad-based diverticulum (> 2 cm): higher complication rate than narrow-necked diverticula
clinicalPearls: Meckel's diverticulum is a TRUE diverticulum (all wall layers) because it arises from the embryonic vitelline duct, unlike acquired pseudodiverticula that herniate through muscular defects
The 'Rule of 2s' is a mnemonic, not a clinical rule: approximately 50% of symptomatic cases present after age 2, and ectopic gastric tissue prevalence varies by study
A negative Meckel's scan does NOT rule out Meckel's diverticulum; it only rules out ectopic gastric tissue; diverticula containing only pancreatic tissue or normal ileal mucosa will be missed
Carcinoid tumors in Meckel's diverticulum are typically benign (< 2 cm) with low metastatic potential; however, resection specimens should always undergo histopathological examination
The incidental Meckel's resection debate has shifted toward resection in young patients since laparoscopic surgery has reduced operative morbidity significantly

ulcerative-colitis-rpn.title: Ulcerative Colitis - RPN Foundation
ulcerative-colitis-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ulcerative Colitis - RPN Foundation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of inflammatory bowel disease
Smoking cessation (paradoxically increases risk - smoking is protective in UC)
Stress and emotional distress worsen symptoms
clinicalPearls: Low-fiber diet during active flares, gradually increase fiber during remission
Total colectomy CURES UC (unlike Crohn's disease which has no surgical cure)
AVOID opioids and anticholinergics during flares - they decrease bowel motility and increase toxic megacolon risk
Colonoscopy surveillance for colorectal cancer begins 8 years after diagnosis
UC vs Crohn's: UC is continuous, mucosal, colon only; Crohn's is skip lesions, transmural, anywhere mouth to anus

ulcerative-colitis-np.title: Ulcerative Colitis - NP Advanced Management
ulcerative-colitis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ulcerative Colitis - NP Advanced Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HLA-DRB1*0103 and other MHC class II associations
IL-23R, IL-10, and CARD9 gene polymorphisms
NSAID and antibiotic use disrupting microbiome
Vitamin D deficiency associated with flare frequency
clinicalPearls: Fecal calprotectin <150 mcg/g correlates with mucosal healing - use as non-invasive monitoring tool between colonoscopies
CMV superinfection occurs in 20-30% of steroid-refractory UC - always test before escalating immunosuppression
TPMT/NUDT15 genotyping is mandatory before thiopurine initiation - homozygous poor metabolizers (1 in 300) develop life-threatening myelosuppression
Dysplasia surveillance: chromoendoscopy with targeted biopsies has replaced random 4-quadrant biopsies per updated guidelines
UC patients have 2-3x increased VTE risk during flares - consider thromboprophylaxis for hospitalized patients

sucralfate-rpn.title: Sucralfate - RPN Medication Guide
sucralfate-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sucralfate - RPN Medication Guide, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Active duodenal ulcer requiring mucosal protection
Stress ulcer prophylaxis in critically ill patients
clinicalPearls: Sucralfate works LOCALLY - it does not reduce acid production like PPIs or H2 blockers
The #1 nursing consideration: give on EMPTY stomach, 1 hour before meals, separate from other meds by 2 hours
Constipation is the most common side effect - encourage fluids and fiber intake
Monitor renal patients for aluminum toxicity: confusion, muscle weakness, bone pain
Do not give with enteral tube feeds - can cause bezoar formation. Flush tube well before and after administration

sucralfate.title: Sucralfate - RN Clinical Pharmacology
sucralfate.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sucralfate - RN Clinical Pharmacology, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Active duodenal or gastric ulcer requiring mucosal protection
Stress ulcer prophylaxis in ICU patients (ventilated, coagulopathic, head injury)
Renal impairment - GFR below 30 (aluminum accumulation risk)
Polypharmacy - multiple medications affected by binding interactions
Enteral feeding (bezoar risk with concurrent tube feeds)
Dysphagia or esophageal stricture (large tablet size)
clinicalPearls: Sucralfate requires pH below 4 to activate - giving it with PPIs simultaneously reduces its effectiveness because PPIs raise gastric pH
The 2-hour medication separation rule is critical: create a visual medication schedule for patients on multiple drugs

Stress ulcer prophylaxis: sucralfate has lower pneumonia risk than PPIs (does not raise gastric pH, so less bacterial colonization) but PPIs are more effective at preventing clinically significant bleeding
Warfarin interaction is clinically significant - monitor INR closely and separate dosing. May need warfarin dose adjustment.
Aluminum toxicity triad in renal failure: dialysis encephalopathy (myoclonus, seizures), osteomalacia (bone pain, fractures), and microcytic anemia (aluminum inhibits heme synthesis)

sucralfate-np.title: Sucralfate - NP Prescriptive Practice
sucralfate-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sucralfate - NP Prescriptive Practice, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Stress ulcer prophylaxis when VAP risk outweighs bleeding risk
CKD stage 4-5 (absolute contraindication for chronic use)
Extensive polypharmacy requiring careful interaction management
Barrett's esophagus with bile acid component
clinicalPearls: Sucralfate vs PPI for stress ulcer prophylaxis: sucralfate preserves gastric acidity (lower VAP risk) but PPIs prevent more clinically significant GI bleeds - individualize based on patient risk profile

The fluoroquinolone interaction is clinically catastrophic: 60-70% absorption reduction can lead to treatment failure for serious infections - NEVER co-administer, and 2-hour separation may be insufficient for some patients

Aluminum toxicity in dialysis patients: historical 'dialysis dementia' from aluminum-containing phosphate binders. Modern practice avoids aluminum-based agents in ESRD - use sevelamer or lanthanum instead
Sucralfate slurry (crushed tablet in water) can be applied topically for radiation-induced oral mucositis - an important off-label NP prescribing application
For patients on more than 5 medications, the practical difficulty of 2-hour separations often makes PPIs the better choice despite theoretical advantages of sucralfate

all-leukemia.title: Leucémie lymphoblastique aiguë
all-leukemia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Leucémie lymphoblastique aiguë, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Neutropenic precautions: No raw food, no flowers
Soft-bristled toothbrush only
Avoid rectal thermometers/enemas

aml-leukemia.title: Leucémie myéloïde aiguë
aml-leukemia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Leucémie myéloïde aiguë, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hand hygiene is the #1 priority
Disinfect all equipment before client contact
Irradiate blood products to prevent graft-vs-host

sickle-cell.title: Doença falciforme
sickle-cell.overview: Doença falciforme é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hydration is the #1 priority to reduce viscosity
Oxygen only if hypoxic (<92%)
Avoid cold compresses - causes vasoconstriction

polycythemia.title: Polycythemia Vera
polycythemia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Polycythemia Vera, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Increase oral fluid intake to reduce blood viscosity
Avoid iron supplements (would fuel further RBC production)
Risk of transformation to myelofibrosis or AML over time
Distinguish primary (low EPO) from secondary (high EPO) polycythemia

thalassemia.title: Thalassémie
thalassemia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Thalassémie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Both parents must be carriers for major forms
clinicalPearls: Thalassemia causes MICROCYTIC anemia (low MCV) just like iron deficiency - but the key difference is that iron stores are NORMAL or HIGH in thalassemia; never give iron supplements
'Chipmunk facies' (frontal bossing, prominent cheekbones, flat nasal bridge) results from massive bone marrow expansion trying to compensate for ineffective erythropoiesis
Iron overload from chronic transfusions is the leading cause of death in thalassemia major - cardiac iron deposition causes fatal arrhythmias and heart failure; chelation compliance saves lives

iron-overload.title: Surcharge en fer
iron-overload.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Surcharge en fer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic blood transfusions (Thalassemia, Sickle Cell, MDS)
Chronic liver disease (Hepatitis C, Alcoholic cirrhosis)
clinicalPearls: Secondary iron overload is an inevitable complication of chronic transfusion therapy (each unit of RBCs adds ~200mg of iron)
Vitamin C increases iron absorption and can trigger cardiac arrhythmias in severely overloaded patients; avoid supplements
Cardiac failure is the most common cause of death in untreated iron overload
Urine turning reddish-pink during deferoxamine therapy is an expected finding (ferrioxamine excretion)

acute-blood-loss.title: Perte sanguine aiguë
acute-blood-loss.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Perte sanguine aiguë, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Initial Hgb/Hct may be NORMAL in acute hemorrhage  -  treat the patient's vitals (tachycardia/hypotension), not the initial lab value
The 'Lethal Triad' of trauma: Hypothermia + Acidosis + Coagulopathy. Keeping the patient warm is a critical nursing intervention
Isotonic crystalloids (NS/LR) are used for initial volume, but over-resuscitation causes 'dilutional coagulopathy'; switch to MTP early
Hypotension is a LATE sign of shock  -  once BP drops, the patient has lost ~30% of blood volume

macrocytic-anemias.title: Anémies macrocytaires
macrocytic-anemias.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Anémies macrocytaires, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: B12: Vegan diet (no animal products), Pernicious Anemia, Metformin use, Gastric bypass/Bariatric surgery, Crohn's disease (terminal ileum), PPI use
Folate: Alcoholism, Pregnancy (increased demand), Poor intake (no green leafy veg), Methotrexate use, Malabsorption (Celiac disease)
clinicalPearls: NEUROLOGICAL symptoms = B12 deficiency. If you give Folate to a B12-deficient patient, the labs improve but the brain/nerves continue to rot
Pernicious anemia means NO intrinsic factor  -  these patients MUST have B12 injections for life; oral B12 will not be absorbed
Hypersegmented neutrophils (neutrophils with >5 lobes) on a blood smear are a classic early sign of megaloblastic anemia
Metformin can cause B12 malabsorption over time; monitor levels in long-term diabetic patients

blood-transfusion-rpn.title: Transfusion sanguine – RPN
blood-transfusion-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Transfusion sanguine – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Heart failure or fluid overload risk
clinicalPearls: Blood products must NEVER be infused with dextrose solutions (causes hemolysis) or Lactated Ringers (calcium causes clotting in the tubing)
The first 15 minutes of a transfusion are the most critical - most severe reactions occur during this time; stay with the patient
Each unit of PRBCs must be completed within 4 hours of removal from the blood bank to prevent bacterial contamination
Two-person verification at the bedside is the single most important safety measure to prevent ABO-incompatible transfusions
If a transfusion reaction is suspected, stop the transfusion immediately, keep the IV line open with normal saline, and notify the provider

hemolytic-transfusion-rpn.title: Réaction hémolytique transfusionnelle – RPN
hemolytic-transfusion-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Réaction hémolytique transfusionnelle – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Clerical or identification errors (most common cause)
Failure to follow two-person verification protocol
Mislabeled blood specimens sent to the blood bank
Patient unable to state name (unconscious, confused, nonverbal)
Multiple patients with similar names on the same unit
Previous alloimmunization from prior transfusions or pregnancies
clinicalPearls: AHTR can be fatal after as little as 10-15 mL of incompatible blood - this is why the first 15 minutes of every transfusion are critically important
The most common cause is human error (wrong patient, mislabeled specimen) - meticulous two-person bedside verification prevents this
Flank pain and dark urine together during a transfusion strongly suggest hemolysis - stop the transfusion immediately
Never discard the blood bag or tubing after a suspected reaction - the blood bank needs them for investigation
Free hemoglobin in the blood is toxic to the kidneys - aggressive IV normal saline helps flush it out and protect renal function

anaphylactic-transfusion-rpn.title: Réaction anaphylactique transfusionnelle – RPN
anaphylactic-transfusion-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Réaction anaphylactique transfusionnelle – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Known IgA deficiency with anti-IgA antibodies
History of prior anaphylactic transfusion reaction
History of severe allergic reactions to blood products
clinicalPearls: Anaphylaxis from a transfusion occurs within SECONDS to MINUTES - if respiratory distress or hypotension occurs immediately after starting blood, think anaphylaxis
Epinephrine is the FIRST-LINE treatment for anaphylaxis - do not delay by giving antihistamines first; epinephrine saves lives
Patients with known IgA deficiency must receive washed RBCs or blood products from IgA-deficient donors
Unlike mild allergic reactions (urticaria only), anaphylaxis involves the airway and/or cardiovascular system - this distinction determines the urgency of response
After an anaphylactic transfusion reaction, the patient should be tested for IgA deficiency and anti-IgA antibodies to guide future transfusion safety

febrile-transfusion-rpn.title: Réaction fébrile transfusionnelle – RPN
febrile-transfusion-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Réaction fébrile transfusionnelle – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of previous febrile transfusion reactions
Multiple prior transfusions (alloimmunization to HLA antigens)
Multiparous women (exposure to fetal HLA antigens)
Prolonged storage of blood products (increased cytokine accumulation)
Immunocompromised patients with heightened inflammatory responses
clinicalPearls: FNHTR is a diagnosis of exclusion - you must ALWAYS rule out acute hemolytic reaction and bacterial contamination first before attributing fever to a benign febrile reaction
Leukoreduction filters remove donor white blood cells and have dramatically reduced FNHTR rates - this is now standard practice in many institutions
A temperature rise of 1 degree Celsius or more above baseline during or within 4 hours of transfusion defines an FNHTR
Premedication with acetaminophen (and sometimes diphenhydramine) is recommended for patients who have had two or more febrile reactions to reduce recurrence
Platelet transfusions cause more febrile reactions than RBC transfusions because platelets are stored at room temperature, allowing more cytokine accumulation

hand-hygiene.title: Hand Hygiene and Handwashing
hand-hygiene.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hand Hygiene and Handwashing, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Lack of accessible hand hygiene supplies
Caring for patients with multidrug-resistant organisms
clinicalPearls: Hand hygiene is the #1 most effective intervention to prevent healthcare-associated infections. Compliance rates in healthcare are often below 50%: every nurse must be a champion for hand hygiene.
Remember: ABHR is preferred for routine hand hygiene, but soap and water is REQUIRED for C.diff, norovirus, and visibly soiled hands. If you can see it, you must wash it.

The WHO 5 Moments framework provides a systematic approach: Before patient contact, Before aseptic procedure, After body fluid exposure risk, After patient contact, After contact with patient surroundings.

Proper technique matters as much as frequency: ensure all surfaces are covered (palms, backs, between fingers, thumbs, fingertips, wrists) for a minimum of 20 seconds. Singing 'Happy Birthday' twice approximates 20 seconds.
Skin integrity is essential: damaged skin harbors more organisms. Use facility-provided moisturizers, avoid hot water, and pat hands dry rather than rubbing to maintain skin health.

ppe-basics.title: PPE: Donning and Doffing
ppe-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de PPE: Donning and Doffing, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incorrect PPE selection for precaution type
Failure to perform hand hygiene between steps
clinicalPearls: Donning order: Gown → Mask/Respirator → Eye Protection → Gloves. Memory aid: 'Going May Eat Grapes.' Gloves go on LAST so they can overlap the gown cuffs.
Doffing order: Gloves → Eye Protection → Gown → Mask/Respirator. The most contaminated item (gloves) comes off FIRST. The mask comes off LAST to maintain respiratory protection as long as possible.
Perform hand hygiene BETWEEN doffing steps: especially immediately after removing gloves and after removing gown. This is the most commonly missed step and a major source of self-contamination.
The #1 doffing error is touching the outside (contaminated surface) of PPE with bare or clean hands. Always grasp ties, bands, or edges: never the front surface of masks, goggles, or gowns.
N95 respirators require annual fit testing AND a user seal check every time they are donned. If you have facial hair in the seal area, you cannot achieve a proper seal: use a PAPR instead.

isolation-precautions-rpn.title: Isolation Precautions Overview
isolation-precautions-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Isolation Precautions Overview, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Standard precautions apply to ALL patients: not just those with known infections. This is the single most important concept in infection prevention.
Memory aid for airborne organisms: 'MTV': Measles, TB, Varicella (Chickenpox). These require negative pressure rooms and N95 respirators.
Contact precautions require dedicated equipment in the room (stethoscope, BP cuff, thermometer). This prevents indirect contact transmission through shared equipment.
C.diff requires BOTH contact precautions AND soap and water hand hygiene (not ABHR). Alcohol does not kill C.diff spores: mechanical removal through handwashing is essential.

Always communicate isolation precaution status during patient handoff/report and when calling for transport, diagnostic testing, or consultations. Every person entering the room must know the required precautions.

sterile-technique.title: Sterile Technique and Asepsis
sterile-technique.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sterile Technique and Asepsis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The cardinal rule: If sterility is questioned or uncertain, consider the item or field CONTAMINATED. Starting over is always the correct choice: never assume sterility.
Never turn your back on a sterile field or leave it unattended. If you must leave, the field is considered contaminated and must be re-established.
The 1-inch border rule: The outer 1 inch of any sterile drape is considered unsterile. Place all sterile items well within the center of the field, away from the edges.
Strike-through contamination occurs when moisture penetrates a sterile barrier, creating a pathway for microorganisms. If any part of the sterile field becomes wet, the ENTIRE field is contaminated.
Sterile items held below waist level are considered contaminated: always keep sterile items within the sterile field and above waist level within your line of sight.

airborne-precautions.title: Airborne Precautions (RN Level)
airborne-precautions.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Airborne Precautions (RN Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Remember 'MTV' for airborne organisms: Measles, TB, Varicella. These are the key airborne-transmitted infections requiring N95/PAPR and negative pressure rooms.

The AIIR door must remain CLOSED at all times: opening the door disrupts negative pressure and allows contaminated air to escape into the hallway. Minimize entries and exits. Combine care activities to reduce door openings.

N95 fit testing is not optional: it is a regulatory requirement (OSHA). Annual fit testing AND a user seal check with every donning are both mandatory. A poorly fitting N95 provides no more protection than a surgical mask.

TB isolation continues until the patient has 3 consecutive negative AFB sputum cultures (collected 8-24 hours apart, early morning specimens). A single negative culture is NOT sufficient to discontinue airborne precautions.

When transporting an airborne-precaution patient, place a surgical mask on the PATIENT (not an N95: the purpose is source control, not filtration). The patient does not need to wear an N95 respirator.

droplet-precautions.title: Droplet Precautions (RN Level)
droplet-precautions.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Droplet Precautions (RN Level), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: KEY distinction: Droplet precautions require a surgical mask; Airborne precautions require an N95 respirator. Droplets are larger (>5 microns), heavier, and fall within 3-6 feet. Airborne particles are smaller (<5 microns) and remain suspended.

No negative pressure room is needed for droplet precautions: this is a critical differentiator from airborne precautions. Regular room ventilation is adequate because droplets do not remain suspended in the air.
Memory aid for common droplet organisms: 'My Flu Is Spreading, Please Stop': Meningococcal meningitis, Flu (Influenza), Invasive group A strep, Streptococcal pharyngitis, Pertussis, Strep pneumoniae.
Meningococcal meningitis requires droplet precautions until 24 hours after effective antibiotic therapy AND close contact prophylaxis. This is a PUBLIC HEALTH EMERGENCY requiring immediate reporting.

Patient transport on droplet precautions: Place a surgical mask on the PATIENT before transport (source control). The patient does not wear an N95: the surgical mask contains their droplets. Notify the receiving department of precaution status before the patient arrives.

mastitis-rpn.title: Mastitis - RPN Foundation
mastitis-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Mastitis - RPN Foundation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Continue breastfeeding - stopping increases risk of abscess formation
Start feeding on the affected breast first to ensure complete emptying
Warm compresses before feeding, cool compresses after for comfort
Proper latch technique prevents recurrence - refer to lactation consultant
If symptoms do not improve within 48 hours of antibiotics, suspect abscess or MRSA

mastitis.title: Mastitis - RN Clinical Management
mastitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Mastitis - RN Clinical Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Nipple fissures or trauma providing bacterial portal of entry
Milk stasis from poor latch, skipped feedings, or weaning
Primiparity with inexperience in breastfeeding technique
S. aureus nasal carriage in mother or infant
Tight bras or pressure on breast tissue
clinicalPearls: Mark erythema borders with skin marker to track progression or resolution on antibiotics
Failure to improve within 48-72 hours of appropriate antibiotics warrants ultrasound for abscess
MRSA prevalence in community-acquired mastitis is 10-15% - culture milk if risk factors present
Total colectomy is NOT related - mastitis is breast tissue infection requiring antibiotics and continued breastfeeding
Galactocele (milk-filled cyst) can mimic abscess - ultrasound differentiates
Inflammatory breast cancer must be considered in non-lactating women with breast erythema and peau d'orange skin

mastitis-np.title: Mastitis - NP Advanced Practice
mastitis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Mastitis - NP Advanced Practice, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: S. aureus nasal colonization (maternal or infant)
MRSA community prevalence and risk factors
Prior breast surgery or radiation altering ductal anatomy
clinicalPearls: PVL-producing MRSA strains cause more necrotizing tissue destruction - suspect if rapid clinical deterioration despite appropriate antibiotics
Abscess aspiration success rate is 85-90% with ultrasound guidance - I&D reserved for failed aspiration or multiloculated collections
MRSA decolonization reduces recurrence by 60% - prescribe mupirocin nasal ointment BID x 5 days plus chlorhexidine washes for mother and infant
Non-lactational breast erythema in women over 40 must be evaluated for inflammatory breast cancer - skin punch biopsy shows dermal lymphatic invasion
Breast milk culture: >10^3 CFU/mL of a single pathogen confirms infection vs contamination

fetal-monitoring-advanced.title: Surveillance fœtale avancée
fetal-monitoring-advanced.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Surveillance fœtale avancée, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: VEAL CHOP: Variable-Cord, Early-Head, Accel-Ok, Late-Placenta
Amnioinfusion can relieve variable decels (cord compression)
Acidemia: Umbilical artery pH < 7.20 indicates fetal compromise

prenatal-basics.title: Soins prénataux
prenatal-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins prénataux, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: GTPAL: Gravida = total pregnancies; Para = deliveries past 20 weeks (T-P-A-L)
Nagele's Rule: LMP first day minus 3 months plus 7 days = EDD
Fundal height at umbilicus = approximately 20 weeks gestation

labor-stages.title: Estágios do parto
labor-stages.overview: Estágios do parto é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Transition phase is the shortest but most intense; provide continuous support
Monitor FHR continuously during active labor and Stage 2
Stage 4 hemorrhage risk highest: assess fundal tone and lochia q15min x1hr

postpartum-basics.title: Soins post-partum
postpartum-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins post-partum, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: A boggy uterus is the #1 cause of postpartum hemorrhage: massage the fundus firmly
Bladder distention displaces the uterus and prevents contraction
Lochia should never contain large clots (>golf ball size) or have a foul odor

breastfeeding-basics.title: Allaitement maternel
breastfeeding-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Allaitement maternel, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Feed on demand, typically every 2-3 hours (8-12 times per day) in the newborn period
Contraindications to breastfeeding: HIV (in developed countries), active herpes on breast, certain medications, active substance abuse
Cabbage leaves and cold compresses help relieve engorgement; warm compresses before feeding aid let-down

placenta-previa-abruption.title: Placenta Previa vs Abruption
placenta-previa-abruption.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Placenta Previa vs Abruption, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Placenta previa: previous C-section, multiple gestation, advanced maternal age, smoking, prior previa
Abruption: hypertension (most common cause), cocaine use, trauma, short umbilical cord, rapid decompression of uterus
clinicalPearls: NEVER perform a vaginal exam with suspected Placenta Previa: may trigger massive hemorrhage
Abruption is a surgical emergency if severe: prepare for emergent C-section
Monitor for Couvelaire uterus (blood infiltrates myometrium, uterus appears purple/bruised)
Abruption risk factors: HTN, cocaine use, abdominal trauma, prior abruption

postpartum-hemorrhage.title: Hémorragie du post-partum
postpartum-hemorrhage.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hémorragie du post-partum, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Fundal massage is the FIRST intervention for uterine atony: firm, circular massage on the fundus
Quantitative blood loss (QBL) measurement is more accurate than visual estimation
Early activation of massive transfusion protocol (1:1:1 ratio of PRBCs:FFP:Platelets)
Two large-bore IVs and type & crossmatch should be done proactively for high-risk patients

gestational-diabetes.title: Diabète gestationnel
gestational-diabetes.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Diabète gestationnel, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Screen ALL pregnant women at 24-28 weeks with 1-hour glucose challenge test (50g glucose, threshold ≥130-140 mg/dL)
If 1-hour screen is positive, confirm with 3-hour GTT (100g glucose)
Postpartum: recheck glucose at 6-12 weeks: 50% of GDM patients develop Type 2 DM within 10 years
Diet and exercise are FIRST-LINE treatment; pharmacotherapy added if glucose targets not met

fetal-monitoring-rn.title: Surveillance fœtale – IA
fetal-monitoring-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Surveillance fœtale – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Late decelerations = uteroplacental insufficiency → turn off Pitocin, position left lateral, O2, IV fluid bolus
Variable decelerations = cord compression → reposition mother (knee-chest, Trendelenburg), amnioinfusion may help
Moderate variability is the SINGLE MOST reassuring sign of fetal well-being
Category III tracing requires immediate intervention: prepare for emergent delivery

postpartum-depression-care.title: Dépression post-partum
postpartum-depression-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Dépression post-partum, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Screen ALL postpartum patients with the Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale (EPDS) at the 4-6 week visit, 3-month visit, and 6-month visit. An EPDS score ≥10 warrants further assessment; ≥13 is consistent with probable depression. Question #10 specifically asks about self-harm: ANY positive response requires immediate safety assessment and intervention.

Differentiate baby blues vs. PPD vs. postpartum psychosis: Baby blues (onset days 3-5, resolves by 2 weeks, mild tearfulness and mood lability, NO treatment needed beyond support). PPD (onset typically 4-6 weeks, persists >2 weeks, significant functional impairment, requires treatment). Postpartum psychosis (onset 48-72 hours, psychiatric emergency: hallucinations, delusions, disorganized behavior, high risk of harm to self or infant: requires IMMEDIATE psychiatric hospitalization, mood stabilizers, and antipsychotics).

Breastfeeding and antidepressants: Untreated PPD is MORE harmful to the infant (impaired bonding, developmental delays, insecure attachment) than SSRI exposure through breast milk. Sertraline and paroxetine have the lowest breast milk transfer. The benefits of treating maternal depression overwhelmingly outweigh the minimal risks of infant SSRI exposure. NEVER advise a mother to choose between breastfeeding and medication: she can do both.

Safety planning is essential: Assess for suicidal/homicidal ideation at every visit. Ask directly: many mothers are afraid to disclose intrusive thoughts of harming their baby because they fear the baby will be taken away. Normalize these fears while emphasizing they are symptoms of illness, not character flaws. Ensure a safety plan is in place: remove access to lethal means, identify support persons, provide crisis numbers.

PPD affects the entire family. Partners report higher rates of depression (8-10%). Infants of mothers with untreated PPD show impaired cognitive and emotional development, insecure attachment, and behavioral problems. Treating maternal PPD improves outcomes for the entire family unit.

Non-pharmacologic interventions are first-line for mild PPD and adjunctive for moderate-severe: Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), interpersonal therapy (IPT), exercise (30 minutes of moderate aerobic activity 3-5x/week has antidepressant efficacy comparable to SSRIs for mild-moderate depression), social support groups, adequate sleep (enlist partner/family for nighttime feeding shifts), omega-3 fatty acid supplementation (EPA/DHA: modest evidence for mood improvement).

med-math-dosage-calculations.title: Dosage Calculation Fundamentals
med-math-dosage-calculations.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Dosage Calculation Fundamentals, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incorrect use of trailing zeros (e.g., 1.0 mg misread as 10 mg)
Lack of leading zeros (e.g., .5 mg misread as 5 mg)
clinicalPearls: The formula method: Desired ÷ Have × Quantity = Amount to Give. This is the most commonly tested method on nursing exams.
NEVER use trailing zeros (write 1 mg, not 1.0 mg) and ALWAYS use leading zeros (write 0.5 mg, not .5 mg)  -  ISMP and Joint Commission patient safety standards.
If your calculated answer seems unreasonable (e.g., 10 tablets, 15 mL IM), STOP and recheck. Most single doses are 1-2 tablets or 0.5-3 mL for injections.
Dimensional analysis is the safest method because units must cancel  -  if they don't, you set it up wrong.
For split tablets: only score tablets that are scored. Unscored tablets cannot be accurately halved.
High-alert medications (insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, potassium chloride) require independent double-checks by two nurses.
Rounding rules: oral liquids round to nearest tenth (0.1 mL). IV rates round to nearest whole number. Pediatric doses  -  do not round down below ordered dose.

med-math-iv-flow-rates.title: IV Flow Rate Calculations
med-math-iv-flow-rates.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de IV Flow Rate Calculations, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Pediatric and elderly patients at higher risk for fluid overload
clinicalPearls: Master formula: gtt/min = (Volume in mL ÷ Time in minutes) × Drop Factor
Pump formula: mL/hr = Total Volume ÷ Total Hours  -  this is the simplest and most commonly used in practice.
Microdrip shortcut: With 60 gtt/mL tubing, gtt/min = mL/hr. No extra calculation needed!
If an IV falls behind schedule, NEVER increase the rate to 'catch up' without a provider order  -  this can cause fluid overload, especially in cardiac and renal patients.
Common drop factors to memorize: Macrodrip = 10, 15, or 20 gtt/mL. Microdrip = always 60 gtt/mL.
Counting drops: Count for 15 seconds and multiply by 4. Adjust the roller clamp until the correct rate is achieved.
Always reassess the IV site and flow rate at least every 1-2 hours for gravity infusions.

med-math-weight-based-dosing.title: Weight-Based Dosing Calculations
med-math-weight-based-dosing.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Weight-Based Dosing Calculations, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Using pounds instead of kilograms (2.2× overdose potential)
Failure to verify safe dose range
Pediatric patients with rapidly changing weights
Obese patients (may need ideal body weight for some drugs)
Decimal point errors in mg/kg calculations
clinicalPearls: The most common and dangerous error: using pounds instead of kilograms. Always convert first: lbs ÷ 2.2 = kg.
Pediatric safe dose range check is NON-NEGOTIABLE: calculate the low and high mg/kg range for the child's weight, then verify the ordered dose falls between them.
mg/kg/day means the TOTAL daily dose  -  divide by the number of doses per day to get each individual dose.
mg/kg/dose means each INDIVIDUAL dose is calculated at that rate.
For obese patients, some medications use ideal body weight (IBW) or adjusted body weight instead of actual weight. Check the drug reference.
Neonatal doses are often in mcg/kg  -  be extremely careful with unit conversions (1 mg = 1000 mcg).
Always weigh the patient  -  never rely on patient-reported or estimated weights for medication calculations.

med-math-infusion-rates.title: Infusion Rate & Titration Calculations
med-math-infusion-rates.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infusion Rate & Titration Calculations, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Incorrect drug concentration used in calculation
Failure to account for patient weight in mcg/kg/min orders
Failure to titrate according to protocol parameters
Using the wrong units (mg vs mcg: 1000-fold difference)
Multiple simultaneous drips with drug interactions
clinicalPearls: Unit conversion is critical: 1 mg = 1,000 mcg. Always convert the drug concentration to match the ordered units before calculating.
For mcg/kg/min orders, you need THREE pieces of information: the ordered rate, the patient's weight in kg, and the drug concentration.
Standard concentrations are used for safety (e.g., dopamine 400 mg/250 mL, heparin 25,000 units/500 mL). Know your facility's standard concentrations.
Titration documentation: record the time, new rate, parameter value that prompted the change, and patient response.
If a patient on a vasopressor drip needs to use the bathroom, DO NOT disconnect the drip  -  use a bedpan or bedside commode.
Always calculate what dose the patient is receiving when you take over care  -  do not assume the previous nurse's rate is correct.
Smart pumps with drug libraries reduce programming errors but do NOT eliminate them  -  always verify the calculation independently.

med-math-pediatric-dosing.title: Pediatric Dosing & Safety
med-math-pediatric-dosing.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pediatric Dosing & Safety, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Weight-based calculation errors (most common pediatric med error)
Using adult doses reduced by fraction (incorrect approach)
Volume errors in small syringes (0.01 mL matters)
Concentration errors when multiple formulations exist
Caregiver measuring errors at home (using kitchen spoons)
clinicalPearls: Golden rule: Always use mg/kg dosing from a pediatric drug reference. NEVER guess, estimate, or use a fraction of the adult dose.
The two most dangerous pediatric medication errors: (1) using pounds instead of kilograms, (2) using the wrong concentration formulation.
Infant acetaminophen drops and children's liquid have DIFFERENT concentrations  -  using the wrong one causes overdose or underdose.
Oral syringes are preferred over dosing cups for accuracy. A kitchen teaspoon is NOT 5 mL  -  it varies from 2-7 mL.
For neonates, doses may be in mcg/kg. Remember: 1 mg = 1,000 mcg. A decimal error here is catastrophic.
Body Surface Area (BSA) method: used for chemotherapy and some specialized drugs. BSA (m²) = √(height cm × weight kg ÷ 3600).
Teach parents: 'The dose that is right for one child may be wrong for another child of a different weight. Never share medications between children.'

med-math-reconstitution.title: Reconstitution & Dilution Calculations
med-math-reconstitution.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Reconstitution & Dilution Calculations, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Using the wrong diluent (can cause precipitation or hemolysis)
Adding incorrect volume of diluent (changes concentration)
Not reading the vial label for specific reconstitution instructions
Failure to label reconstituted multi-dose vials
Using reconstituted medication past its expiration
Not accounting for displacement volume in high-concentration reconstitution
clinicalPearls: Displacement volume: the powder itself occupies space. If you add 3 mL diluent to a 1 g vial and get 3.5 mL total volume, the displacement volume is 0.5 mL. This matters for dose accuracy.
BWFI (bacteriostatic water) is NEVER used for neonates  -  the benzyl alcohol preservative causes gasping syndrome and can be fatal.
After reconstitution, the clock starts ticking on stability. Most reconstituted medications are stable for 24 hours at room temperature or 7 days refrigerated  -  but ALWAYS check the specific drug.
If the reconstituted solution appears cloudy, has particles, or has changed color, DO NOT administer  -  discard and prepare a new dose.
For IV piggyback medications, reconstitution is Step 1, followed by further dilution in a compatible IV solution (usually NS or D5W).
Always label reconstituted multi-dose vials: Date, Time, Concentration (mg/mL), Initials, Expiration date/time.

med-math-unit-conversions.title: Unit Conversions in Medication Math
med-math-unit-conversions.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Unit Conversions in Medication Math, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to include units in calculations
Relying on memory instead of verified conversion factors
clinicalPearls: The 'staircase' method for metric conversions: King Henry Died By Drinking Chocolate Milk = Kilo, Hecto, Deka, Base, Deci, Centi, Milli. Each step = multiply or divide by 10.
Critical conversions to memorize: 1 g = 1000 mg, 1 mg = 1000 mcg, 1 L = 1000 mL, 1 kg = 2.2 lb, 1 tsp = 5 mL, 1 tbsp = 15 mL, 1 oz = 30 mL.
ISMP 'Do Not Use' abbreviations: Write 'units' (not 'U'), write 'mcg' (not 'μg'), write 'mL' (not 'cc').
Dimensional analysis prevents errors because if the units don't cancel correctly, the setup is wrong  -  it's a built-in error check.
When in doubt, ask: 'Does this answer make sense?' A single dose of 500,000 mg of anything should raise a red flag.
For pediatric patients, the difference between mg and mcg is the difference between a therapeutic dose and a lethal dose.

med-math-drip-rate-formulas.title: Drip Rate Formulas & Shortcuts
med-math-drip-rate-formulas.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Drip Rate Formulas & Shortcuts, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Using wrong drop factor for the tubing being used
Forgetting to convert hours to minutes
Not verifying the rate by counting drops after setting up
Gravity infusion rate drift due to patient position changes
clinicalPearls: Drop factor shortcuts save time on exams: 60 gtt/mL → gtt/min = mL/hr. 20 gtt/mL → ÷3. 15 gtt/mL → ÷4. 10 gtt/mL → ÷6.
The shortcut works because: Drop Factor ÷ 60 min/hr = the divisor. For 15 gtt/mL: 60÷15 = 4, so divide mL/hr by 4.
Counting drops: count for 15 seconds and multiply by 4. This is more accurate than counting for a full minute because patient position can change during counting.
If the IV is infusing by gravity, patient arm position matters. Raising the arm above the IV bag can stop the flow. Lowering the arm below the bag increases flow rate.
Time-taping the IV bag: mark hourly volumes on tape attached to the bag. This provides quick visual verification that the infusion is on track.
If an infusion is behind schedule, NEVER speed it up without a provider order. Rapid infusion can cause fluid overload, especially in cardiac/renal/pediatric patients.

med-math-safe-dose-range.title: Safe Dose Range Verification
med-math-safe-dose-range.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Safe Dose Range Verification, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to check the drug reference before administering
Not accounting for renal/hepatic dose adjustments
Assuming the prescriber's order is always correct
Not calculating the safe range for each individual patient
Using adult dose ranges for pediatric patients
Polypharmacy with multiple drugs affecting the same system
clinicalPearls: The nurse's obligation: You are legally and ethically required to verify dose safety before administration. 'The doctor ordered it' is NOT a defense for administering an unsafe dose.
Safe dose range verification is MANDATORY for all pediatric patients, all weight-based medications, and all narrow therapeutic index drugs.
When holding a medication for safety concerns, always document: (1) the medication held, (2) the reason (with calculation), (3) the prescriber notified, (4) the prescriber's response.
Narrow therapeutic index drugs (digoxin, lithium, warfarin, aminoglycosides, vancomycin, theophylline, phenytoin) require serum drug levels for monitoring.
Renal impairment affects drug clearance  -  many drugs require dose reduction or extended intervals when GFR is decreased.
When in doubt: HOLD the medication, check the reference, calculate the range, and CALL the prescriber. It is always safer to verify than to guess.

schizophrenia.title: Schizophrenia
schizophrenia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Schizophrenia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of schizophrenia or psychotic disorders
Prenatal exposure to infections or malnutrition
Cannabis use during adolescence accelerating dopamine sensitization
Advanced paternal age increasing de novo genetic mutations
Childhood trauma and adverse childhood experiences
Obstetric complications including hypoxia at birth
clinicalPearls: Never argue with a patient's delusions - instead acknowledge feelings ('I can see that is frightening for you') and redirect to reality-based topics

The prodromal phase of schizophrenia often presents with social withdrawal, declining academic performance, and unusual perceptual experiences years before frank psychosis - early intervention services improve long-term outcomes

Neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS) is a life-threatening emergency caused by dopamine blockade presenting with hyperthermia, lead-pipe rigidity, altered mental status, and autonomic instability - stop the antipsychotic immediately and administer dantrolene

major-depressive-disorder.title: Major Depressive Disorder
major-depressive-disorder.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Major Depressive Disorder, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of mood disorders indicating genetic vulnerability
Female sex - lifetime prevalence approximately twice that of males due to hormonal fluctuations
Chronic medical illness (diabetes, cardiovascular disease, hypothyroidism)
History of childhood abuse or neglect altering stress-response circuits
Social isolation and lack of meaningful relationships
Substance use disorders - alcohol is a CNS depressant that depletes monoamines
Recent significant loss (bereavement, divorce, job loss)
Postpartum hormonal shifts (estrogen and progesterone withdrawal)
clinicalPearls: The period of greatest suicide risk is 2-3 weeks after starting antidepressant therapy - the medication restores psychomotor energy before lifting depressed mood, giving patients the energy to act on suicidal plans they previously lacked motivation to execute
Always ask about bipolar history before prescribing an antidepressant alone - SSRIs given without a mood stabilizer can trigger a manic episode in undiagnosed bipolar disorder
Tyramine-rich foods (aged cheese, cured meats, red wine) must be avoided with MAOIs because tyramine potentiates norepinephrine release, causing hypertensive crisis

ptsd.title: Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder
ptsd.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Direct exposure to combat, sexual assault, natural disaster, or life-threatening event
Prior history of childhood trauma or abuse
Female sex - approximately twice the prevalence of males
Peritraumatic dissociation (dissociating during the traumatic event)
Lack of social support following the trauma
Repeated occupational trauma exposure (first responders, military)
Traumatic brain injury co-occurring with psychological trauma
clinicalPearls: Benzodiazepines are contraindicated in PTSD because they impair fear extinction learning, increase dissociation risk, and have high abuse potential in this population - they worsen long-term outcomes despite short-term anxiety relief
Trauma-informed care means asking 'What happened to you?' rather than 'What is wrong with you?' - always allow the patient to maintain control over the pace of disclosure

Peritraumatic dissociation (dissociating during the actual traumatic event) is the strongest predictor of developing PTSD - these patients fail to integrate the memory through normal hippocampal processing

panic-disorder.title: Panic Disorder
panic-disorder.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Panic Disorder, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of panic disorder or anxiety disorders
Female sex - twice as common as in males
Excessive caffeine or stimulant use lowering panic threshold
History of childhood physical or sexual abuse
Comorbid depression or other anxiety disorders
Smoking - nicotine alters respiratory chemoreceptor sensitivity
clinicalPearls: A panic attack peaks within 10 minutes and typically resolves within 20-30 minutes - if symptoms persist beyond 30 minutes, reassess for an actual medical emergency such as myocardial infarction or pulmonary embolism

Hyperventilation during panic causes respiratory alkalosis, which reduces ionized calcium and produces tetany-like symptoms (paresthesias, carpopedal spasm) - this is why patients feel they are 'dying' even though they are medically stable
Avoid paper bag rebreathing in panic attacks - while it corrects hypocapnia, it can worsen hypoxia if the patient has an underlying pulmonary or cardiac condition causing the dyspnea

antisocial-personality-disorder.title: Antisocial Personality Disorder
antisocial-personality-disorder.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Antisocial Personality Disorder, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of childhood abuse, neglect, or unstable home environment
Male sex - three to five times more common in males
Parental antisocial behavior or substance abuse
Low socioeconomic status and peer delinquency
Traumatic brain injury affecting frontal lobe function
Substance use disorders (especially alcohol and stimulants)
clinicalPearls: ASPD cannot be diagnosed before age 18, but conduct disorder in childhood is a prerequisite - look for the triad of fire-setting, cruelty to animals, and bedwetting (MacDonald triad) as warning signs

Patients with ASPD are highly skilled at manipulating staff through flattery, intimidation, or splitting - maintain consistent limits across all team members and communicate openly about patient interactions

There is no medication that treats ASPD itself - pharmacotherapy targets specific symptoms (impulsive aggression, emotional dysregulation, comorbid depression) rather than the core personality pathology

conversion-disorder.title: Conversion Disorder (Functional Neurological Symptom Disorder)
conversion-disorder.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Conversion Disorder (Functional Neurological Symptom Disorder), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Recent significant psychological stressor or trauma
History of physical or sexual abuse
Female sex - two to three times more common
Comorbid anxiety, depression, or dissociative disorders
History of other somatic symptom or functional neurological disorders
Lower socioeconomic status and limited health literacy
Alexithymia - difficulty identifying and expressing emotions
clinicalPearls: Conversion disorder symptoms are NOT consciously produced (unlike malingering) and NOT imagined (unlike factitious disorder) - the neural circuit disruption is real, but functional rather than structural

The Hoover sign is the most reliable bedside test for functional leg weakness: ask the patient to flex the strong hip against resistance while observing involuntary extension of the 'paralyzed' leg, proving the motor pathway is intact

Avoid using the term 'psychogenic' with patients as it implies the symptoms are not real - use 'functional neurological disorder' and explain that the brain's software (function) is disrupted while the hardware (structure) is intact

alcohol-withdrawal.title: Alcohol Withdrawal
alcohol-withdrawal.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Alcohol Withdrawal, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of heavy prolonged alcohol use (typically greater than 10 years)
Previous episodes of alcohol withdrawal or delirium tremens
Concurrent acute illness or infection during withdrawal
Older age with longer duration of alcohol dependence
Elevated blood alcohol level at presentation indicating high tolerance
History of withdrawal seizures (kindling effect increases severity with each episode)
Hepatic dysfunction impairing alcohol and medication metabolism
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS give thiamine BEFORE glucose - glucose metabolism requires thiamine as a cofactor, and administering glucose to a thiamine-depleted alcoholic patient will consume remaining thiamine stores, precipitating acute Wernicke encephalopathy (confusion, ataxia, ophthalmoplegia)

The kindling phenomenon means each successive alcohol withdrawal episode is more severe than the last - a patient who had mild tremors in a previous withdrawal may progress to seizures or DTs in the current episode
Delirium tremens has a 5-15% mortality rate if untreated - the most common cause of death is cardiovascular collapse from autonomic instability, not seizures

opioid-withdrawal.title: Opioid Withdrawal
opioid-withdrawal.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Opioid Withdrawal, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic opioid use for more than two weeks at therapeutic or supratherapeutic doses
Abrupt discontinuation of long-acting opioids or methadone
Administration of opioid antagonists (naloxone) to opioid-dependent patients
Lack of access to medication-assisted treatment (MAT) programs
Co-occurring mental health disorders (depression, PTSD, anxiety)
clinicalPearls: Opioid withdrawal is extremely uncomfortable but NOT directly life-threatening in healthy adults, unlike alcohol and benzodiazepine withdrawal which can cause fatal seizures - however, dehydration from GI losses can be dangerous

The most dangerous period is AFTER withdrawal when tolerance has been lost - patients who relapse and use their previous dose are at extreme risk for fatal respiratory depression because their receptors have resensitized

Never administer naloxone to manage withdrawal symptoms - naloxone is an antagonist that will precipitate severe, sudden withdrawal; it is reserved for acute opioid overdose with respiratory depression

insomnia.title: Insomnia
insomnia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insomnia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex - hormonal fluctuations during menstrual cycle, pregnancy, and menopause disrupt sleep architecture
Advancing age - decreased melatonin production and reduced slow-wave sleep
Psychiatric disorders (depression, anxiety, PTSD) causing hyperarousal
Shift work or irregular schedules disrupting circadian rhythm
Excessive caffeine, nicotine, or alcohol use (alcohol fragments sleep architecture)
Chronic pain conditions preventing sustained sleep
Screen exposure before bedtime suppressing melatonin via blue light
Obstructive sleep apnea or restless leg syndrome causing frequent awakenings
clinicalPearls: CBT-I is first-line treatment for chronic insomnia and is MORE effective than medications long-term because it addresses the underlying behavioral and cognitive perpetuating factors rather than just inducing sedation

Sleep restriction therapy sounds counterintuitive but works by building homeostatic sleep drive - limit time in bed to match actual sleep time, then gradually extend as sleep efficiency improves above 85%

Alcohol is the most commonly used 'sleep aid' but actually worsens insomnia - it suppresses REM sleep, causes rebound wakefulness in the second half of the night, and produces fragmented, non-restorative sleep

narcolepsy.title: Narcolepsy
narcolepsy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Narcolepsy, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HLA-DQB1*06:02 haplotype (present in 98% of Type 1 narcolepsy patients)
Family history of narcolepsy (though most cases are sporadic)
Post-streptococcal autoimmune response targeting orexin neurons
H1N1 influenza or H1N1 vaccination (Pandemrix) as potential autoimmune trigger
Adolescent or young adult onset (typical peak onset 10-20 years of age)
Traumatic brain injury affecting hypothalamic structures
clinicalPearls: Cataplexy is pathognomonic for Type 1 narcolepsy - if a patient reports sudden muscle weakness triggered by laughing or emotional situations, think narcolepsy first
Narcolepsy is NOT just 'being tired' - it is an autoimmune-mediated loss of orexin neurons that destabilizes the sleep-wake boundary, causing REM sleep phenomena to intrude into wakefulness

The MSLT must follow a normal overnight polysomnography to rule out sleep deprivation as a cause of daytime sleepiness - two or more SOREMPs (sleep-onset REM periods) with mean sleep latency under 8 minutes is diagnostic

agoraphobia.title: Agoraphobia
agoraphobia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Agoraphobia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of panic disorder (agoraphobia frequently develops secondary to panic attacks)
Traumatic experience in a public place or enclosed space
Female sex - twice as prevalent as in males
Childhood separation anxiety or behavioral inhibition temperament
Family history of agoraphobia or panic disorder
Substance use as a coping mechanism for anxiety
clinicalPearls: Agoraphobia can exist without panic disorder (DSM-5 separated them) - the core fear is about being in situations where escape is difficult or help is unavailable, not just about having panic attacks

Avoidance is the maintaining factor in agoraphobia - each avoided situation reinforces the belief that the situation is dangerous, strengthening the fear through negative reinforcement; exposure therapy breaks this cycle
The most functionally impaired agoraphobia patients become completely housebound - assess for secondary complications including depression, deconditioning, vitamin D deficiency, and social isolation

intimate-partner-violence.title: Intimate Partner Violence
intimate-partner-violence.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Intimate Partner Violence, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Previous history of IPV victimization or childhood exposure to domestic violence
Social isolation from family and friends (often enforced by the abuser)
Financial dependence on the abuser or shared financial resources
Pregnancy and the postpartum period (violence often escalates during pregnancy)
Substance use by either partner lowering inhibitory control
Low self-esteem and history of depression or anxiety
Cultural or religious beliefs discouraging separation
Immigration status dependency on the abuser
clinicalPearls: The most dangerous time for a victim is when they attempt to leave the relationship - homicide risk increases significantly at separation; this is why a detailed safety plan is essential BEFORE leaving

Strangulation is the single most important risk factor for IPV homicide - any history of strangulation by the partner means the victim is 7 times more likely to be killed; assess for delayed airway swelling and refer for imaging

Never write 'IPV' or 'domestic violence' on discharge paperwork that the abuser might see - document safety resources given in a way that does not endanger the patient (e.g., include hotline number inside a medication instruction sheet)

bulimia-nervosa.title: Bulimia Nervosa
bulimia-nervosa.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bulimia Nervosa, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex - approximately 10:1 female to male ratio
Adolescent or young adult onset (peak 18-25 years)
History of dieting or weight-related teasing
Participation in appearance-focused activities (gymnastics, dance, modeling, wrestling)
Family history of eating disorders, mood disorders, or substance abuse
Perfectionism and low self-esteem with self-worth tied to body shape and weight
History of childhood sexual abuse or trauma
Comorbid depression, anxiety, or borderline personality disorder
clinicalPearls: Hypokalemia is the most dangerous acute complication of bulimia nervosa - it can cause fatal cardiac dysrhythmias (torsades de pointes) and must be corrected aggressively with cardiac monitoring
Unlike anorexia nervosa, patients with bulimia typically maintain a normal or near-normal BMI, which can delay recognition and diagnosis - severity is determined by purging frequency, not weight

Russell sign (knuckle calluses) is a hallmark physical finding but may be absent in patients who use instruments rather than fingers for emesis induction, or who primarily use laxatives or diuretics as compensatory behaviors

dsm5-personality-disorders.title: DSM-5 Personality Disorders (Cluster A/B/C Overview)
dsm5-personality-disorders.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de DSM-5 Personality Disorders (Cluster A/B/C Overview), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Childhood abuse, neglect, or invalidating family environment
Insecure or disorganized attachment with primary caregivers
Genetic predisposition (family history of personality disorders or related psychiatric conditions)
