Temperamental extremes in childhood (behavioral inhibition, emotional reactivity, callous-unemotional traits)
Traumatic brain injury affecting frontal lobe development
Substance abuse during critical neurodevelopmental periods
Chronic peer victimization or bullying during childhood and adolescence
Parental psychopathology (personality disorders, substance use, mood disorders)
clinicalPearls: Remember the clusters using the mnemonic: Cluster A is 'weird' (odd/eccentric), Cluster B is 'wild' (dramatic/emotional/erratic), and Cluster C is 'worried' (anxious/fearful)

Borderline personality disorder is the most commonly tested personality disorder on nursing exams - key features are unstable relationships, fear of abandonment, identity disturbance, impulsivity, chronic emptiness, and self-harm; DBT is the gold-standard treatment

Personality disorders are ego-syntonic (the person sees their behavior as normal) unlike most Axis I disorders which are ego-dystonic (the person recognizes something is wrong) - this makes engagement in treatment extremely challenging

postmortem-care.title: Postmortem Care
postmortem-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Postmortem Care, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Not applicable in the traditional sense - postmortem care applies to all deceased patients
Consideration of religious and cultural practices affecting care timing
Infectious disease precautions (hepatitis B/C, HIV, tuberculosis, COVID-19)
Pending autopsy or forensic investigation requiring evidence preservation
Organ or tissue donation status affecting care procedures
Family availability and emotional readiness for viewing
Legal documentation requirements varying by jurisdiction
clinicalPearls: If an autopsy or forensic investigation is pending, do NOT remove any tubes, IVs, catheters, endotracheal tubes, or drains - leave everything in place as they are considered evidence; removing them could compromise the investigation

Contact the organ procurement organization BEFORE discontinuing life-sustaining measures in potential donors - timing is critical for organ viability, and the nurse should never assume a patient is not eligible for donation

Allow family members as much private time with the body as they need - cultural and religious practices vary widely (some require immediate care, others prohibit touching the body) and the nurse should ask the family about their preferences and accommodate them

serotonin-syndrome.title: Serotonin Syndrome
serotonin-syndrome.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Serotonin Syndrome, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Concurrent use of two or more serotonergic medications (most common cause)
SSRI combined with MAOI (most dangerous combination - requires 14-day washout between medications)
Addition of tramadol or meperidine to SSRI therapy
Linezolid (antibiotic with MAOI activity) added to an SSRI
SSRI combined with triptans for migraine treatment
Illicit drug use: MDMA (ecstasy) or LSD with serotonergic medications
High-dose SSRI therapy or recent dose escalation
Over-the-counter dextromethorphan combined with serotonergic medications
clinicalPearls: The key differentiator between serotonin syndrome and neuroleptic malignant syndrome (NMS): serotonin syndrome has RAPID onset (hours), CLONUS and HYPERREFLEXIA, and MYDRIASIS; NMS has GRADUAL onset (days-weeks), LEAD-PIPE RIGIDITY, BRADYREFLEXIA, and is associated with antipsychotics, not serotonergic drugs

The most commonly tested drug interactions causing serotonin syndrome on nursing exams: SSRI + MAOI (most dangerous), SSRI + tramadol, SSRI + linezolid, SSRI + St. John's Wort, SSRI + dextromethorphan

Antipyretics (acetaminophen, NSAIDs) are ineffective for serotonin syndrome hyperthermia because the fever is generated by excessive skeletal muscle contraction, not by prostaglandin-mediated hypothalamic set-point elevation - cooling measures and muscle relaxation (benzodiazepines) are needed

tardive-dyskinesia.title: Tardive Dyskinesia
tardive-dyskinesia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tardive Dyskinesia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prolonged use of first-generation (typical) antipsychotics (haloperidol, chlorpromazine)
Higher cumulative antipsychotic dose and longer duration of treatment
Older age - elderly patients are more susceptible to nigrostriatal dopamine receptor changes
Female sex - higher incidence, especially postmenopausal women
Comorbid mood disorders (depression, bipolar disorder) treated with antipsychotics
Pre-existing extrapyramidal symptoms or movement disorders
African American descent (higher incidence possibly related to pharmacogenomic differences in D2 receptor density)
Concurrent anticholinergic medication use masking early TD symptoms
clinicalPearls: Tardive dyskinesia may be IRREVERSIBLE even after stopping the causative antipsychotic - this is why prevention through lowest effective dose and regular AIMS monitoring is critical; early detection improves the chance of reversibility

Do NOT increase the antipsychotic dose to suppress TD movements - while higher doses may temporarily mask TD by increasing D2 blockade, they worsen the underlying receptor supersensitivity and guarantee worse TD when the medication is eventually reduced

The AIMS examination must be performed at baseline before starting antipsychotics, then every 6 months during treatment - it systematically scores involuntary movements in 7 body regions (face, lips, jaw, tongue, upper extremities, lower extremities, trunk) on a 0-4 severity scale

newborn-assessment.title: Évaluation du nouveau-né
newborn-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation du nouveau-né, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Palmar grasp reflex: infant grips finger placed in palm: disappears by 3-4 months
Acrocyanosis (blue hands/feet) is normal in first 24-48 hours; central cyanosis is NOT normal
Head circumference (33-35cm) should be measured and compared to chest circumference (30.5-33cm)

neonatal-thermoreg.title: Thermorégulation néonatale
neonatal-thermoreg.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Thermorégulation néonatale, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Premature birth (large body surface area to mass ratio)
clinicalPearls: Neutral thermal environment (NTE): ambient temperature where the neonate expends the least energy to maintain normal body temp
Skin-to-skin contact (kangaroo care) is the most effective warming intervention for stable newborns
Dry the newborn immediately after birth and place a cap on the head: the head is the largest heat-loss surface

neonatal-feeding.title: Alimentation néonatale
neonatal-feeding.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Alimentation néonatale, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Feed on demand, not on a rigid schedule: typically every 2-3 hours for breastfed, every 3-4 hours for formula-fed
Burp infant after every 1-2 oz (formula) or when switching breasts to prevent regurgitation
Weight gain of approximately 15-30g (0.5-1 oz) per day is expected after initial loss

neonatal-jaundice-basics.title: Icterícia neonatal
neonatal-jaundice-basics.overview: Icterícia neonatal é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Jaundice in the first 24 hours is ALWAYS pathological: notify provider immediately
Risk factors: ABO incompatibility, Rh incompatibility, breastfeeding jaundice (insufficient intake), prematurity, bruising from birth trauma
Press on the skin over a bony prominence (forehead, sternum) and observe the underlying color for blanch jaundice assessment

neonatal-respiratory-distress.title: Détresse respiratoire néonatale
neonatal-respiratory-distress.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Détresse respiratoire néonatale, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Position prone or side-lying to optimize ventilation and oxygenation
Minimal stimulation and clustered care to reduce oxygen consumption
Monitor ABGs and pulse oximetry: target SpO2 88-95% in preterm infants to avoid retinopathy of prematurity
Prenatal betamethasone to the mother is the BEST prevention if preterm delivery is anticipated

neonatal-sepsis.title: Sepsis néonatal
neonatal-sepsis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sepsis néonatal, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS obtain blood culture BEFORE starting antibiotics: but do NOT delay treatment waiting for results
GBS prophylaxis (intrapartum penicillin to GBS-positive mothers) has dramatically reduced early-onset sepsis
Hypothermia is a more common sign of neonatal sepsis than fever: do not dismiss low temperatures
CBC with differential: look for leukopenia, bandemia (left shift), and thrombocytopenia as sepsis markers

hyperbilirubinemia.title: Hyperbilirubinémie
hyperbilirubinemia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hyperbilirubinémie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS protect eyes during phototherapy with opaque eye shields: check positioning frequently
Monitor for bronze baby syndrome (grayish-brown skin discoloration) which occurs with conjugated hyperbilirubinemia under phototherapy
Increase feeding frequency (every 2-3 hours) to promote bilirubin excretion through stool
Jaundice within the first 24 hours is ALWAYS pathological: never dismiss as physiological

nec-necrotizing.title: Entérocolite nécrosante
nec-necrotizing.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Entérocolite nécrosante, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Breast milk is the single most PROTECTIVE factor against NEC: encourage maternal breast milk for all preterm infants
Bell Staging: Stage I (suspected: nonspecific signs), Stage II (definite: pneumatosis), Stage III (advanced: perforation, shock)
Surgical consult is required for pneumoperitoneum (free air) indicating bowel perforation
Serial abdominal X-rays and girth measurements are essential for monitoring progression

necrotizing-enterocolitis-basics-rpn.title: Entérocolite nécrosante – RPN
necrotizing-enterocolitis-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Entérocolite nécrosante – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Breast milk is the best protection against NEC: always encourage and support breast milk use for preterm infants
Increasing abdominal girth + feeding intolerance + bloody stools = report immediately (classic NEC triad)
Measure abdominal girth consistently at the same anatomical landmark each time
Gentle abdominal handling is critical: avoid unnecessary pressure on a distended abdomen

meconium-aspiration-basics-rpn.title: Aspiration méconiale – RPN
meconium-aspiration-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Aspiration méconiale – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Green or yellow-green amniotic fluid means meconium is present: alert the team immediately
Thick meconium carries a higher aspiration risk than thin meconium
Sudden respiratory deterioration in a MAS baby may mean pneumothorax: report immediately
Minimal handling and a quiet environment help reduce the infant's oxygen demand
Post-term infants are at highest risk for meconium aspiration

meconium-ileus-rpn.title: Iléus méconial – RPN
meconium-ileus-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Iléus méconial – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to pass meconium within 24-48 hours
clinicalPearls: Failure to pass meconium within 24-48 hours is a red flag: report immediately
Meconium ileus is strongly linked to cystic fibrosis: sweat chloride testing will likely be ordered
Bilious (green) vomiting in a newborn always indicates possible bowel obstruction
Monitor hydration carefully if Gastrografin enema is given: it causes significant fluid shifts

meconium-ileus.title: Iléus méconial
meconium-ileus.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Iléus méconial, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autosomal recessive inheritance pattern for CF
Known CFTR gene mutations in family
clinicalPearls: Meconium ileus is the presenting sign of CF in approximately 15-20% of CF patients
Simple vs complex classification determines management: simple may respond to enema therapy, complex requires surgery
Ground-glass appearance (Neuhauser sign) on X-ray represents air mixed with thick meconium in the terminal ileum
Microcolon indicates the colon has never been used (meconium never reached it)
Aggressive hydration is critical during Gastrografin enema: the hyperosmolar solution can cause dangerous fluid shifts
All infants with meconium ileus require CF workup regardless of newborn screening results

meconium-ileus-np.title: Iléus méconial – IPA
meconium-ileus-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Iléus méconial – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Homozygous or compound heterozygous CFTR mutations (especially Class I-III)
Family history of CF or meconium ileus
Modifier genes affecting meconium ileus susceptibility (e.g., CFM1 locus on chromosome 19q13)
clinicalPearls: F508del is the most common CFTR mutation worldwide: present in ~70% of CF alleles, with Class II dysfunction (protein misfolding and degradation)
Simple vs complex classification is the primary determinant of surgical vs non-surgical management
Neuhauser sign (ground-glass RLQ opacity) reflects air trapped within thick meconium: pathognomonic for meconium ileus
Peritoneal calcifications indicate prenatal perforation with sterile meconium peritonitis
All infants with meconium ileus require comprehensive CF evaluation regardless of newborn screening results: false negatives occur
Distal intestinal obstruction syndrome (DIOS) is the older-child equivalent of meconium ileus and may recur throughout life
CFTR modulator therapy has revolutionized CF care: genotype-directed therapy should be planned from diagnosis
Genetic counseling is essential: 25% recurrence risk with each pregnancy for carrier parents

neuro-basics.title: Bases en neurologie
neuro-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bases en neurologie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Sudden LOC change = priority
Find the cause (UTI, Hypoxia, Glucose)

stroke.title: Acidente vascular cerebral
stroke.overview: Acidente vascular cerebral é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NPO until swallow screen
CT Scan (non-contrast) first

cp-management.title: Paralysie cérébrale
cp-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Paralysie cérébrale, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Physical therapy and ROM are daily priorities
Monitor for skin breakdown under assistive devices
Higher risk for UTIs and Constipation

increased-icp.title: Pression intracrânienne élevée
increased-icp.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pression intracrânienne élevée, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Avoid coughing, straining, or extreme hip flexion
Keep HOB at 30 degrees (Neutral head position)
GCS < 8 = Intubate

stroke-advanced.title: AVC avançado
stroke-advanced.overview: AVC avançado é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Atrial fibrillation (highest risk for embolic stroke)
clinicalPearls: CT scan (non-contrast) is the priority to rule out bleed
NPO until swallow screen is passed
Monitor for increased ICP post-stroke

seizure-safety.title: Segurança em convulsões
seizure-safety.overview: Segurança em convulsões é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Nothing in the mouth during a seizure
Turn client to the side to prevent aspiration
Time the seizure and note the characteristics

neuritis-neuropathy.title: Névrite et neuropathie
neuritis-neuropathy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Névrite et neuropathie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Diabetic foot care is the single most important nursing intervention for diabetic neuropathy: Inspect feet DAILY (patient may not feel injuries), never walk barefoot, properly fitted shoes (no pressure points), trim nails straight across, check bath water temperature with elbow (not feet: cannot sense hot water), report any break in skin integrity immediately. Loss of protective sensation + poor circulation = high amputation risk.

Vitamin B12 deficiency neuropathy is REVERSIBLE if caught early. It presents with paresthesias, loss of proprioception and vibratory sense (posterior column involvement), and megaloblastic anemia. However, if left untreated for >6 months, nerve damage becomes permanent (subacute combined degeneration of the spinal cord). Common in: metformin users, strict vegans, pernicious anemia, chronic PPI use, post-gastric bypass.

Nerve conduction studies (NCS) and electromyography (EMG) are the gold standard for diagnosing and characterizing peripheral neuropathy. NCS measures conduction velocity (slowed in demyelinating neuropathy) and amplitude (reduced in axonal neuropathy). EMG detects denervation potentials (fibrillations, positive sharp waves) in affected muscles.

Guillain-Barré syndrome is an ASCENDING demyelinating polyneuropathy (starts in feet, progresses upward): respiratory failure occurs when it reaches the diaphragm. Monitor forced vital capacity (FVC): intubate if FVC <20 mL/kg or declining rapidly. Treated with IVIG or plasmapheresis.

Postherpetic neuralgia (PHN) occurs in ~20% of patients after shingles (herpes zoster), especially elderly patients. The varicella-zoster virus damages sensory nerve fibers during reactivation, leaving persistent neuropathic pain in the dermatomal distribution that can last months to years. Prevention: Shingrix vaccine (recombinant, 2-dose series) recommended for adults >50: >90% effective at preventing shingles and PHN.

febrile-seizure.title: Convulsion fébrile
febrile-seizure.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Convulsion fébrile, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age 6 months to 5 years (peak incidence 12-18 months)
Family history of febrile seizures (genetic predisposition)
clinicalPearls: Simple febrile seizures are BENIGN - they do NOT cause brain damage, do NOT increase epilepsy risk significantly, and do NOT require long-term anticonvulsant therapy
Antipyretics (acetaminophen, ibuprofen) treat fever but do NOT prevent febrile seizures - the seizure occurs with the RATE of temperature rise, often before parents even realize the child is febrile
Teach parents: during a seizure, place the child on their side (recovery position), do NOT put anything in the mouth, time the seizure, and call 911 if it lasts longer than 5 minutes

parkinsons-disease.title: Parkinson's Disease
parkinsons-disease.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Parkinson's Disease, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Male sex (1.5x higher risk than females)
Rural living and well water consumption
LRRK2, SNCA, PINK1, or Parkin gene mutations
clinicalPearls: Medication timing is critical: even 30 minutes late can cause 'off' episodes
Falls are the leading cause of injury: assess gait and balance every visit
Levodopa should be taken on an empty stomach for best absorption
Monitor for hallucinations (visual most common) especially in elderly patients

multiple-sclerosis.title: Esclerose múltipla
multiple-sclerosis.overview: Esclerose múltipla é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Uhthoff's phenomenon: symptoms worsen with heat but this is NOT a relapse
Lhermitte's sign is highly suggestive of cervical cord demyelination
Fatigue is the most disabling symptom for many patients: teach energy conservation
UTI and other infections can trigger pseudo-relapses: treat the infection first

delirium-vs-dementia.title: Délire vs démence
delirium-vs-dementia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Délire vs démence, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Advanced age (strongest risk factor for both)
Pre-existing dementia (strongest risk factor for delirium)
Acute infection (UTI most common cause of delirium in elderly)
clinicalPearls: Delirium is a medical EMERGENCY: always look for the underlying cause
The #1 cause of acute confusion in elderly hospitalized patients is delirium, NOT dementia progression
Hypoactive delirium is frequently MISSED: screen all elderly patients
Never use physical restraints for delirium: they make it worse
Delirium superimposed on dementia is very common and has the worst outcomes

betamethasone-dexamethasone.title: Antenatal Corticosteroids (Betamethasone & Dexamethasone)
betamethasone-dexamethasone.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Antenatal Corticosteroids (Betamethasone & Dexamethasone), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Placenta previa with anticipated preterm delivery
Multiple gestation with threatened preterm birth
clinicalPearls: A single rescue course may be given if ≥14 days have passed since the initial course and delivery remains imminent before 34 weeks
Corticosteroids cause a transient decrease in fetal movement and heart rate variability for 24-48 hours: this is expected and should not be confused with fetal distress
Late preterm steroid administration (34-36+6 weeks) may be considered but carries a higher risk of neonatal hypoglycemia
Betamethasone is generally preferred over dexamethasone due to its longer half-life and simpler dosing schedule

opioid-analgesics-ob.title: Opioid Analgesics in Labor
opioid-analgesics-ob.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Opioid Analgesics in Labor, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Preterm labor (increased neonatal sensitivity to respiratory depression)
Anticipated precipitous delivery (risk of administering too close to birth)
Maternal hepatic or renal impairment (altered drug metabolism)
Polyhydramnios or fetal compromise (reduced fetal drug tolerance)
Maternal use of other CNS depressants
clinicalPearls: Time opioid administration carefully: avoid giving within 1-2 hours of anticipated delivery to reduce neonatal respiratory depression risk
Butorphanol may cause a transient sinusoidal-like FHR pattern that should not be confused with true sinusoidal pattern indicating fetal anemia
Naloxone reverses opioid effects in the neonate but has a shorter half-life than most opioids: monitor for re-sedation
Mixed agonist-antagonists (butorphanol, nalbuphine) should never be given to opioid-dependent clients as they precipitate withdrawal

prostaglandins-ob.title: Prostaglandins for Cervical Ripening
prostaglandins-ob.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prostaglandins for Cervical Ripening, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Previous cesarean section or uterine surgery (contraindication for misoprostol)
Grand multiparity (increased uterine sensitivity to prostaglandins)
Non-reassuring fetal heart rate pattern prior to administration
History of asthma (prostaglandins may trigger bronchospasm)
Placenta previa or unexplained vaginal bleeding
clinicalPearls: Bishop score <6 indicates an unfavorable cervix that will benefit from prostaglandin ripening before oxytocin induction
Misoprostol is CONTRAINDICATED in clients with a prior uterine scar: always verify surgical history before administration
Cervidil can be removed if tachysystole develops; misoprostol cannot be retrieved once administered: this is a key safety distinction
Allow at least 4 hours after misoprostol or 30 minutes after Cervidil removal before initiating oxytocin infusion

oxytocin-ob.title: Oxytocin (Pitocin) for Labor Induction & Augmentation
oxytocin-ob.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oxytocin (Pitocin) for Labor Induction & Augmentation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Grand multiparity (uterus more sensitive to oxytocin, higher rupture risk)
Prior cesarean section or uterine surgery
Non-reassuring fetal status prior to induction
clinicalPearls: Oxytocin must ALWAYS be administered via infusion pump as a secondary (piggyback) line: never bolused during labor
The short half-life (3-5 minutes) is a safety advantage: discontinue the infusion and effects resolve quickly
Water intoxication risk increases with prolonged infusion and hypotonic IV fluids: use isotonic solutions and monitor I&O
Postpartum: oxytocin is the first-line uterotonic for preventing and treating postpartum hemorrhage

pph-medications.title: Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH) Medications
pph-medications.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Postpartum Hemorrhage (PPH) Medications, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Uterine atony (most common cause: overdistended uterus, prolonged labor, chorioamnionitis)
Coagulation disorders (DIC, von Willebrand disease, thrombocytopenia)
Use of uterine relaxants (magnesium sulfate, general anesthesia)
clinicalPearls: Remember the PPH medication contraindications: Methergine = hypertension, Hemabate = asthma (mnemonic: 'M for Methergine, M for elevated BP'; 'H for Hemabate, H for hard to breathe')
Oxytocin is always the FIRST-LINE treatment for PPH due to uterine atony: escalate to other uterotonics only when it is ineffective
Tranexamic acid (TXA) should be administered within 3 hours of hemorrhage onset for maximum benefit: do not delay
The 4 T's of PPH etiology: Tone (atony), Tissue (retained placenta), Trauma (lacerations), Thrombin (coagulopathy)

rh-immune-globulin.title: Rh Immune Globulin (RhoGAM)
rh-immune-globulin.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rh Immune Globulin (RhoGAM), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Previous pregnancy with Rh-positive fetus without adequate RhIG prophylaxis
Amniocentesis, CVS, or other invasive prenatal procedures
Vaginal bleeding during pregnancy (threatened abortion, placental abruption)
clinicalPearls: RhoGAM is given at 28 weeks antepartum AND within 72 hours postpartum if the newborn is Rh-positive: both doses are essential
RhIG is a blood product: follow institutional blood product administration protocols including dual verification
If the mother is already sensitized (positive indirect Coombs), RhIG will NOT be effective: it can only prevent sensitization, not reverse it
A standard 300 mcg dose covers approximately 30 mL of fetal whole blood: Kleihauer-Betke testing determines if additional doses are needed for large hemorrhages

rubella-vaccine.title: Rubella Vaccine (MMR) Postpartum
rubella-vaccine.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rubella Vaccine (MMR) Postpartum, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Non-immune rubella status identified during prenatal screening (titer <1:8)
Incomplete or unknown childhood immunization history
Immigration from countries without universal rubella vaccination programs
Exposure to rubella during pregnancy (cannot vaccinate until postpartum)
Close contact with young children who may transmit rubella
First trimester exposure carries highest teratogenic risk for CRS
clinicalPearls: MMR is a LIVE vaccine: absolutely contraindicated during pregnancy; administer only in the postpartum period
Instruct client to avoid pregnancy for at least 28 days after MMR vaccination
Breastfeeding is safe and NOT a contraindication to MMR administration

If RhoGAM and MMR are both needed postpartum, they can be given at the same time but at different sites: however, rubella titer should be rechecked in 3 months as the blood product may interfere with seroconversion

lung-surfactants.title: Exogenous Lung Surfactants for Neonates
lung-surfactants.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Exogenous Lung Surfactants for Neonates, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prematurity (<34 weeks gestation): primary risk factor for RDS
Low birth weight (<2500 g), very low birth weight (<1500 g)
Male sex (delayed lung maturity compared to females)
Cesarean birth without labor (lack of catecholamine surge that promotes fluid clearance)
White race (higher incidence than other races)
clinicalPearls: Do NOT suction the ET tube for 1-2 hours after surfactant administration: suctioning removes surfactant from the airways and reduces effectiveness
Rapid improvement in oxygenation after surfactant is expected: adjust ventilator settings promptly to prevent hyperoxia and air leak
Natural (animal-derived) surfactants are generally preferred over older synthetic formulations due to the presence of surfactant proteins
INSURE technique (INtubate-SURfactant-Extubate) allows surfactant delivery with minimal ventilator time in stable preterm neonates

eye-prophylaxis-newborn.title: Erythromycin Eye Prophylaxis for Newborns
eye-prophylaxis-newborn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Erythromycin Eye Prophylaxis for Newborns, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Maternal gonorrhea or chlamydia infection (known or unknown)
Lack of prenatal care or STI screening
Vaginal delivery through an infected birth canal
Premature rupture of membranes with prolonged exposure
History of sexually transmitted infections in the mother
clinicalPearls: Erythromycin eye prophylaxis is legally mandated in most jurisdictions for ALL newborns: do not omit even for cesarean births
May delay administration up to 1 hour to allow initial bonding and breastfeeding, but do not exceed the time window
Do NOT wipe or irrigate ointment from the eyes: it must remain to be effective
Parents should be reassured that the temporary blurriness from the ointment does not affect the newborn's long-term vision

phytonadione.title: Phytonadione (Vitamin K) for Newborns
phytonadione.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Phytonadione (Vitamin K) for Newborns, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: All newborns are at risk (universal prophylaxis is standard of care)
Exclusively breastfed infants (breast milk is low in vitamin K compared to formula)
Maternal use of anticonvulsants (phenytoin, carbamazepine, phenobarbital) that interfere with vitamin K metabolism
Maternal use of warfarin or other anticoagulants during pregnancy
Prematurity (lower hepatic stores of vitamin K-dependent factors)
Hepatic disease or biliary atresia in the neonate (impaired vitamin K absorption)
Delayed or refused vitamin K prophylaxis (late VKDB risk increases significantly)
clinicalPearls: Vitamin K is a universal newborn prophylaxis: ALL neonates should receive it within the first hour of life regardless of feeding method
IM route is superior to oral route for preventing late VKDB: always advocate for IM administration
Late VKDB (2-12 weeks) often presents as sudden intracranial hemorrhage with devastating neurological consequences: most cases occur in infants who did not receive IM vitamin K
Do NOT massage the injection site after administration: this increases bruising risk in the vitamin K-deficient neonate

hep-b-vaccine-newborn.title: Hepatitis B Vaccine & HBIG for Newborns
hep-b-vaccine-newborn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hepatitis B Vaccine & HBIG for Newborns, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Mother is HBsAg-positive (hepatitis B surface antigen positive)
Maternal HBsAg status unknown at time of delivery
Mother with acute hepatitis B infection during pregnancy
Mother from endemic region with high HBV prevalence
Mother who did not receive prenatal care or HBV screening
Mother who is HBeAg-positive (high viral load, increased transmission risk)
clinicalPearls: The 12-hour window is CRITICAL: both HBIG and first vaccine dose must be given within 12 hours of birth for infants of HBsAg-positive mothers
HBIG and hepatitis B vaccine are given at DIFFERENT anatomical sites (e.g., right and left vastus lateralis): never mix in the same syringe
The hepatitis B vaccine is NOT a live vaccine: it is safe for all newborns including immunocompromised infants
Post-vaccination testing (HBsAg and anti-HBs) at 9-12 months confirms whether the infant is protected or was infected despite prophylaxis

contraceptives.title: Contraceptive Methods
contraceptives.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Contraceptive Methods, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of deep vein thrombosis (DVT) or pulmonary embolism (PE): contraindication for estrogen-containing methods
Smoking and age >35 years (increased thromboembolic risk with estrogen)
Migraine with aura (increased stroke risk with combined hormonal contraceptives)
Active liver disease or hepatic tumors
Breastfeeding within the first 21 days postpartum (estrogen suppresses lactation)
History of stroke or coronary artery disease
clinicalPearls: ACHES mnemonic for combined hormonal contraceptive danger signs: Abdominal pain, Chest pain, Headaches (severe), Eye problems, Severe leg pain: seek immediate care
Progestin-only methods are safe for breastfeeding clients and those with estrogen contraindications (DVT history, migraine with aura, smokers >35)
The copper IUD (Paragard) is the most effective form of emergency contraception when inserted within 5 days of unprotected intercourse
Depo-Provera may cause bone density loss with use >2 years: recommend calcium/vitamin D supplementation and periodic reassessment

hodgkin-lymphoma.title: Hodgkin Lymphoma
hodgkin-lymphoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hodgkin Lymphoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of Hodgkin lymphoma in first-degree relatives
Higher socioeconomic status (correlates with delayed EBV exposure)
clinicalPearls: Reed-Sternberg cells are the hallmark diagnostic finding - often described as owl-eye cells on biopsy
Hodgkin lymphoma spreads contiguously (node to adjacent node), unlike non-Hodgkin lymphoma which spreads non-contiguously
Alcohol-induced pain at the site of involved lymph nodes is nearly pathognomonic for Hodgkin lymphoma

non-hodgkin-lymphoma.title: Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma
non-hodgkin-lymphoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autoimmune disorders (Sjogren syndrome, rheumatoid arthritis, celiac disease)
Chronic infections (H. pylori, hepatitis C, EBV, HTLV-1)
Exposure to pesticides, herbicides, or organic solvents
clinicalPearls: NHL spreads non-contiguously (skips lymph node groups), unlike Hodgkin lymphoma which spreads in an orderly, contiguous pattern
Burkitt lymphoma is one of the fastest-growing human malignancies and has a strong association with EBV and the t(8;14) translocation
Tumor lysis syndrome risk is highest in aggressive, bulky NHL - ensure allopurinol or rasburicase and aggressive IV hydration are initiated before chemotherapy

multiple-myeloma.title: Multiple Myeloma
multiple-myeloma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Multiple Myeloma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age over 65 years (median age at diagnosis is 69)
Prior monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance (MGUS)
Family history of multiple myeloma or MGUS
Occupational exposure to petroleum products, pesticides, or radiation
clinicalPearls: Remember CRAB for multiple myeloma: Calcium elevation, Renal insufficiency, Anemia, Bone lesions - presence of any one defines symptomatic disease requiring treatment
Bence Jones proteinuria is caused by excess immunoglobulin light chains and is toxic to renal tubules - aggressive hydration is critical to prevent renal failure
Pathologic fractures can occur with minimal stress - avoid using a blood pressure cuff on an arm with known lytic lesions and implement strict fall precautions

breast-cancer.title: Breast Cancer
breast-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Breast Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: BRCA1 or BRCA2 gene mutations (lifetime risk 45-72%)
First-degree family history of breast cancer
Early menarche or late menopause (prolonged estrogen exposure)
Nulliparity or first pregnancy after age 30
Obesity in postmenopausal women (adipose aromatase converts androgens to estrogen)
clinicalPearls: The most important prognostic factors are lymph node status, tumor size, and receptor status - node-negative, ER-positive, HER2-negative tumors have the best prognosis
Inflammatory breast cancer is a clinical diagnosis (erythema, peau d'orange, rapid onset) and requires neoadjuvant chemotherapy before surgery
Sentinel lymph node biopsy has replaced full axillary dissection for clinically node-negative patients, significantly reducing lymphedema risk
Tamoxifen reduces breast cancer recurrence by approximately 50% in ER-positive tumors but carries endometrial cancer risk - annual gynecologic screening is essential

mastectomy.title: Mastectomy Post-Operative Care
mastectomy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Mastectomy Post-Operative Care, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Axillary lymph node dissection (primary risk for lymphedema)
Radiation therapy to the axilla or chest wall
Wound infection or seroma formation postoperatively
Extensive surgical dissection and tissue removal
Sedentary lifestyle with limited arm mobility postoperatively
Cellulitis or injury to the ipsilateral arm
clinicalPearls: Lymphedema can develop months to years after surgery - patient education about lifelong prevention strategies is critical at every follow-up visit
Never use the operative side for blood draws, IV access, blood pressure, or injections - apply a medical alert band to the affected arm
Jackson-Pratt drains are typically removed when output drops below 30 mL in 24 hours - teach the patient to empty and record drain output at home

cervical-cancer.title: Cervical Cancer
cervical-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cervical Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Long-term oral contraceptive use (greater than 5 years)
clinicalPearls: Cervical cancer is the most preventable gynecologic cancer through HPV vaccination and Pap screening
Postcoital bleeding is a red flag symptom that warrants evaluation
HPV vaccine is most effective when given before sexual debut

prostate-cancer.title: Prostate Cancer
prostate-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prostate Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: African American descent (highest incidence and mortality)
Family history of prostate cancer (first-degree relative)
clinicalPearls: Prostate cancer screening is a shared decision between patient and provider, considering risks and benefits
Bone metastases from prostate cancer are typically osteoblastic (sclerotic), unlike most other cancers
ADT increases risk of osteoporosis, metabolic syndrome, and cardiovascular disease, requiring ongoing monitoring

kaposi-sarcoma.title: Kaposi Sarcoma
kaposi-sarcoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Kaposi Sarcoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HIV/AIDS with CD4 count below 200 cells per microliter
Mediterranean, Middle Eastern, or sub-Saharan African descent
Men who have sex with men (higher HHV-8 seroprevalence)
clinicalPearls: AIDS-related KS treatment priority is immune reconstitution through effective ART - many lesions regress with CD4 count recovery alone
Purple or violaceous non-blanching skin lesions in an immunosuppressed patient should raise immediate suspicion for Kaposi sarcoma
Hard palate lesions are the most common oral manifestation - examine the oral cavity thoroughly in all HIV-positive patients

merkel-cell-carcinoma.title: Merkel Cell Carcinoma
merkel-cell-carcinoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Merkel Cell Carcinoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age over 50 years (median age at diagnosis is 75-80)
Chronic UV radiation exposure and fair skin
Immunosuppression (organ transplant, HIV/AIDS, chronic lymphocytic leukemia)
History of other UV-related skin cancers
clinicalPearls: AEIOU mnemonic for MCC: Asymptomatic, Expanding rapidly, Immunosuppression, Older than 50, UV-exposed site - if a nodule fits these criteria, biopsy promptly
CK20-positive perinuclear dot pattern on immunohistochemistry is the hallmark diagnostic finding that distinguishes MCC from other small round blue cell tumors
MCC has a higher mortality rate than melanoma stage-for-stage - early detection and aggressive multimodal treatment are critical

neuroblastoma.title: Neuroblastoma
neuroblastoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Neuroblastoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age under 5 years (90% diagnosed before age 5, peak at 1-2 years)
Familial neuroblastoma (rare, autosomal dominant ALK mutations)
clinicalPearls: Neuroblastoma classically presents as an abdominal mass that crosses the midline (unlike Wilms tumor which does not cross the midline) - this distinction is high-yield for exams
Periorbital ecchymoses (raccoon eyes) in a young child without trauma history should raise suspicion for neuroblastoma with orbital metastasis
Stage 4S neuroblastoma in infants under 12 months may spontaneously regress without treatment - this unique biologic behavior is not seen in older children

retinoblastoma.title: Retinoblastoma
retinoblastoma.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Retinoblastoma, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of retinoblastoma (autosomal dominant inheritance with high penetrance)
Germline RB1 gene mutation (present in all hereditary cases)
Young age (90% diagnosed before age 3, average 18 months)
Prior retinoblastoma in one eye (risk for bilateral disease in hereditary form)
Survivors of hereditary retinoblastoma at increased risk for second primary cancers
clinicalPearls: Leukocoria (white pupillary reflex) is the hallmark presenting sign - any child with an abnormal or absent red reflex requires urgent ophthalmologic referral
Retinoblastoma follows the Knudson two-hit hypothesis: hereditary cases need only one somatic hit (bilateral, early onset) while sporadic cases need two hits (unilateral, later onset)
Hereditary retinoblastoma survivors have a significantly increased lifetime risk of second primary cancers (osteosarcoma, soft tissue sarcoma) - lifelong cancer surveillance is mandatory

radiation-therapy.title: Radiation Therapy Nursing Care
radiation-therapy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Radiation Therapy Nursing Care, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Poor nutritional status before treatment initiation
Concurrent chemotherapy (radiosensitizing agents increase tissue damage)
Large treatment field size and high total radiation dose
Treatment to areas with overlapping skin folds (axilla, groin, under breast)
Obesity (skin folds create friction and moisture trapping)
clinicalPearls: Never remove radiation markings from the skin - these guide the linear accelerator to deliver radiation precisely to the tumor and protect surrounding tissues
Skin reactions from radiation therapy follow a predictable progression: erythema then dry desquamation then moist desquamation - early intervention at each stage prevents worsening
Radiation fatigue is cumulative and typically peaks 1-2 weeks after treatment completion before gradually improving - prepare patients for this timeline

tumor-classification.title: Tumor Classification and Nomenclature
tumor-classification.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tumor Classification and Nomenclature, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of hereditary cancer syndromes (Li-Fraumeni, Lynch, BRCA)
Environmental carcinogen exposure (tobacco, asbestos, radiation, UV light)
Chronic inflammation and tissue injury (Barrett esophagus, ulcerative colitis)
Viral infections (HPV, EBV, HBV, HCV, HHV-8)
Advancing age (cumulative genetic mutations over time)
Obesity (associated with endometrial, breast, colon, and other cancers)
clinicalPearls: Carcinomas arise from epithelial tissue (most common malignancy type), sarcomas arise from mesenchymal (connective) tissue - this naming convention is essential for classification
TNM staging describes anatomic disease extent (T=tumor, N=nodes, M=metastasis) while grading describes cellular differentiation (G1=well differentiated to G4=undifferentiated)
Staging is determined at diagnosis and does not change even if the disease progresses - restaging uses different terminology (recurrent, progressive, or refractory disease)

paraneoplastic-syndromes.title: Paraneoplastic Syndromes
paraneoplastic-syndromes.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Paraneoplastic Syndromes, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Small cell lung cancer (most common cause of endocrine paraneoplastic syndromes)
Squamous cell carcinoma of the lung (PTHrP-mediated hypercalcemia)
Thymoma (associated with myasthenia gravis and pure red cell aplasia)
clinicalPearls: Paraneoplastic syndromes may present before the cancer is diagnosed - new-onset SIADH, hypercalcemia, or unexplained neurologic symptoms in an adult should prompt malignancy workup
Small cell lung cancer is the most common cause of ectopic ACTH (Cushing) and ectopic ADH (SIADH), while squamous cell lung cancer is the most common cause of PTHrP-mediated hypercalcemia
Lambert-Eaton myasthenic syndrome differs from myasthenia gravis in that weakness improves with repeated muscle use (the opposite pattern) and is strongly associated with small cell lung cancer

treatment-of-cancer.title: Treatment of Cancer
treatment-of-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Treatment of Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Pre-existing organ dysfunction (renal, hepatic, cardiac) increasing treatment toxicity risk
Poor nutritional status and cachexia before treatment
Prior chemotherapy or radiation exposure (cumulative toxicity)
Bone marrow reserve depletion from disease infiltration
Genetic polymorphisms affecting drug metabolism (DPD deficiency for 5-FU)
clinicalPearls: The nadir (lowest blood count) occurs 7-14 days after chemotherapy - this is when infection risk peaks and patients must practice strict neutropenic precautions (no raw foods, no crowds, no rectal temperatures)

Vesicant chemotherapy agents (doxorubicin, vincristine, vinblastine) cause tissue necrosis if extravasated - stop infusion immediately, aspirate residual drug, and administer specific antidote per protocol
Tumor lysis syndrome is an oncologic emergency - ensure adequate hydration and allopurinol or rasburicase before initiating treatment for highly proliferative tumors

esophageal-cancer.title: Esophageal Cancer
esophageal-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Esophageal Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Tobacco use and heavy alcohol consumption (synergistic for squamous cell type)
Obesity (increases GERD risk and promotes adenocarcinoma)
Hot beverage consumption (thermal injury to esophageal mucosa)
Plummer-Vinson syndrome (iron deficiency with esophageal webs)
clinicalPearls: Progressive dysphagia (solids to liquids) is the hallmark symptom and usually indicates the tumor already obstructs more than 60% of the esophageal lumen - early-stage disease is often asymptomatic
Barrett esophagus is the precursor to esophageal adenocarcinoma - patients with GERD symptoms lasting more than 5 years should be considered for endoscopic screening
After esophagectomy, the gastric conduit has no sphincter mechanism - patients must remain upright after eating and sleep with the head of bed elevated to prevent reflux and aspiration

stomach-cancer.title: Stomach Cancer
stomach-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Stomach Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Helicobacter pylori infection (strongest modifiable risk factor)
Chronic atrophic gastritis and intestinal metaplasia
Diet high in smoked, salted, or preserved foods and low in fruits and vegetables
Family history of gastric cancer or hereditary diffuse gastric cancer (CDH1 mutation)
Blood type A (slightly increased risk for diffuse type)
Prior partial gastrectomy (gastric stump cancer after 15-20 years)
clinicalPearls: Alarm symptoms requiring urgent EGD: unintentional weight loss, dysphagia, persistent vomiting, GI bleeding, iron deficiency anemia, or new epigastric mass in a patient over 55
Virchow node (left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy) is a classic sign of metastatic gastric cancer and should prompt abdominal imaging and endoscopy
After total gastrectomy, patients require lifelong B12 injections because intrinsic factor (produced by gastric parietal cells) is essential for B12 absorption in the terminal ileum

colorectal-cancer.title: Colorectal Cancer
colorectal-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Colorectal Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age over 45 years (average-risk screening begins at 45)
Personal history of adenomatous polyps or previous CRC
First-degree relative with CRC (doubles lifetime risk)
Lynch syndrome (HNPCC) or familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP)
Inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis or Crohn colitis) of 8+ years duration
Western diet high in red and processed meats, low in fiber
Obesity, physical inactivity, and heavy alcohol consumption
Type 2 diabetes mellitus and insulin resistance
clinicalPearls: Right-sided colon cancers present with iron deficiency anemia and occult bleeding (stool is still liquid in the right colon), while left-sided cancers present with obstruction and visible rectal bleeding (stool is formed in the left colon)
CEA is not useful for screening (too many false positives) but is essential for monitoring treatment response and detecting recurrence after curative resection
The adenoma-to-carcinoma sequence takes 10-15 years - regular colonoscopy with polypectomy effectively prevents CRC by removing precancerous polyps before malignant transformation

bladder-tumors.title: Bladder Tumors
bladder-tumors.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bladder Tumors, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cigarette smoking (strongest risk factor, accounts for 50% of cases)
Occupational exposure to aromatic amines (rubber, dye, textile, leather industries)
Chronic bladder inflammation (recurrent UTIs, indwelling catheters, Schistosoma haematobium)
Male sex (3-4 times more common in men)
clinicalPearls: Painless gross hematuria in any adult requires urologic evaluation with cystoscopy to rule out bladder cancer - never assume it is caused by a UTI without proper workup

BCG immunotherapy is the most effective intravesical treatment for high-risk non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer - it is a live vaccine and must never be given to immunosuppressed patients or with traumatic catheterization
Bladder cancer has the highest recurrence rate of any solid tumor - lifelong cystoscopic surveillance is required, typically every 3 months for the first 2 years then at increasing intervals

kidney-tumors.title: Kidney Tumors
kidney-tumors.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Kidney Tumors, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Obesity (excess body fat linked to hormonal and metabolic changes)
Von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) syndrome (autosomal dominant, bilateral RCC)
Acquired cystic kidney disease (patients on long-term dialysis)
Family history of renal cell carcinoma
Occupational exposure to cadmium, asbestos, or trichloroethylene
clinicalPearls: RCC is known as the internist's tumor because of its diverse paraneoplastic manifestations - polycythemia, hypercalcemia, hypertension, and Stauffer syndrome can all be the presenting feature
A new left-sided varicocele in a male that does not reduce when supine should raise suspicion for left renal vein obstruction by a renal mass or tumor thrombus
RCC is resistant to traditional chemotherapy and radiation - targeted therapies (VEGF inhibitors) and immune checkpoint inhibitors are the mainstays of systemic treatment for advanced disease

penile-cancer.title: Penile Cancer
penile-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Penile Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Poor genital hygiene with smegma accumulation
clinicalPearls: HPV vaccination is the most effective primary prevention for HPV-related penile cancer - the 9-valent vaccine covers HPV 16 and 18 responsible for the majority of HPV-related penile cancers
Any persistent penile lesion that does not resolve within 4 weeks requires biopsy - delayed diagnosis is common because patients delay seeking care due to embarrassment
Neonatal circumcision reduces penile cancer risk by 3-fold by eliminating phimosis, chronic balanitis, and smegma retention - but adult circumcision does not confer the same protective benefit

male-breast-cancer.title: Male Breast Cancer
male-breast-cancer.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Male Breast Cancer, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: BRCA2 gene mutation (strongest genetic risk factor for male breast cancer)
Age over 60 years (median age at diagnosis is 67)
Family history of breast cancer (male or female relatives)
Conditions causing hyperestrogenism: cirrhosis, obesity, exogenous estrogen
Testicular disorders (undescended testes, orchitis, prior orchiectomy)
Gynecomastia (marker of hyperestrogenism but not a direct cause)
clinicalPearls: All men diagnosed with breast cancer should be tested for BRCA mutations (especially BRCA2) - a positive result has implications for cancer surveillance and for genetic counseling of family members
Male breast cancer is almost always ER-positive (over 90%), making tamoxifen the cornerstone of adjuvant hormonal therapy - aromatase inhibitors alone are not effective in men
Men often present at more advanced stages because of low awareness and delayed medical attention for breast symptoms - any subareolar mass or nipple change in a male should be promptly evaluated

cervical-cancer-basics-rpn.title: Cervical Cancer Basics (RPN)
cervical-cancer-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cervical Cancer Basics (RPN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Persistent high-risk HPV infection (types 16 and 18 cause approximately 70% of cases)
Smoking (doubles the risk of cervical cancer)
Multiple sexual partners or early onset of sexual activity
History of sexually transmitted infections (chlamydia, herpes)
Prolonged oral contraceptive use (greater than 5 years)
clinicalPearls: HPV vaccination is the most effective primary prevention strategy for cervical cancer - strongly encourage completion of the vaccine series
Abnormal vaginal bleeding, especially postcoital bleeding, is the most common early warning sign of cervical cancer and should always be reported
Pap screening detects precancerous changes years before invasive cancer develops - emphasize adherence to screening recommendations

cervical-cancer-screening-np.title: Cervical Cancer Screening & Advanced Management (NP)
cervical-cancer-screening-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cervical Cancer Screening & Advanced Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Persistent high-risk HPV infection (HPV 16 confers highest risk for squamous cell carcinoma; HPV 18 for adenocarcinoma)
Immunosuppression (HIV-positive women have 6-fold increased risk; CD4 count inversely correlates with CIN progression)
Smoking (nitrosamines concentrate in cervical mucus and cause direct DNA damage; synergistic carcinogenic effect with HPV)
High parity (3 or more full-term pregnancies increases risk through hormonal and mechanical cervical trauma mechanisms)
Long-term combined oral contraceptive use (greater than 5 years; estrogen promotes viral gene expression in HPV-infected cells)
Co-infection with Chlamydia trachomatis (chronic inflammation facilitates HPV persistence and integration)
DES exposure in utero (increased risk of clear cell adenocarcinoma of the cervix and vagina)
MTHFR polymorphisms affecting folate metabolism (impaired DNA methylation and repair)
clinicalPearls: ASCCP risk-based management uses HPV genotype-specific risk stratification: HPV 16-positive ASC-US carries a 5-year CIN 3+ risk of approximately 4%, warranting immediate colposcopy, while non-16/18 HPV-positive ASC-US has lower risk and may be managed with 1-year surveillance

Adenocarcinoma in situ (AIS) of the cervix requires cold knife conization (not LEEP) with negative margins for fertility-sparing management, as skip lesions are common and thermal artifact from LEEP obscures margin evaluation

Pembrolizumab has changed the treatment paradigm for recurrent cervical cancer - the KEYNOTE-826 trial demonstrated significant overall survival benefit when added to chemotherapy with or without bevacizumab in PD-L1-positive (CPS greater than or equal to 1) recurrent or metastatic disease

tumor-lysis-rpn.title: Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS)
tumor-lysis-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tumor Lysis Syndrome (TLS), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Large tumor burden with bulky disease or high white blood cell count
Rapidly proliferating malignancies (Burkitt lymphoma, acute lymphoblastic leukemia, acute myeloid leukemia)
High sensitivity of tumor to cytotoxic therapy
Elevated baseline uric acid or LDH levels before treatment
Inadequate hydration before initiation of chemotherapy
clinicalPearls: TLS can occur within 12 to 72 hours after initiating chemotherapy - the highest risk period requires intensive monitoring even if the patient appears clinically stable

Rasburicase is contraindicated in patients with G6PD deficiency due to risk of severe hemolytic anemia - screen high-risk populations (African, Mediterranean, Southeast Asian descent) before administration

Do not administer calcium unless the patient is symptomatic (tetany, seizures, cardiac instability) because correcting calcium while phosphorus is elevated can cause widespread calcium phosphate precipitation in tissues including the kidneys and heart

osteoarthritis.title: Osteoarthritis
osteoarthritis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Osteoarthritis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age over 50 - cartilage water content increases while proteoglycan concentration decreases
Obesity - excess mechanical load accelerates cartilage breakdown in weight-bearing joints
Previous joint trauma or surgery disrupting normal cartilage architecture
Repetitive occupational stress from kneeling, squatting, or heavy lifting
Female sex after menopause due to estrogen decline affecting cartilage metabolism
Genetic predisposition affecting collagen structure and chondrocyte function
Malalignment deformities such as genu varum or genu valgum altering load distribution
clinicalPearls: Heberden nodes (DIP) and Bouchard nodes (PIP) are pathognomonic for OA and distinguish it from RA which affects MCP and PIP joints symmetrically
OA morning stiffness lasts less than 30 minutes while RA stiffness persists for more than 1 hour - this is a high-yield exam differentiator
A cane should be held in the hand opposite the affected joint and advanced with the affected leg to redistribute weight effectively

fibromyalgia.title: Fibromyalgia
fibromyalgia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Fibromyalgia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex - affects women 7 times more frequently due to differences in pain processing and hormonal influences
History of physical or emotional trauma triggering central sensitization
Co-existing mood disorders including depression and anxiety sharing serotonin pathway disruption
Sleep disorders - disrupted stage IV sleep prevents tissue repair and pain modulation
Family history suggesting genetic predisposition in serotonin transporter gene polymorphisms
Autoimmune conditions such as lupus or RA that may trigger secondary fibromyalgia
Chronic stress activating prolonged HPA axis stimulation and cortisol dysregulation
Viral infections including hepatitis C and HIV potentially triggering central sensitization
clinicalPearls: Fibromyalgia is NOT an inflammatory or autoimmune condition - all inflammatory markers should be normal; abnormal results warrant investigation for other diagnoses
Opioids are contraindicated in fibromyalgia because they worsen central sensitization through opioid-induced hyperalgesia and provide no long-term benefit
The old 'tender point' exam requiring 11 of 18 points has been replaced by the WPI and SSS criteria - updated exams reflect this change

muscle-strain.title: Muscle Strain
muscle-strain.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Muscle Strain, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inadequate warm-up before activity - cold muscles have decreased elasticity and are prone to tearing
Muscle fatigue reducing the protective stretch reflex threshold
Previous muscle injury creating scar tissue with decreased compliance at the injury site
Poor flexibility and tight musculature limiting available range before fiber failure
Muscle imbalance between agonist and antagonist groups creating abnormal force distribution
Dehydration and electrolyte imbalance impairing muscle contractile function
Sudden eccentric loading (lengthening under force) which generates the highest mechanical stress
clinicalPearls: Ice should never be applied directly to skin - always use a barrier cloth to prevent frostbite; limit application to 20-minute intervals
The HARM principle (Heat, Alcohol, Running, Massage) identifies interventions to avoid in the first 48-72 hours because they increase bleeding and swelling
Pain with passive stretching of the affected muscle is the earliest indicator of compartment syndrome and requires emergent evaluation

myoglobinuria.title: Myoglobinuria
myoglobinuria.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Myoglobinuria, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Rhabdomyolysis from crush injuries, prolonged immobilization, or extreme exertion
Statin therapy combined with fibrates or CYP3A4 inhibitors increasing myotoxicity risk
Heat stroke causing direct thermal injury to myocytes
Seizure activity producing sustained involuntary muscle contraction and metabolic exhaustion
Alcohol and illicit drug use (cocaine, amphetamines) causing vasospasm and muscle ischemia
Hypokalemia and hypophosphatemia destabilizing sarcolemmal membrane integrity
Malignant hyperthermia triggering uncontrolled calcium release and sustained muscle contraction
Compartment syndrome restricting blood flow and causing ischemic muscle death
clinicalPearls: Dipstick-positive blood with no RBCs on microscopy is the hallmark finding - the dipstick detects heme from myoglobin, not intact red blood cells
Aggressive IV fluids are the cornerstone of treatment because they dilute myoglobin concentration, increase tubular flow rate, and maintain renal perfusion pressure
CK peaks at 24-72 hours and is a more reliable marker of muscle injury than myoglobin, which is cleared from blood within 6-8 hours

rhabdomyolysis.title: Rhabdomyolyse
rhabdomyolysis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rhabdomyolyse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: CK peaks at 24-72 hours after injury
Monitor for compartment syndrome (5 P's: Pain, Pallor, Pulselessness, Paresthesia, Paralysis)
Aggressive fluid resuscitation is the KEY intervention
Monitor potassium closely - hyperkalemia can cause fatal arrhythmias
Avoid calcium supplementation unless symptomatic hyperkalemia (calcium may deposit in damaged muscle)

bone-tumors.title: Bone Tumors
bone-tumors.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bone Tumors, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age 10-25 years - peak incidence during adolescent growth spurts when osteoblast activity is highest
Male sex - osteosarcoma occurs 1.5 times more frequently in males
Rapid skeletal growth - taller adolescents have increased risk correlating with growth velocity
Prior radiation therapy to bone causing DNA damage in osteoprogenitor cells
Paget disease of bone with increased osteoblast turnover creating opportunities for malignant transformation
Hereditary retinoblastoma (RB1 gene mutation) increasing osteosarcoma risk 500-fold
Li-Fraumeni syndrome (TP53 mutation) predisposing to multiple tumor types including osteosarcoma
clinicalPearls: Sunburst pattern on X-ray indicates osteosarcoma (metaphysis) while onion-skin pattern indicates Ewing sarcoma (diaphysis) - this is a classic radiographic differentiator
Never biopsy a suspected bone tumor without orthopedic oncology consultation - improper biopsy tract placement can contaminate tissue planes and prevent limb salvage surgery
Night pain that wakes a child from sleep and is unresponsive to usual analgesics should raise immediate suspicion for bone malignancy rather than 'growing pains'

osteogenesis-imperfecta.title: Osteogenesis Imperfecta
osteogenesis-imperfecta.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Osteogenesis Imperfecta, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autosomal dominant inheritance - one affected parent confers 50% risk to each offspring
Spontaneous new mutations account for approximately 25% of cases with no family history
COL1A1 mutations typically produce milder disease (quantitative collagen deficiency)
COL1A2 mutations often produce severe disease (qualitative structural defect)
Multiple pregnancies increase fracture risk in affected neonates during delivery
Vitamin D deficiency further compromising already fragile bone mineralization
clinicalPearls: Blue sclera, multiple fractures, and hearing loss form the classic triad of osteogenesis imperfecta - blue sclera is the most recognizable feature on exam questions
OI must be considered and ruled out before accusing caregivers of child abuse in infants with multiple fractures - genetic testing should be performed
Type I is the mildest and most common form - patients have normal or near-normal stature with blue sclera and fractures that decrease after puberty

ankylosing-spondylitis.title: Ankylosing Spondylitis
ankylosing-spondylitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ankylosing Spondylitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: HLA-B27 positive genotype - present in over 90% of patients with AS
Male sex - affects men 2-3 times more frequently and typically with more severe disease
Age of onset typically 20-30 years - onset after age 45 is rare and should prompt alternative diagnoses
