Family history of spondyloarthropathy - 20% risk if first-degree relative is affected and HLA-B27 positive
Inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn disease, ulcerative colitis) sharing genetic susceptibility loci
Psoriasis - psoriatic arthritis and AS share the spondyloarthropathy disease spectrum
Preceding genitourinary or gastrointestinal infection triggering reactive arthritis in susceptible individuals
clinicalPearls: AS pain improves with activity and worsens with rest - this is the opposite of mechanical back pain and is a critical exam differentiator
The 'question mark posture' (loss of lumbar lordosis with thoracic kyphosis and cervical hyperextension) is the classic late-stage deformity
Young male with chronic low back pain lasting more than 3 months, morning stiffness, and improvement with exercise should always trigger evaluation for ankylosing spondylitis

contracture.title: Contracture
contracture.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Contracture, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prolonged immobilization from casting, bedrest, or mechanical ventilation
Burn injuries causing dermal scarring and contraction across joint surfaces
Spasticity from upper motor neuron lesions (stroke, TBI, cerebral palsy) maintaining flexed positions
Joint inflammation or pain causing protective guarding in flexed postures
Peripheral nerve injury resulting in unopposed action of innervated muscle groups
Advanced age with decreased tissue elasticity and increased susceptibility to immobility effects
Inadequate positioning and range of motion exercises during hospitalization
clinicalPearls: Prevention is far easier than treatment - contractures can begin forming within 5-7 days of immobilization, making early ROM exercises essential for every immobilized patient
Never place a pillow under the knee of a bedbound patient - this promotes hip and knee flexion contractures that severely impair ambulation recovery
Foot drop (plantar flexion contracture) from peroneal nerve compression or ankle immobilization is the most common preventable contracture in hospitalized patients

chronic-fatigue-syndrome.title: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
chronic-fatigue-syndrome.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex - affects women 2-4 times more frequently than men
Preceding acute viral infection (EBV, HHV-6, enteroviruses) triggering immune dysregulation
Age 30-50 years at onset with peak incidence in the fourth decade
History of significant physical or emotional stressor preceding symptom onset
Genetic predisposition with familial clustering suggesting heritable susceptibility
Pre-existing autoimmune conditions sharing immune dysregulation pathways
COVID-19 infection - significant proportion of long COVID patients meet ME/CFS criteria
clinicalPearls: Post-exertional malaise (PEM) is the hallmark distinguishing feature - patients feel worse 12-48 hours after activity rather than better, which is opposite to depression where exercise typically helps
Graded exercise therapy (GET) has been shown to harm many ME/CFS patients - activity pacing within the energy envelope is the evidence-based approach
All standard laboratory values must be normal for ME/CFS diagnosis - any abnormal findings should trigger investigation for an alternative diagnosis

disuse-atrophy.title: Disuse Atrophy
disuse-atrophy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Disuse Atrophy, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prolonged bedrest from illness, surgery, or trauma - muscle loss begins within 24-48 hours
Cast immobilization restricting muscle contraction in the affected extremity
Mechanical ventilation preventing diaphragm contraction causing ventilator-induced diaphragmatic dysfunction
Spinal cord injury eliminating voluntary motor signals below the lesion level
Advanced age with sarcopenia as baseline - older adults lose muscle mass faster with immobility
Malnutrition and inadequate protein intake preventing muscle protein synthesis
Critical illness with catabolic hormonal milieu (elevated cortisol, decreased testosterone/growth hormone)
Chronic disease states including heart failure, COPD, and cancer causing cachexia
clinicalPearls: Muscle strength decreases by 1-1.5% per day and 10-15% per week of bedrest - early mobilization is the single most effective intervention to prevent disuse atrophy
It takes 2-3 times longer to regain muscle mass than it took to lose it - a patient on 2 weeks of bedrest may require 4-6 weeks of rehabilitation
Isometric exercises (contracting the muscle without moving the joint) can be performed even in casted extremities and significantly reduce atrophy compared to complete immobilization

traction.title: Traction
traction.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Traction, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Pin site contamination from improper cleaning technique introducing bacteria to bone
Prolonged immobilization during traction increasing risk for DVT, PE, and pressure injuries
Excessive traction weight causing nerve damage, vascular compression, or nonunion
Improper countertraction allowing the patient to slide toward the weight, negating the pulling force
Pre-existing peripheral vascular disease compromising perfusion in the traction extremity
Skin breakdown under adhesive wraps or boot in skin traction application
Malnutrition impairing wound healing at pin sites and fracture healing
clinicalPearls: Weights must hang freely at all times - never rest on the bed, floor, or be lifted during repositioning; removing traction weight disrupts fracture alignment and causes muscle spasm
The 5 P's of neurovascular assessment (Pain, Pallor, Pulselessness, Paresthesia, Paralysis) must be performed every 1-2 hours - paralysis is the LAST finding and indicates irreversible damage
Skin traction is limited to 5-8 pounds maximum and is temporary; skeletal traction can use 25-30 pounds and is used for definitive long bone fracture management

knee-arthroplasty.title: Knee Arthroplasty
knee-arthroplasty.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Knee Arthroplasty, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: DVT and PE risk elevated due to surgical venous injury, immobility, and inflammatory hypercoagulability
Infection risk at the prosthetic joint - biofilm formation on the prosthesis is difficult to eradicate
Advanced age with multiple comorbidities increasing anesthetic and surgical risk
Obesity increasing mechanical stress on the prosthetic components and wound healing complications
Diabetes mellitus impairing wound healing and increasing surgical site infection risk
Prior knee surgery creating scar tissue that complicates surgical approach and ROM recovery
Anticoagulant or antiplatelet therapy increasing bleeding risk perioperatively
clinicalPearls: PE is the number one cause of death after total knee replacement - aggressive DVT prophylaxis and early ambulation are essential preventive measures
Never place a pillow directly behind the knee after TKA - this promotes flexion contracture and inhibits venous return; elevate the entire leg instead
CPM machine settings should be increased gradually (approximately 10 degrees per day) with a goal of 90 degrees flexion by discharge - full ROM recovery may take 3-6 months

residual-limb-care.title: Residual Limb Care Post Amputation
residual-limb-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Residual Limb Care Post Amputation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Peripheral vascular disease (the most common reason for lower extremity amputation)
Diabetes mellitus causing neuropathy, peripheral vascular disease, and impaired wound healing
Smoking causing vasoconstriction that compromises residual limb perfusion and flap survival
Infection at the surgical site delaying wound healing and potentially requiring revision
Poor nutritional status impairing tissue repair and increasing infection susceptibility
Pre-amputation pain - patients with significant pain before surgery have higher phantom pain incidence
Traumatic amputation with irregular wound edges and contamination versus planned surgical amputation
clinicalPearls: Elastic bandage must be wrapped in a figure-eight pattern (NOT circular) from distal to proximal to create the conical shape needed for prosthetic fitting and to prevent tourniquet effect
Elevate the residual limb for the first 24-48 hours post-op to reduce edema, then discontinue elevation and begin prone positioning to prevent hip flexion contracture
Mirror therapy works by placing a mirror to reflect the intact limb, creating a visual illusion that allows the brain to 'move' the phantom limb and reduce cortical pain signals

rib-fractures.title: Rib Fractures
rib-fractures.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rib Fractures, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Blunt chest trauma from motor vehicle accidents, falls, or assault
Osteoporosis causing fractures with minimal force, especially in elderly women
Chronic steroid use weakening bone cortex through osteoclast activation
Elderly age - rib compliance decreases with calcification of costal cartilage
Contact sports and high-velocity recreational activities
Repetitive stress from severe chronic coughing (pathologic cough fractures)
Metastatic bone disease weakening ribs from tumor-replaced cortex
clinicalPearls: Never apply rib binders or external splints - they restrict chest wall expansion and promote atelectasis, the opposite of what is needed. Teach patients to pillow-splint during coughing instead
The most dangerous complication of rib fractures is NOT the fracture itself but the pneumonia and respiratory failure that result from pain-induced hypoventilation and secretion retention
Left lower rib fractures raise suspicion for splenic injury; right lower rib fractures raise suspicion for hepatic injury - always assess for abdominal organ damage

osteomalacia.title: Osteomalacia
osteomalacia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Osteomalacia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inadequate sunlight exposure - UV-B radiation converts 7-dehydrocholesterol to cholecalciferol in the skin
Dietary vitamin D deficiency especially in strict vegan diets without supplementation
Malabsorption syndromes (celiac disease, Crohn disease, post-gastric bypass) reducing fat-soluble vitamin absorption
Chronic kidney disease preventing conversion of 25-hydroxyvitamin D to active 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D
Hepatic disease impairing 25-hydroxylation of vitamin D in the liver
Anticonvulsant therapy (phenytoin, carbamazepine) accelerating hepatic vitamin D catabolism via CYP450 induction
Dark skin pigmentation requiring more sunlight exposure for equivalent vitamin D synthesis
Elderly institutionalized patients with limited sun exposure and decreased skin synthesis capacity
clinicalPearls: Osteomalacia has too much osteoid with too little mineral; osteoporosis has too little bone overall with normal mineralization - this is the key pathologic distinction
Looser zones (pseudofractures) are pathognomonic for osteomalacia - they appear as radiolucent bands perpendicular to the cortex at sites of mechanical stress
Secondary hyperparathyroidism with elevated ALP, low calcium, and low phosphorus is the characteristic lab pattern - if calcium is normal, osteomalacia is unlikely

acute-lumbosacral-strain.title: Acute Lumbosacral Strain
acute-lumbosacral-strain.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Acute Lumbosacral Strain, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Improper lifting mechanics - bending at the waist rather than squatting with a straight back
Sudden twisting movements while lifting heavy objects
Weak core musculature providing inadequate spinal stabilization
Sedentary lifestyle with deconditioning of paraspinal muscles
Obesity increasing mechanical load on lumbar structures
Occupations requiring repetitive heavy lifting, bending, or prolonged sitting
Prior lumbar strain creating scar tissue with decreased flexibility at the injury site
clinicalPearls: Prolonged bedrest (more than 48 hours) is harmful in acute lumbosacral strain - it promotes deconditioning, increases disability perception, and delays return to function
The absence of neurologic deficits and negative straight leg raise distinguishes simple strain from disc herniation with nerve root compression
Red flag symptoms requiring emergent evaluation: saddle anesthesia, bowel/bladder incontinence, bilateral leg weakness (cauda equina syndrome), fever with back pain (spinal infection)

lumbosacral-disc-herniation.title: Lumbosacral Disc Herniation
lumbosacral-disc-herniation.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lumbosacral Disc Herniation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Repetitive flexion and rotation loading of the lumbar spine degrading annulus fibrosus integrity
Heavy lifting with improper mechanics concentrating compressive forces on the posterior disc
Prolonged sitting causing sustained flexion loading and posterior disc pressure
Smoking decreasing disc nutrition through vasoconstriction of vertebral end plate vasculature
Age 30-50 years - disc degeneration sufficient to weaken annulus but nucleus still gelatinous enough to herniate
Obesity increasing axial compression loading on lumbar discs
Tall stature associated with increased mechanical stress on lumbar segments
Occupational vibration exposure (truck driving) accelerating disc degeneration
clinicalPearls: Cauda equina syndrome (bilateral leg weakness, saddle anesthesia, bowel/bladder dysfunction) requires emergent MRI and surgical decompression within 24-48 hours to prevent permanent neurologic damage
The positive straight leg raise test is the most sensitive clinical test for lumbar disc herniation - positive when radicular pain (not just hamstring tightness) is reproduced at less than 60 degrees
90% of disc herniations resolve with conservative management - surgery is reserved for failed conservative therapy, progressive neurologic deficit, or cauda equina syndrome

low-back-pain.title: Low Back Pain
low-back-pain.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Low Back Pain, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Sedentary lifestyle with weak core musculature providing inadequate spinal support
Obesity increasing biomechanical stress on lumbar structures
Occupations requiring heavy lifting, prolonged standing, or whole-body vibration
Smoking reducing vertebral body blood flow and impairing disc nutrition
Psychological factors - depression, anxiety, and job dissatisfaction strongly predict chronicity
Poor posture and ergonomics creating sustained abnormal loading patterns
Age-related disc degeneration and facet joint osteoarthritis
clinicalPearls: 85% of low back pain is non-specific mechanical pain that resolves within 6 weeks - routine imaging leads to unnecessary findings and interventions that do not improve outcomes
Red flags for cauda equina syndrome (saddle anesthesia, bowel/bladder dysfunction, bilateral neurologic deficits) require emergent MRI and are the most critical findings to recognize
Psychosocial yellow flags (fear avoidance, catastrophizing, depression, work dissatisfaction) are stronger predictors of chronic disability than any anatomic finding on imaging

septic-arthritis.title: Septic Arthritis
septic-arthritis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Septic Arthritis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Pre-existing joint disease (rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis) providing damaged tissue susceptible to bacterial seeding
Prosthetic joint - biofilm formation on prosthetic surfaces is extremely difficult to eradicate
Immunosuppression from diabetes, HIV, corticosteroid therapy, or chemotherapy
IV drug use providing direct hematogenous bacterial access
Recent joint surgery, arthrocentesis, or intra-articular injection breaching the sterile joint barrier
Skin infections or cellulitis providing contiguous spread pathway to nearby joints
Advanced age with decreased immune function and increased comorbidities
Bacteremia from any source allowing hematogenous seeding of the synovium
clinicalPearls: A hot, swollen, painful monoarticular joint is septic arthritis until proven otherwise - emergent arthrocentesis is both diagnostic and therapeutic and should never be delayed
Synovial fluid WBC greater than 50,000/mm3 with greater than 75% neutrophils is highly suggestive of septic arthritis and warrants immediate IV antibiotics
Gonococcal septic arthritis should be considered in sexually active young adults presenting with migratory polyarthralgia progressing to monoarticular septic arthritis with or without skin lesions

fat-embolism.title: Fat Embolism
fat-embolism.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Fat Embolism, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Long bone fractures, especially femur shaft fractures - the most common precipitant
Multiple fractures and major trauma releasing larger volumes of marrow fat
Orthopedic procedures involving intramedullary reaming and prosthesis insertion
Young adults and adolescents - active red marrow contains more fat than elderly yellow marrow
Inadequate fracture immobilization allowing continued bone marrow disruption
Liposuction and lipectomy procedures releasing adipose tissue into venous circulation
Pancreatitis with lipase activation and fat necrosis releasing free fatty acids
clinicalPearls: The classic triad of fat embolism syndrome - respiratory distress, neurologic changes, and petechial rash - typically presents 24-72 hours after long bone fracture; petechiae are the LAST component to appear
Petechial rash on the chest, axillae, and conjunctivae is pathognomonic for FES but is transient and easily missed - deliberately inspect these areas in all long bone fracture patients
Early surgical fixation of long bone fractures within 24 hours significantly reduces fat embolism incidence compared to delayed fixation

crutch-paralysis.title: Crutch Paralysis
crutch-paralysis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Crutch Paralysis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Improper crutch fitting - axillary pad too high pressing into the axilla during use
Bearing weight on the axillary pad rather than the hand grips during ambulation
Prolonged crutch use without proper education on weight distribution
Thin body habitus with less subcutaneous tissue protecting neurovascular structures
Pre-existing neuropathy (diabetic) making nerves more susceptible to compression injury
Improper crutch height measurement - should be 2-3 finger widths below the axillary fold
clinicalPearls: Weight must be borne on the HAND GRIPS, never the axillary pads - this is the single most important teaching point for preventing crutch paralysis
Proper crutch height places the pad 2-3 finger widths (1.5-2 inches) below the axillary fold with elbows flexed 15-30 degrees when hands are on the grips
If a patient develops tingling or numbness in the hands during crutch use, crutch fit and technique must be immediately reassessed before nerve damage becomes permanent

syndactyly.title: Syndactyly
syndactyly.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Syndactyly, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autosomal dominant inheritance pattern in familial cases (non-syndromic syndactyly)
Male sex - affected approximately twice as frequently as females
Genetic syndromes such as Apert syndrome (FGFR2 mutation) causing complex syndactyly
Teratogenic exposure during the 6th-8th week of gestation when digital separation occurs
Family history of limb anomalies suggesting inherited disruption of limb patterning genes
Amniotic band syndrome causing secondary syndactyly from mechanical constriction
clinicalPearls: Syndactyly of border digits (thumb-index or ring-little finger) requires earlier surgical correction because length discrepancy between fused digits causes angular deformity and progressive tethering during growth
The most common presentation is simple complete syndactyly between the 3rd and 4th fingers (long and ring fingers)
When syndactyly is associated with other congenital anomalies, a thorough genetic evaluation is warranted as it may be part of a recognizable syndrome with implications for other organ systems

polydactyly.title: Polydactyly
polydactyly.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Polydactyly, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autosomal dominant inheritance in isolated postaxial polydactyly
African American ethnicity for postaxial polydactyly (10 times more common than in Caucasians)
Genetic syndromes such as trisomy 13 (Patau syndrome), Ellis-van Creveld, and Bardet-Biedl syndrome
Family history - strong familial pattern with variable expression
Teratogenic exposure during limb bud development (4th-8th week of gestation)
Associated anomalies suggesting chromosomal abnormality when bilateral or multiple digits involved
clinicalPearls: Postaxial polydactyly (extra little finger) in an African American newborn is typically isolated, autosomal dominant, and benign - rudimentary Type B digits can be ligated in the nursery
Polydactyly associated with other congenital anomalies (cardiac defects, cleft lip, microcephaly) should trigger evaluation for trisomy 13 (Patau syndrome) and other chromosomal abnormalities
Never use epinephrine-containing local anesthetic for digital procedures - digits have end-artery blood supply and vasoconstriction causes ischemic necrosis

hip-dysplasia.title: Dysplasie de la hanche
hip-dysplasia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Dysplasie de la hanche, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Screen ALL newborns with Ortolani and Barlow maneuvers at birth and at every well-baby visit. High-risk infants (breech, family history) should have hip ultrasound at 4-6 weeks.
Pavlik harness must be worn 23 hours/day: educate parents on proper positioning, diapering with harness, and skin inspection. Never lift infant by legs.
Monitor skin integrity under harness straps every 8-12 hours: look for redness, irritation, or pressure areas. Place a thin cotton undershirt beneath harness.
Double or triple diapering is NOT an effective treatment: this is an outdated practice. Proper orthopedic referral and Pavlik harness are the standard of care.

clubfoot.title: Clubfoot (Talipes Equinovarus)
clubfoot.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Clubfoot (Talipes Equinovarus), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Ponseti method has >95% success rate when started early - it has replaced extensive surgical correction as first-line treatment
Brace compliance is the KEY to preventing recurrence - 80% recurrence rate without bracing vs <5% with proper brace use
A positional clubfoot (flexible, corrects with gentle pressure) is NOT true clubfoot and resolves with stretching exercises alone

rickets.title: Rickets
rickets.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rickets, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Exclusive breastfeeding without vitamin D supplementation
Dark skin pigmentation (reduced UV vitamin D synthesis)
Geographic latitude with low UV exposure
clinicalPearls: ALL breastfed infants should receive 400 IU/day of vitamin D starting within the first few days of life - breast milk contains insufficient vitamin D
Rachitic rosary (beading at costochondral junctions) is pathognomonic for rickets - palpate along the rib cage during assessment
Alkaline phosphatase is the first lab to elevate and last to normalize - use it to monitor treatment response

scoliosis.title: Scoliosis
scoliosis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Scoliosis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex (8:1 for curves requiring treatment)
clinicalPearls: Adam's forward bend test is the gold standard screening tool - a positive test (visible rib hump or trunk asymmetry) requires X-ray confirmation
Brace compliance is everything - the brace only works if worn as prescribed; address adolescent body image concerns proactively
Post-spinal fusion: log-roll ONLY, watch for superior mesenteric artery syndrome (abdominal distension, bilious vomiting, inability to tolerate feeds)

osteoporosis-rn.title: Osteoporosis - RN Clinical Management
osteoporosis-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Osteoporosis - RN Clinical Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Postmenopausal estrogen deficiency accelerating osteoclast-mediated bone resorption
Advanced age with declining osteoblast proliferative capacity and reduced growth factor signaling
Low body weight (BMI less than 19) providing less mechanical loading stimulus for bone formation
Family history of osteoporotic fracture suggesting genetic variation in collagen structure or RANKL/OPG ratio
Prolonged glucocorticoid therapy (prednisone greater than 5 mg/day for 3 or more months) suppressing osteoblast function and increasing osteoclast lifespan
Smoking reducing estrogen levels, impairing osteoblast activity, and increasing cortisol
Excessive alcohol intake (more than 3 drinks per day) directly toxic to osteoblasts
Sedentary lifestyle removing mechanical loading signals that stimulate bone formation
Calcium and vitamin D deficiency reducing substrate for mineralization and triggering secondary hyperparathyroidism
Hyperthyroidism, hyperparathyroidism, and hypogonadism accelerating bone turnover
clinicalPearls: DEXA T-score of -2.5 or below diagnoses osteoporosis, but treatment may also be indicated for osteopenia with high FRAX score (10-year hip fracture risk of 3 percent or greater, or major osteoporotic fracture risk of 20 percent or greater)
Height loss greater than 2 cm at a single visit or greater than 4 cm cumulative should prompt vertebral fracture assessment - many compression fractures are clinically silent

Bisphosphonate drug holidays (temporary discontinuation after 3-5 years) are considered for lower-risk patients because the drug remains bound to bone for years, but high-risk patients should continue treatment

osteoporosis-advanced-np.title: Osteoporosis - NP Advanced Prescriptive Management
osteoporosis-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Osteoporosis - NP Advanced Prescriptive Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Postmenopausal estrogen deficiency removing suppression of RANKL and osteoclast apoptosis signals
Glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis through suppression of Wnt signaling, osteoblast apoptosis induction, and enhanced osteoclastogenesis
Aromatase inhibitor therapy in breast cancer eliminating residual peripheral estrogen conversion
Androgen deprivation therapy in prostate cancer removing testosterone-mediated bone protection
Type 2 diabetes with paradoxically increased fracture risk despite normal or elevated BMD due to altered bone quality (advanced glycation end-products crosslinking collagen)
Chronic kidney disease-mineral bone disorder (CKD-MBD) with impaired vitamin D activation and secondary/tertiary hyperparathyroidism
Genetic polymorphisms in LRP5, ESR1, SOST, and RANKL genes affecting peak bone mass and remodeling rates
Celiac disease and inflammatory bowel disease with malabsorption and chronic inflammatory cytokine-driven bone loss
clinicalPearls: Anabolic-first sequencing: in very high-risk patients (T-score below -3.0, multiple vertebral fractures, recent hip fracture), starting with teriparatide or romosozumab and then transitioning to a bisphosphonate produces greater BMD gains than antiresorptive-first strategies

Denosumab must never be simply discontinued - always transition to a bisphosphonate (preferably zoledronic acid 6 months after the last denosumab dose) to prevent rebound vertebral fractures from explosive osteoclast reactivation

FRAX has limitations: it underestimates risk in patients with type 2 diabetes (who have normal BMD but poor bone quality), glucocorticoid doses above 7.5 mg/day, multiple recent fractures, and lumbar spine BMD much lower than femoral neck BMD

rib-fractures-rpn.title: Rib Fractures - RPN Fundamentals
rib-fractures-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rib Fractures - RPN Fundamentals, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Falls, especially in elderly patients with osteoporosis
Motor vehicle accidents causing blunt chest trauma
Direct blows during contact sports or physical assault
Chronic severe coughing from respiratory infections
Osteoporosis causing fractures with minimal force
Chronic steroid use weakening bone structure
clinicalPearls: Pain control is treatment - adequate analgesia allows the patient to breathe deeply, cough effectively, and use the incentive spirometer, preventing the most dangerous complication (pneumonia)
Never wrap, bind, or strap the chest for rib fractures - this was abandoned decades ago because it restricts breathing and dramatically increases pneumonia risk
Report any sudden change in breathing, new absent breath sounds, or crackling under the skin immediately - these may indicate pneumothorax requiring emergency intervention

rib-fractures-np.title: Rib Fractures - NP Advanced Management
rib-fractures-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rib Fractures - NP Advanced Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: High-energy blunt chest trauma (motor vehicle collision, fall from height) producing multiple fractures and pulmonary contusion
Elderly patients (age greater than 65) with rib fractures have significantly higher pneumonia and mortality rates than younger adults with equivalent injuries
Anticoagulant therapy increasing hemothorax risk from intercostal vessel laceration
Pre-existing COPD with limited pulmonary reserve unable to compensate for impaired ventilation
Bilateral rib fractures compounding ventilatory impairment and pain burden
First and second rib fractures indicating very high-energy mechanism and associated with great vessel and brachial plexus injury
Lower rib fractures (ribs 9-12) associated with hepatic injury (right) and splenic injury (left) requiring abdominal evaluation
Flail chest with underlying pulmonary contusion as the primary driver of respiratory failure
clinicalPearls: In flail chest, respiratory failure is primarily caused by the underlying pulmonary contusion, not the paradoxical chest wall motion - stabilizing the flail segment alone does not resolve hypoxemia if significant contusion is present

The 'Rule of 65': patients aged 65 or older with 3 or more rib fractures have mortality rates exceeding 20 percent and should be strongly considered for ICU-level monitoring with early epidural or regional analgesia

Surgical rib fixation (SSRF) is an emerging standard for flail chest - evidence from recent trials demonstrates significant reductions in ventilator days, ICU length of stay, pneumonia rates, and long-term pain compared to non-operative management

rotavirus-rpn.title: Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - RPN Basics
rotavirus-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - RPN Basics, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Rotavirus is the most common cause of severe dehydrating diarrhea in children under 5 worldwide
Alcohol-based hand sanitizers do NOT effectively kill rotavirus: use soap and water for hand hygiene
Dehydration is the primary danger: monitor weight, urine output, and hydration status closely
Contact precautions are essential: rotavirus is extremely contagious and survives on surfaces for days

rotavirus.title: Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - RN Clinical Management
rotavirus.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - RN Clinical Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Low socioeconomic status and crowded living conditions
Premature infants with immature gut immunity
clinicalPearls: Rotavirus diarrhea involves BOTH osmotic (disaccharidase loss) and secretory (NSP4 enterotoxin) mechanisms: this dual mechanism explains the severity
Tachycardia is the earliest and most sensitive cardiovascular indicator of dehydration in children: do not wait for hypotension
Oral rehydration therapy is as effective as IV rehydration for mild-moderate dehydration and is the WHO-recommended first-line treatment
Early refeeding (within 4-6 hours of rehydration) promotes enterocyte regeneration and shortens illness duration: do NOT withhold food
Alcohol-based hand sanitizers are NOT effective against rotavirus: soap and water hand washing is mandatory
Stool viral shedding can persist 10+ days after symptom resolution: maintain contact precautions accordingly

rotavirus-np.title: Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - NP Advanced Practice
rotavirus-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Rotavirus Gastroenteritis - NP Advanced Practice, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age 6-24 months (waning maternal antibody, immature mucosal immunity)
Incomplete vaccination or vaccine failure (more common in low-income settings)
Severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) and other primary immunodeficiencies (risk of chronic shedding and severe disease)
Malnutrition (impaired mucosal barrier repair and immune function)
Zinc deficiency (impairs enterocyte proliferation and immune response)
Microvillus inclusion disease and other congenital enteropathies
Prematurity with immature gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT)
clinicalPearls: Rotavirus diarrhea involves four interconnected mechanisms: osmotic (disaccharidase loss), secretory (NSP4 calcium-mediated chloride secretion), neural (ENS activation via serotonin), and paracellular (tight junction disruption)
NSP4 was the first viral enterotoxin identified: it acts through PLC-IP3-calcium signaling independent of cAMP (distinguishing it from cholera toxin mechanism)
Hypernatremic dehydration is more common with rotavirus than bacterial diarrhea because of the high free-water-to-solute loss ratio: correct sodium slowly over 48-72 hours to prevent cerebral edema
Vaccine efficacy correlates with mucosal IgA response: lower efficacy in developing countries is multifactorial (maternal antibody interference, enteropathy, microbiome, zinc deficiency)
Post-infectious lactose intolerance may persist 2-6 weeks after viral clearance due to slow regeneration of brush border lactase: temporary lactose-free formula may be needed
Rotavirus can trigger autoimmune responses: molecular mimicry between VP7 and pancreatic islet cell antigens has been proposed as a mechanism linking rotavirus to type 1 diabetes development

kawasaki-critical.title: Maladie de Kawasaki
kawasaki-critical.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Maladie de Kawasaki, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Need baseline and follow-up echoes
Postpone live vaccines for 11 months post IVIG
Monitor for MI symptoms even in children

congenital-heart.title: Cardiopathie congénitale
congenital-heart.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cardiopathie congénitale, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Cyanotic defects require immediate surgical or prostaglandin intervention
Polycythemia is a compensatory response to chronic hypoxia
Monitor for congestive heart failure in acyanotic defects

pyloric-intussusception.title: Sténose du pylore et intussusception
pyloric-intussusception.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sténose du pylore et intussusception, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Pyloric stenosis: male sex (4:1), firstborn, family history
clinicalPearls: Pyloric: Vomiting is non-bilious
Intussusception: Air enema is diagnostic and often curative
Monitor for passage of normal stool

adhd-basics.title: Trouble déficitaire de l'attention
adhd-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Trouble déficitaire de l'attention, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Premature birth or low birth weight
clinicalPearls: Monitor growth (height and weight) - stimulants may suppress appetite and growth
Take stimulants in the morning to minimize insomnia
Drug holidays during school breaks may be considered
Behavioral therapy should accompany medication

separation-anxiety.title: Anxiété de séparation
separation-anxiety.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Anxiété de séparation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Recent loss or change (divorce, move, death)
clinicalPearls: Establish consistent routines
Practice brief separations and gradually increase
Avoid prolonged goodbyes
Validate feelings without reinforcing avoidance
Normal vs pathological depends on age and severity

separation-anxiety-rn.title: Anxiété de séparation – IA
separation-anxiety-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Anxiété de séparation – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of anxiety disorders or panic disorder
Traumatic separation experience (hospitalization, parental deployment, divorce)
Comorbid generalized anxiety or social anxiety disorder
Maternal anxiety disorder (modeling and genetic transmission)
Recent life transition (school change, relocation, new sibling)
clinicalPearls: SCARED screening tool: score ≥25 indicates clinically significant anxiety warranting intervention
Accommodation by parents (allowing avoidance) maintains and worsens the disorder - parent coaching is essential
School refusal is a behavioral emergency requiring rapid intervention to prevent chronic non-attendance
Comorbidity is the rule, not the exception: screen for depression, GAD, and social anxiety
CBT + SSRI combination is superior to either treatment alone for moderate-severe cases

separation-anxiety-np.title: Anxiété de séparation – IPA
separation-anxiety-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Anxiété de séparation – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: 5-HTTLPR short allele carrier status (serotonin transporter polymorphism)
Glucocorticoid receptor polymorphisms (BclI, N363S) affecting HPA axis regulation
Epigenetic modifications from early adverse experiences (NR3C1 hypermethylation)
Behavioral inhibition temperament (high amygdala reactivity phenotype)
CRF1 receptor overexpression in amygdala circuits
Adverse childhood experiences during critical attachment period (6-24 months)
Reduced prefrontal-amygdala functional connectivity on neuroimaging
clinicalPearls: Pharmacogenomics: CYP2D6 poor metabolizers may need 50% lower SSRI starting doses; ultra-rapid metabolizers may need higher doses or alternative agents
5-HTTLPR short allele carriers show greater amygdala reactivity but also greater treatment response to combined CBT + SSRI
CAMS trial evidence hierarchy: CBT + sertraline (80.7%) > CBT alone (59.7%) ≈ sertraline alone (54.9%) > placebo (23.7%)
Epigenetic changes from early stress are potentially reversible with effective treatment (reduced NR3C1 methylation after successful CBT)
Monitor for SSRI discontinuation syndrome if stopping abruptly (except fluoxetine due to long half-life) - taper over 2-4 weeks

lead-poisoning.title: Intoxication au plomb
lead-poisoning.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Intoxication au plomb, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Living in pre-1978 housing with lead-based paint
clinicalPearls: Screen at ages 1 and 2
Most common source: old paint in pre-1978 housing
Blood lead level >5 mcg/dL requires investigation
Hand washing before eating reduces ingestion
Wet mopping removes lead dust (dry sweeping spreads it)

varicella.title: Varicelle
varicella.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Varicelle, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER give aspirin to children with varicella: risk of Reye's syndrome (acute hepatic encephalopathy). Use acetaminophen for fever.
Isolate patient until ALL lesions have crusted over (typically 5-7 days after rash onset). Airborne + Contact precautions in hospital.
Varicella vaccine (Varivax) is a live attenuated vaccine given at 12-15 months and 4-6 years. Contraindicated in immunocompromised and pregnant patients.
Exposed susceptible contacts should receive varicella vaccine within 72 hours or VZIG (varicella-zoster immune globulin) if immunocompromised.

dehydration-peds.title: Déshydratation pédiatrique
dehydration-peds.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Déshydratation pédiatrique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Infants and young children (higher body water percentage)
clinicalPearls: Monitor urine output closely: best indicator of rehydration success. Goal: >1 mL/kg/hr in children, 4-6 wet diapers/day in infants.
Daily weights are the most accurate measure of fluid status: weigh at same time, same scale, same clothing. A 1kg weight loss = approximately 1L fluid deficit.
Assess anterior fontanelle in infants: sunken fontanelle indicates significant dehydration. Also reassess with rehydration (should normalize).
Teach parents ORT technique: small frequent sips (teaspoon/syringe), not large volumes at once. Vomiting does NOT mean ORT has failed: wait 15-20 minutes and restart slowly.

foreign-body-aspiration.title: Aspiration de corps étranger
foreign-body-aspiration.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Aspiration de corps étranger, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Small objects (coins, buttons, beads, small toys)
Round foods (grapes, hot dogs, nuts, popcorn)
clinicalPearls: Prevention is KEY: Cut grapes and hot dogs lengthwise (not round coins), avoid nuts/popcorn/hard candy for children <4 years, choose age-appropriate toys (no small parts), supervise meals.
Do NOT perform blind finger sweeps: may push object deeper into airway. Only remove visible objects from the mouth.
Age-appropriate intervention: Infants <1 year = back blows + chest thrusts. Children >1 year = abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver). NEVER use Heimlich on infants.
After any choking event, child should be evaluated by healthcare provider even if object was expelled: assess for retained fragments, airway trauma, or aspiration pneumonia.

androgen-insensitivity-rpn.title: Insensibilité aux androgènes – RPN
androgen-insensitivity-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insensibilité aux androgènes – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of AIS (X-linked recessive inheritance pattern)
Maternal carrier status (mother carries the gene mutation on X chromosome)
Phenotypically female infant with bilateral inguinal hernias or masses
History of undescended testes in phenotypically female child
clinicalPearls: AIS is an X-linked condition: a phenotypically female person has a 46,XY karyotype but cannot respond to androgens
Inguinal hernias in a phenotypically female infant should raise suspicion for AIS and prompt further investigation
Primary amenorrhea in an adolescent with female appearance but absent uterus is a key presentation
Sensitive, family-centered communication is essential: use preferred terms and respect privacy
Psychosocial support and referral to counseling are critical components of care

androgen-insensitivity.title: Insensibilité aux androgènes
androgen-insensitivity.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insensibilité aux androgènes, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: X-linked recessive inheritance (AR gene mutation on Xq11-12)
Maternal carrier status (50% of 46,XY offspring will be affected)
Family history of AIS, primary amenorrhea, or inguinal hernias in phenotypic females
Consanguinity (increases likelihood of homozygous or compound heterozygous mutations)
clinicalPearls: Complete AIS presents as a phenotypically female individual with 46,XY karyotype, absent uterus, and inguinal or abdominal testes
Breast development occurs normally at puberty due to aromatization of testicular testosterone to estrogen
Gonadal malignancy risk in CAIS is approximately 2-5% and increases with age; gonadectomy timing is individualized
Partial AIS presents with ambiguous genitalia and requires careful evaluation for gender assignment
Psychosocial support is paramount: disclosure should be age-appropriate, patient-centered, and involve mental health professionals

androgen-insensitivity-np.title: Insensibilité aux androgènes – IPA
androgen-insensitivity-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insensibilité aux androgènes – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: AR gene mutations on Xq11-12 (over 1,000 documented mutations)
X-linked recessive inheritance: carrier mothers have 50% chance of affected 46,XY offspring
De novo AR mutations (approximately 30% of CAIS cases have no family history)
Ligand-binding domain mutations (most common in CAIS, disrupting testosterone/DHT binding)
DNA-binding domain mutations (disrupting zinc finger-ARE interaction)
Polyglutamine repeat expansion in exon 1 (CAG repeat length inversely correlates with AR transactivation efficiency)
Family history of primary amenorrhea, inguinal hernias in phenotypic females, or infertility in 46,XY individuals
clinicalPearls: AR gene mutations are classified by affected functional domain: LBD mutations (most common in CAIS) disrupt ligand binding, DBD mutations impair ARE recognition, and NTD mutations affect coactivator recruitment

Gonadal malignancy risk differs dramatically by AIS type: CAIS 2-5% lifetime (low, can delay gonadectomy to allow natural puberty) vs PAIS with intra-abdominal gonads up to 15-50% (high, early gonadectomy recommended)

Differentiate AIS from 5-alpha reductase deficiency: both present as 46,XY DSD but testosterone-to-DHT ratio is normal in AIS and markedly elevated (>20:1) in 5-ARD; 5-ARD patients virilize at puberty while CAIS patients do not
2006 Chicago Consensus replaced outdated terminology: 'testicular feminization' is no longer used; correct term is 'complete androgen insensitivity syndrome' classified under 46,XY DSD

Pharmacogenomic consideration: CAG repeat length in AR exon 1 inversely correlates with receptor transactivation efficiency; longer repeats associated with reduced androgen sensitivity even in the general population

herbals-safety.title: Sécurité des produits naturels
herbals-safety.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sécurité des produits naturels, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always ask about herbals in the pre-op assessment
G's (Ginkgo, Garlic, Ginger, Ginseng) = Bleeding risk
Herbals are not regulated like pharmaceuticals

electrolyte-safety.title: Sécurité des électrolytes
electrolyte-safety.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sécurité des électrolytes, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER give Potassium IV Push (Lethal)
Monitor for cardiac changes with K+ shifts
Fluid follows Sodium

cardiac-meds.title: Medicamentos cardíacos
cardiac-meds.overview: Medicamentos cardíacos é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Hold Beta Blockers if HR < 50 or SBP < 100
ACE inhibitors can cause life-threatening angioedema
Potassium levels affect Digoxin toxicity

anticoagulant-safety.title: Segurança dos anticoagulantes
anticoagulant-safety.overview: Segurança dos anticoagulantes é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Monitor PTT for Heparin, PT/INR for Warfarin
Avoid Vitamin K-rich foods (kale/spinach) on Warfarin
Report black tarry stools immediately

insulin-safety.title: Segurança da insulina
insulin-safety.overview: Segurança da insulina é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Check blood glucose before administration
Rule of 15 for hypoglycemia
Rotate injection sites

lithium-toxicity.title: Toxicité du lithium
lithium-toxicity.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Toxicité du lithium, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Avoid NSAIDs - they increase lithium levels
Ensure 2-3L fluid intake daily
Monitor renal function and thyroid

factor-xa-inhibitors.title: Inhibiteurs du facteur Xa
factor-xa-inhibitors.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Inhibiteurs du facteur Xa, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: No dietary restrictions needed (unlike warfarin which requires consistent Vitamin K intake)
Renal dose adjustment required, especially with declining GFR - monitor kidney function
Shorter half-life than warfarin means missed doses lead to rapid loss of anticoagulant protection
Avoid concurrent use with strong CYP3A4 inhibitors (ketoconazole, ritonavir)
Assess for bleeding clinically - do NOT monitor with PT/INR (not reliable for DOACs)

methotrexate-safety-rpn.title: Méthotrexate – RPN
methotrexate-safety-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Méthotrexate – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Stomatitis is often the first sign of methotrexate toxicity - inspect the mouth at every assessment
Folic acid supplementation reduces side effects but must be taken as prescribed (usually not on methotrexate dosing day)
Report fever immediately - may indicate life-threatening neutropenia
Safe handling: methotrexate is a cytotoxic drug even at low doses
Patient education: avoid alcohol, report any signs of infection or unusual bruising

methotrexate-safety.title: Sécurité du méthotrexate
methotrexate-safety.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sécurité du méthotrexate, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Renal impairment (methotrexate is primarily renally excreted - toxicity risk increases dramatically)
Hepatic disease or heavy alcohol use (additive hepatotoxicity)
Pre-existing myelosuppression or bone marrow dysfunction
Concurrent NSAID use (decreases renal clearance of methotrexate)
Third-space fluid collections (pleural effusion, ascites - acts as drug reservoir)
clinicalPearls: Leucovorin rescue vs folic acid supplementation: leucovorin is for HIGH-dose MTX (oncology) emergencies; folic acid is for LOW-dose chronic therapy side effect reduction
Third-space fluid collections (pleural effusions, ascites) act as methotrexate reservoirs, causing prolonged toxicity - drain before treatment

Ectopic pregnancy protocol: monitor serial beta-hCG levels - expect initial rise before decline; separation pain (days 3-7) is expected, but severe pain or hemodynamic instability requires surgical evaluation
Drug interaction priority: NSAIDs + methotrexate = decreased renal clearance = increased toxicity risk
Pulmonary toxicity (MTX pneumonitis) can occur at ANY dose and is not dose-dependent - maintain high index of suspicion for new respiratory symptoms

methotrexate-safety-np.title: Méthotrexate – IPA
methotrexate-safety-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Méthotrexate – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: MTHFR C677T homozygosity (TT genotype - increased toxicity risk)
Reduced folylpolyglutamate synthetase activity (decreased intracellular drug activation)
GGH (gamma-glutamyl hydrolase) overexpression (increased polyglutamate breakdown, reduced efficacy)
Renal impairment (GFR <30 mL/min - contraindicated; GFR 30-60 requires dose reduction)
Third-space fluid collections acting as drug reservoirs (pleural effusion, ascites)
Concurrent medications affecting renal tubular secretion (NSAIDs, probenecid, penicillins, PPIs)
clinicalPearls: MTHFR C677T TT genotype: consider pre-treatment genotyping - these patients may need 50% dose reduction and enhanced folic acid supplementation (5 mg/week vs standard 1 mg)
Adenosine pathway is the dominant anti-inflammatory mechanism at low doses - this is why methotrexate works for RA despite not being cytotoxic at 7.5-25 mg/week doses

Ectopic pregnancy management: single-dose vs multi-dose protocol selection is based on initial beta-hCG level, ectopic mass size, and presence of fetal cardiac activity - higher values favor multi-dose or surgical approach
Polyglutamation kinetics explain the 6-8 week delay in therapeutic onset for RA - counsel patients on expected timeline

Drug interaction mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis → decreased GFR → decreased MTX clearance → toxicity. This interaction is the most clinically significant and frequently tested.

oxybutynin-rpn.title: Oxybutynin (Ditropan) - Anticholinergic for Overactive Bladder
oxybutynin-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oxybutynin (Ditropan) - Anticholinergic for Overactive Bladder, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Elderly patients (Beers Criteria - HIGH anticholinergic burden risk)
Concurrent use of other anticholinergic medications
clinicalPearls: Beers Criteria flags oxybutynin as HIGH risk in elderly due to anticholinergic burden - causes falls, confusion, and cognitive decline
Dry mouth is the most common side effect - recommend sugar-free candy or gum and frequent sips of water
Educate patients to avoid hot environments because impaired sweating increases heat stroke risk
Monitor post-void residual volume - anticholinergics can worsen urinary retention, especially in men with BPH
Review the full medication list for anticholinergic stacking (diphenhydramine, tricyclics, promethazine combined with oxybutynin)

levetiracetam-rpn.title: Lévetiracétam – RPN
levetiracetam-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lévetiracétam – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Renal impairment (requires dose adjustment - renally eliminated)
History of depression or psychiatric disorders
Suicidal ideation (FDA black box warning for all antiepileptics)
Abrupt discontinuation (risk of rebound seizures or status epilepticus)
Pregnancy (discuss risk-benefit - generally considered safer than many alternatives)
Children and adolescents (higher incidence of behavioral side effects)
clinicalPearls: NEVER stop levetiracetam abruptly - gradual taper over 2 to 4 weeks required to prevent rebound seizures or status epilepticus
Keppra rage refers to behavioral side effects (irritability, aggression, hostility, mood changes) - most common reason for discontinuation
Minimal drug interactions make levetiracetam a preferred choice for patients on multiple medications
Renal elimination means dose must be adjusted for kidney impairment - monitor renal function
FDA black box warning: all antiepileptics carry increased risk of suicidal thoughts and behavior - screen patients regularly

short-acting-insulin-rpn.title: Insuline à action rapide – RPN
short-acting-insulin-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Insuline à action rapide – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Renal impairment (delayed insulin clearance increases hypoglycemia risk)
Irregular meal patterns or skipped meals
NPO status without insulin dose adjustment
Concurrent use of beta-blockers (masks hypoglycemia symptoms)
Stacking insulin doses (giving correction before prior dose peaks)
clinicalPearls: Regular insulin is the ONLY insulin that can be given intravenously - used for DKA and hyperkalemia emergencies
Clear before cloudy - when mixing Regular and NPH in one syringe, always draw up Regular (clear) first to prevent NPH from contaminating the Regular vial
Regular insulin is a CLEAR solution - if it appears cloudy, do NOT use it (NPH is the cloudy insulin)
IV insulin requires an infusion pump, continuous blood glucose monitoring every 1 to 2 hours, and potassium monitoring (insulin drives K+ into cells)
Administer subcutaneous regular insulin 30 minutes before meals - ensure the patient will actually eat to prevent hypoglycemia

infant-reflexes-rpn.title: Infant Reflexes - RPN Foundation
infant-reflexes-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infant Reflexes - RPN Foundation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prematurity (reflexes may be weak or absent)
clinicalPearls: Absent Moro reflex at birth suggests significant neurological compromise - report immediately
Asymmetric Moro reflex suggests brachial plexus injury (Erb palsy) or clavicle fracture on the weak side
Persistence of primitive reflexes beyond normal age suggests upper motor neuron dysfunction or cerebral palsy
Strong sucking and rooting reflexes are essential for successful breastfeeding initiation
Babinski sign is NORMAL in infants - it becomes abnormal (pathological) after 2 years of age when it indicates upper motor neuron lesion
Tonic neck reflex should not be obligatory (infant should be able to turn out of position)

infant-reflexes-np.title: Infant Reflexes - NP Advanced Assessment
infant-reflexes-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infant Reflexes - NP Advanced Assessment, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Metabolic encephalopathy (phenylketonuria, maple syrup urine disease)
clinicalPearls: The 'reflex timeline' is a key NP diagnostic tool: absence at birth = brainstem dysfunction; persistence beyond expected age = cortical dysfunction; asymmetry = focal lesion
Obligatory tonic neck response (cannot turn out of position for more than 30 seconds) is always pathological - distinguish from normal tonic neck reflex which the infant can override
Postural reflexes are cortically mediated and EMERGE as primitive reflexes disappear - their emergence confirms normal cortical maturation
General movements assessment (Prechtl) at 3 months is the strongest early predictor of cerebral palsy - absent fidgety movements have 98% sensitivity for CP
Babinski sign transition from normal (infant) to pathological (after 2 years) marks completion of corticospinal tract myelination

vitals-assessment.title: Évaluation des signes vitaux
vitals-assessment.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Évaluation des signes vitaux, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always compare to baseline
Orthostatic vitals (lying/sitting/standing)
Pain is the 5th vital sign
Document and report trends

wound-care-basics.title: Soins des plaies
wound-care-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins des plaies, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Clean to dirty technique
Irrigate with normal saline at body temperature
Document size/color/drainage/odor
Adequate nutrition (protein/Vitamin C) essential for healing

catheterization.title: Cathétérisme urinaire
catheterization.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cathétérisme urinaire, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always attempt least invasive method first (bladder scan)
Secure catheter to prevent traction injury
Monitor output (>30mL/hr adult)
Daily assessment for continued need

ngtube-care.title: Sonde nasogastrique
ngtube-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sonde nasogastrique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER use auscultation alone to verify placement
Flush with 30mL water every 4 hours and before/after meds
Do not mix medications together through tube
Hold feedings if residual >250-500mL per facility policy

iv-therapy.title: Terapia intravenosa
iv-therapy.overview: Terapia intravenosa é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Change peripheral IV every 72-96 hours per policy
Always check the 5 Rights before administration
Calculate drip rates (gtt/min = volume x drop factor / time)
Never piggyback incompatible medications

blood-transfusion.title: Transfusão sanguínea
blood-transfusion.overview: Transfusão sanguínea é um tema clínico essencial em enfermagem. Esta lição abrange a fisiopatologia, avaliação clínica, intervenções de enfermagem e manejo baseado em evidências para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Stay with client for first 15 minutes
Vitals at baseline/15min/30min/end
Use 0.9% NS only (never LR or D5W)
Infuse within 4 hours
Use blood tubing with 170-micron filter

chest-tube-mgmt.title: Gestion du drain thoracique
chest-tube-mgmt.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Gestion du drain thoracique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Keep drainage system below chest level
Never clamp without an order
Encourage deep breathing and coughing
Milk tubing only if ordered (prevents clots)
Have petroleum gauze at bedside for emergencies

trach-care.title: Soins de trachéotomie
trach-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins de trachéotomie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Keep spare trach and obturator at bedside ALWAYS
Suction only as needed (not on a schedule)
Cuff pressure <25 cmH2O
Communication alternatives (speaking valve, writing board)
Never cut gauze to place around trach (fraying risk)

osteoporosis-basics.title: Ostéoporose
osteoporosis-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ostéoporose, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Weight-bearing exercise strengthens bones
Fall prevention is critical
DEXA screening recommended for women >65
Avoid bending and twisting movements

scoliosis-basics.title: Scoliose
scoliosis-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Scoliose, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: School screening programs for early detection
Monitor closely during growth spurts
Brace compliance is key to preventing surgery
Post-surgical care: log-roll only, monitor neurological status

inhaled-spacers.title: Espaceurs pour inhalateurs
inhaled-spacers.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Espaceurs pour inhalateurs, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of oral thrush from inhaled corticosteroids
clinicalPearls: Use spacer with all MDIs for optimal delivery
Clean spacer weekly with warm soapy water and air dry
Children and elderly benefit most from spacers
Spacer eliminates need for perfect hand-breath coordination

meds-to-infants.title: Médicaments chez le nourrisson
meds-to-infants.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Médicaments chez le nourrisson, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always verify weight-based dose calculation (double-check with another nurse)
Never mix medication in bottle - infant may not finish and won't get full dose
IM injections in vastus lateralis for infants
Always check for allergies with parents/caregivers

cleansing-enemas.title: Lavements évacuateurs
cleansing-enemas.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lavements évacuateurs, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Contraindicated in recent rectal surgery or low platelet count
Stop if client reports severe cramping
Never force the tube - risk of perforation
Document results: amount, color, consistency

wound-irrigation.title: Irrigation des plaies
wound-irrigation.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Irrigation des plaies, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Use warm (body temperature) solution
Wear PPE including splash guard/gown
Document wound appearance before and after irrigation
Pulsatile lavage for deep wounds

vac-dressing.title: Pansement VAC
vac-dressing.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pansement VAC, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Skin grafts and flaps needing protection
clinicalPearls: Foam MUST be cut to fit the wound precisely: it should fill the wound cavity without overlapping onto intact peri-wound skin (causes maceration and pressure injury on healthy tissue). Count the number of foam pieces placed in the wound and document: ALL pieces must be accounted for at each dressing change.

Maintain an airtight seal. Air leaks are the most common issue. Troubleshooting: ensure skin is clean and dry before applying drape, use skin prep/barrier wipes to improve adhesion, apply drape wrinkle-free, use additional drape strips to seal around tubes and irregular wound shapes. A small pinhole in the drape will prevent the machine from reaching target pressure.

Dressing changes are typically every 48-72 hours (Monday-Wednesday-Friday schedule is common). For infected wounds, change every 24 hours. Moisten foam with normal saline 15-30 minutes before removal to prevent it from adhering to granulation tissue (pulling off dry foam tears new granulation and is extremely painful).

Contraindications: malignancy in or near the wound, untreated osteomyelitis, non-enteric and unexplored fistulas, necrotic tissue with eschar (debride first), exposed blood vessels or organs, active uncontrolled bleeding. Anticoagulated patients require extra caution.

The VAC pump must remain on for a minimum of 22 hours per day for therapeutic benefit. Brief disconnections for bathing or ambulation are acceptable but should be minimized. Clamp the tubing before disconnecting to maintain wound bed stability.

Assess wound at each dressing change: measure dimensions (length x width x depth), describe tissue type (% granulation vs. slough vs. necrotic), note drainage color/amount/odor, inspect peri-wound skin. Document and photograph for comparison.

hemodialysis-care.title: Soins d'hémodialyse
hemodialysis-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins d'hémodialyse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER take blood pressure, draw blood, start IVs, or apply tourniquets on the access arm (AV fistula/graft). These can damage the access, promote thrombosis, or introduce infection. The access is the patient's lifeline: protect it.

Assess thrill and bruit EVERY shift. Absence of thrill/bruit = thrombosis until proven otherwise: notify the provider immediately. A change from a strong thrill to a weak one or from a bruit to silence over days suggests developing stenosis.

Post-dialysis nursing care: Monitor vital signs every 15-30 minutes for 1 hour (watch for delayed hypotension as fluid continues to redistribute). Weigh patient (compare to dry weight and pre-dialysis weight: fluid removal should match the difference). Assess access site for bleeding (apply firm pressure for 10-20 minutes after needle removal: patients are heparinized). Monitor for dialysis disequilibrium symptoms (headache, nausea, altered LOC).
