Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Personal history of testicular cancer in contralateral testis
clinicalPearls: A painless testicular mass in a young male is cancer until proven otherwise
Never perform transscrotal biopsy as it risks tumor seeding to inguinal lymph nodes
Testicular cancer has greater than 95% cure rate when detected early, even with metastatic disease

erectile-dysfunction.title: Erectile Dysfunction
erectile-dysfunction.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Erectile Dysfunction, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ED may be the earliest manifestation of systemic atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease
PDE5 inhibitors are absolutely contraindicated with nitrates in any form due to risk of fatal hypotension
Always assess for psychogenic causes, as performance anxiety and depression are common contributing factors

sexually-transmitted-infections.title: Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs)
sexually-transmitted-infections.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Men who have sex with men (MSM)
clinicalPearls: Many STIs are asymptomatic, so routine screening in high-risk populations is essential for early detection
Always screen for co-infection when one STI is identified
Jarisch-Herxheimer reaction (fever, myalgia within 24 hours of syphilis treatment) is expected and self-limiting, not an allergy

menopause.title: Menopause
menopause.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Menopause, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chemotherapy or radiation to the pelvis
clinicalPearls: HRT should be used at the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration necessary
Any postmenopausal bleeding must be evaluated to rule out endometrial cancer
Bone loss accelerates in the first 5-7 years after menopause, making early intervention critical

infertility.title: Infertility
infertility.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Infertility, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Exposure to environmental toxins or radiation
clinicalPearls: Both partners must be evaluated simultaneously, as male factors contribute to approximately 40% of infertility cases
Ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome is a potentially life-threatening complication of ovulation induction requiring close monitoring
Age is the single strongest predictor of female fertility, with significant decline after age 35

ectopic-pregnancy.title: Ectopic Pregnancy
ectopic-pregnancy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ectopic Pregnancy, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prior tubal surgery or tubal ligation
IUD use (does not cause ectopic but if pregnancy occurs with IUD, higher chance of ectopic)
clinicalPearls: Ruptured ectopic pregnancy is a life-threatening surgical emergency requiring immediate intervention
Shoulder pain (Kehr sign) in a pregnant patient with abdominal pain suggests intraperitoneal bleeding irritating the diaphragm
Any reproductive-age woman with abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding must have ectopic pregnancy ruled out with beta-hCG and ultrasound

delayed-puberty.title: Delayed Puberty
delayed-puberty.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Delayed Puberty, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of late puberty (constitutional delay)
Chronic illness (celiac disease, inflammatory bowel disease, cystic fibrosis)
clinicalPearls: Constitutional delay is the most common cause and has a positive family history of late puberty in parents or siblings
Bone age is delayed in constitutional delay but matches chronological age in gonadal failure
Anosmia combined with delayed puberty is a hallmark of Kallmann syndrome and warrants MRI evaluation of olfactory bulbs

precocious-puberty.title: Precocious Puberty
precocious-puberty.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Precocious Puberty, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Female sex (central precocious puberty is more common in girls)
CNS pathology (tumors, hamartomas, hydrocephalus, trauma, radiation)
Exposure to exogenous sex steroids (environmental estrogens, contaminated products)
clinicalPearls: Central precocious puberty in boys always warrants brain MRI as CNS pathology is found in up to 50% of cases
The goal of GnRH agonist therapy is to preserve adult height potential by slowing epiphyseal fusion
Peripheral precocious puberty does not respond to GnRH agonist therapy because it is GnRH-independent

dysmenorrhea.title: Dysmenorrhea
dysmenorrhea.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Dysmenorrhea, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Primary dysmenorrhea is caused by prostaglandin overproduction and typically responds to NSAIDs when initiated before pain onset
Dysmenorrhea that progressively worsens, begins after age 25, or is associated with dyspareunia should raise suspicion for secondary causes, especially endometriosis
Heat therapy at 40°C has been shown to be as effective as ibuprofen for primary dysmenorrhea pain relief

abnormal-uterine-bleeding.title: Abnormal Uterine Bleeding (AUB)
abnormal-uterine-bleeding.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Abnormal Uterine Bleeding (AUB), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: PALM-COEIN is the standardized FIGO classification: Polyp, Adenomyosis, Leiomyoma, Malignancy (structural) and Coagulopathy, Ovulatory dysfunction, Endometrial, Iatrogenic, Not yet classified (non-structural)
Heavy menstrual bleeding from menarche should prompt evaluation for an underlying coagulopathy, especially von Willebrand disease, which affects up to 13% of women with menorrhagia
Any postmenopausal bleeding requires endometrial biopsy to rule out endometrial cancer until proven otherwise

vaginitis.title: Vaginitis
vaginitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Vaginitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Antibiotic use (disrupts Lactobacillus, predisposes to BV and candidiasis)
Multiple or new sexual partners (BV, trichomoniasis)
clinicalPearls: BV is the most common cause of vaginal discharge in reproductive-age women and is characterized by fishy odor, elevated pH, and clue cells
Vulvovaginal candidiasis maintains a normal vaginal pH (3.8-4.5), which differentiates it from BV and trichomoniasis
Trichomoniasis is a sexually transmitted infection requiring partner treatment, while BV and uncomplicated candidiasis are not classified as STIs

bacterial-vaginosis.title: Bacterial Vaginosis
bacterial-vaginosis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bacterial Vaginosis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Douching (disrupts vaginal pH and Lactobacillus colonization)
African American ethnicity (higher prevalence linked to microbiome community state types)
clinicalPearls: BV is not considered a sexually transmitted infection but is associated with sexual activity and is rare in women who have never been sexually active
BV increases the risk of acquiring HIV, HSV-2, Chlamydia, and gonorrhea due to loss of the protective Lactobacillus-dominant barrier
Recurrence rates exceed 50% within 12 months due to persistent biofilm that harbors Gardnerella and Atopobium vaginae despite antibiotic treatment
Treatment of male sexual partners has not been shown to reduce recurrence in female partners in clinical trials
BV in pregnancy is associated with preterm birth, premature rupture of membranes, chorioamnionitis, and postpartum endometritis

cervicitis.title: Cervicitis
cervicitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cervicitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age under 25 years (highest prevalence of Chlamydia and gonorrhea)
Co-infection with other STIs (increases cervical vulnerability)
clinicalPearls: Up to 70-80% of chlamydial cervicitis is asymptomatic, making screening essential for sexually active women under 25 and those with risk factors
Mucopurulent cervicitis should be treated empirically before NAAT results return due to the risk of ascending infection causing PID
Cervicitis significantly increases HIV acquisition risk by 3-5 fold due to disrupted mucosal barrier and increased CCR5+ target cells
Untreated chlamydial cervicitis progresses to PID in approximately 10-15% of cases, with subsequent risks of tubal factor infertility, ectopic pregnancy, and chronic pelvic pain

vulvodynia.title: Vulvodynia
vulvodynia.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Vulvodynia, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of recurrent vulvovaginal candidiasis (epithelial inflammation may trigger nerve sensitization)
History of early or prolonged oral contraceptive use (hormonal influence on vestibular mucosa)
Pelvic floor muscle dysfunction or hypertonicity
History of anxiety, depression, or catastrophizing pain behaviors
Genetic polymorphisms affecting inflammatory cytokine production (IL-1β, IL-1RA)
History of childhood physical or sexual abuse
Comorbid chronic pain conditions (fibromyalgia, irritable bowel syndrome, interstitial cystitis)
clinicalPearls: Vulvodynia affects approximately 8-16% of women at some point in their lifetime, yet the average time to diagnosis is 4-5 years due to lack of awareness and visible pathology
Provoked vestibulodynia (formerly vulvar vestibulitis syndrome) is the most common subtype, characterized by pain with vestibular touch or pressure
Normal-appearing vulvar tissue on examination does not rule out vulvodynia - the pathology is at the microscopic nerve fiber and mast cell level
Multimodal treatment combining pharmacotherapy, pelvic floor physical therapy, and cognitive behavioral therapy produces the best outcomes

bartholinitis.title: Bartholinitis
bartholinitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bartholinitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Age 20-30 years (peak incidence during reproductive years)
History of prior Bartholin cyst or abscess (high recurrence rate)
clinicalPearls: Bartholin gland cysts and abscesses account for approximately 2% of all gynecologic visits in reproductive-age women
Simple I&D without catheter placement has a recurrence rate of up to 50%, while Word catheter placement reduces recurrence to approximately 10-15%
In women over 40 years old, any new or persistent Bartholin gland mass should be biopsied to rule out Bartholin gland carcinoma (rare but important differential)

The Word catheter is a small balloon-tipped catheter inserted into the abscess cavity after drainage and inflated with 2-3 mL of saline; it remains for 4-6 weeks to allow epithelialization of a new drainage tract

uterine-prolapse.title: Uterine Prolapse
uterine-prolapse.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Uterine Prolapse, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Vaginal childbirth (especially prolonged labor, macrosomia, operative vaginal delivery)
Multiparity (risk increases with each vaginal delivery)
Advanced age and postmenopausal estrogen deficiency
Connective tissue disorders (Ehlers-Danlos syndrome, Marfan syndrome)
Prior pelvic surgery (hysterectomy disrupts Level I support)
Family history of pelvic organ prolapse
clinicalPearls: Uterine prolapse affects up to 50% of parous women to some degree, though only 10-20% are symptomatic enough to seek treatment
The POP-Q system is the standardized, objective measurement system for prolapse staging, replacing older subjective grading systems
Pessaries successfully manage symptoms in approximately 70-90% of women who are properly fitted, serving as both a bridge to surgery and a long-term treatment option
Kegel exercises performed correctly and consistently for at least 3 months can improve prolapse symptoms by one POP-Q stage in mild to moderate cases

benign-ovarian-cysts.title: Benign Ovarian Cysts
benign-ovarian-cysts.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Benign Ovarian Cysts, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Reproductive age (functional cysts occur during ovulatory years)
Ovulation induction medications (clomiphene, gonadotropins increase multi-follicular development)
Tamoxifen use (associated with ovarian cyst formation)
clinicalPearls: Functional ovarian cysts are extremely common and usually resolve without intervention within 1-3 menstrual cycles; reassurance is key
Ovarian torsion is a surgical emergency - do not delay intervention for imaging if clinical suspicion is high; normal Doppler flow does not exclude torsion due to dual ovarian blood supply

Dermoid cysts (mature teratomas) contain fat, hair, and teeth and have a characteristic echogenic appearance on ultrasound; they carry a 3-5% risk of torsion and should be removed if >5 cm or symptomatic
Any ovarian cyst in a postmenopausal woman warrants evaluation for malignancy with CA-125, ultrasound characterization, and possible surgical referral

endometrial-polyp.title: Endometrial Polyp
endometrial-polyp.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Endometrial Polyp, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Perimenopause and postmenopause (declining progesterone levels with relative estrogen excess)
Obesity (peripheral aromatization of androgens to estrogen in adipose tissue)
Tamoxifen therapy (estrogen-agonist effect on endometrium, 20-35% incidence)
Hormone replacement therapy with estrogen (especially without adequate progestogen opposition)
Polycystic ovary syndrome (chronic anovulation, unopposed estrogen)
clinicalPearls: Any postmenopausal vaginal bleeding requires investigation to rule out endometrial cancer - endometrial polyps are the most common benign cause but malignancy must be excluded
Saline infusion sonography (SIS) is superior to standard transvaginal ultrasound for detecting intracavitary polyps, with sensitivity approaching 95%
The malignant transformation rate of endometrial polyps is approximately 0.5-3%, with highest risk in postmenopausal women, symptomatic polyps >1.5 cm, and tamoxifen users
Hysteroscopic polypectomy is both diagnostic and therapeutic - it allows direct visualization, targeted removal, and histopathologic examination in a single procedure

galactorrhea.title: Galactorrhea
galactorrhea.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Galactorrhea, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Medications that block dopamine (antipsychotics, metoclopramide, domperidone)
clinicalPearls: Prolactinomas are the most common functioning pituitary adenoma - over 90% of microprolactinomas respond to dopamine agonist therapy without surgery
Always check TSH before imaging - hypothyroidism-induced hyperprolactinemia resolves with thyroid hormone replacement alone
Medication-induced hyperprolactinemia is extremely common - antipsychotics are the most frequent cause, and switching to aripiprazole (partial D2 agonist) can resolve symptoms

urethritis.title: Urethritis
urethritis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Urethritis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: New sexual partner within past 60 days
Male sex (more commonly symptomatic in males)
clinicalPearls: Always treat for both gonorrhea and chlamydia when gonorrhea is confirmed, as coinfection rates are 20-40%
Chlamydia is the most common bacterial STI worldwide and is often asymptomatic - screening is essential for sexually active individuals under 25
Mycoplasma genitalium is increasingly recognized as a major cause of persistent or recurrent non-gonococcal urethritis resistant to standard doxycycline therapy

urethral-stricture.title: Urethral Stricture
urethral-stricture.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Urethral Stricture, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Hypospadias repair or prior urethral surgery
clinicalPearls: Urethral stricture has a high recurrence rate after dilation and DVIU (50-70% within 2 years) - urethroplasty has the best long-term success rate (85-95%)
The most common cause in developed countries is iatrogenic injury from catheterization or endoscopic procedures - use the smallest catheter size possible and adequate lubrication
Urinary retention from stricture is a urologic emergency requiring prompt intervention - do NOT force a catheter through a stricture as this can create a false passage

phimosis.title: Phimosis
phimosis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Phimosis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Poor hygiene leading to chronic inflammation
Age - physiologic phimosis is normal in children under 3
clinicalPearls: Never forcefully retract the foreskin in a child with physiologic phimosis - this causes microtears, bleeding, and scarring that converts physiologic phimosis to pathologic phimosis
Topical corticosteroid therapy is first-line for pathologic phimosis with a success rate of 60-90%, reducing the need for circumcision
Paraphimosis (foreskin trapped behind glans) is a urologic emergency - the constricting band causes venous congestion, edema, and potential ischemic necrosis of the glans if not reduced promptly

priapism.title: Priapism
priapism.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Priapism, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Sickle cell disease or trait (most common cause in children)
Intracavernosal injection therapy for erectile dysfunction (alprostadil)
clinicalPearls: Ischemic priapism is a urologic emergency analogous to compartment syndrome - every hour of delay reduces the chance of preserving erectile function
The glans penis and corpus spongiosum remain SOFT in priapism because they have separate venous drainage - a fully rigid glans suggests a different diagnosis

Sickle cell disease accounts for approximately 65% of priapism cases in children - all children with sickle cell disease and their families should receive anticipatory guidance about priapism recognition and the importance of emergency evaluation within 4 hours

balanitis.title: Balanitis
balanitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Balanitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Broad-spectrum antibiotic use (disrupts normal flora, promotes Candida)
Irritant exposure (harsh soaps, latex condoms, spermicides)
clinicalPearls: Recurrent candidal balanitis in a previously healthy male should prompt screening for diabetes mellitus - balanitis may be the initial presentation of undiagnosed type 2 diabetes

Zoon balanitis (plasma cell balanitis) presents as a shiny, moist, cayenne pepper-speckled erythematous plaque on the glans - it is benign but can mimic erythroplasia of Queyrat (carcinoma in situ), so biopsy is essential for differentiation
Circumcision is the definitive treatment for recurrent balanitis - it eliminates the preputial microenvironment that promotes microbial colonization

cryptorchidism.title: Cryptorchidism
cryptorchidism.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cryptorchidism, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Prematurity and low birth weight (most significant risk factor)
Maternal exposure to endocrine disruptors (pesticides, phthalates, DES)
Maternal diabetes mellitus or gestational diabetes
Genetic syndromes (Klinefelter syndrome, Prader-Willi syndrome, Kallmann syndrome)
clinicalPearls: Orchiopexy should be performed by 12 months of age - germ cell degeneration from elevated temperature begins as early as 6 months and becomes significant by 12-18 months, permanently reducing fertility potential

Cryptorchidism increases testicular cancer risk 5-10 fold even after orchiopexy - the risk is intrinsic to the dysgenetic gonad, not solely due to position; orchiopexy allows surveillance by placing the testis in a palpable location
Bilateral non-palpable testes in a newborn with hypospadias is a disorder of sex development until proven otherwise - urgent karyotype and endocrine evaluation are required

orchitis.title: Orchitis
orchitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Orchitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Mumps infection in postpubertal males (20-30% develop orchitis)
Urinary tract infection with vesicoureteral reflux
clinicalPearls: The CRITICAL differentiation is orchitis versus testicular torsion - torsion is a surgical emergency requiring detorsion within 6 hours to save the testis; when in doubt, surgical exploration is mandatory
Mumps orchitis occurs 4-8 days after parotid swelling and causes testicular atrophy in 30-50% of affected testes - bilateral involvement can result in infertility
Doppler ultrasound showing INCREASED blood flow to the affected testis confirms orchitis; ABSENT blood flow suggests torsion and requires emergent surgical exploration

prostatitis.title: Prostatitis
prostatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prostatitis, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Urinary tract infections (most common bacterial source)
Prostatic calculi (serve as nidus for biofilm formation)
clinicalPearls: NEVER perform vigorous prostatic massage in acute bacterial prostatitis (Category I) - the inflamed, infected prostate can release bacteria into the bloodstream, causing bacteremia and potentially sepsis

Chronic prostatitis/CPPS (Category III) accounts for 90-95% of prostatitis cases and often responds best to a multimodal approach combining alpha-blockers, anti-inflammatories, pelvic floor physical therapy, and psychological support rather than antibiotics alone

Fluoroquinolones achieve the highest prostatic tissue penetration of any antibiotic class - this is why they are first-line despite FDA black box warnings for serious adverse effects; the 4-6 week treatment duration is essential because shorter courses lead to relapse from bacteria harbored in prostatic tissue

abg-advanced-np.title: Advanced ABG Interpretation (NP)
abg-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Advanced ABG Interpretation (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Critical illness requiring frequent ABG monitoring
clinicalPearls: ROME: Respiratory Opposite (pH↑ PaCO2↓), Metabolic Equal (pH↓ HCO3↓)
Always calculate anion gap in metabolic acidosis  -  MUDPILES: Methanol, Uremia, DKA, Propylene glycol, INH/Iron, Lactic acidosis, Ethylene glycol, Salicylates
A normal pH with abnormal PaCO2 and HCO3 indicates full compensation or a mixed disorder  -  clinical context is essential

airway-assessment-rn.title: Comprehensive Airway Assessment (RN)
airway-assessment-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Comprehensive Airway Assessment (RN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Trauma to face, neck, or chest
clinicalPearls: Always assess airway FIRST in any deteriorating patient (ABC approach)
Stridor indicates ≥ 50% airway obstruction  -  this is a medical emergency
If a patient cannot protect their airway (absent gag, GCS ≤ 8), anticipate the need for intubation and notify the provider immediately

alpha-1-antitrypsin-deficiency-np.title: Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency (NP)
alpha-1-antitrypsin-deficiency-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of early-onset emphysema or liver disease
clinicalPearls: Screen ALL COPD patients under 45 and those with basilar emphysema for AAT deficiency
AAT deficiency is the most common genetic cause of liver disease in children
Augmentation therapy is NOT a cure  -  it slows progression; smoking cessation remains the single most important intervention

ards-management-np.title: ARDS Advanced Management (NP)
ards-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de ARDS Advanced Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Lung-protective ventilation saves lives: 6 mL/kg IBW, plateau < 30  -  permissive hypercapnia is acceptable
Prone positioning for 16+ hours reduces mortality in moderate-severe ARDS  -  use early
Address the underlying cause (sepsis, aspiration) aggressively while supporting oxygenation

asthma-emergency-np.title: Asthma Emergency Management (NP)
asthma-emergency-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Asthma Emergency Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: ICU admission for asthma in past year
≥ 2 hospitalizations or ≥ 3 ED visits in past year
Comorbid psychiatric illness or substance abuse
Low socioeconomic status with limited healthcare access
clinicalPearls: A rising PaCO2 in an asthma exacerbation is a pre-arrest sign  -  the patient is tiring and losing the ability to compensate
Pulsus paradoxus > 25 mmHg indicates severe air trapping and is associated with impending respiratory arrest
If intubation is needed, use low respiratory rates, long expiratory times, and permissive hypercapnia to avoid auto-PEEP and barotrauma

asthma-outpatient-np.title: Asthma Outpatient Management (NP)
asthma-outpatient-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Asthma Outpatient Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: GINA 2023 no longer recommends SABA-only treatment  -  even Step 1 patients should use ICS-formoterol PRN
The Asthma Action Plan is the single most important patient education tool  -  review at every visit
Always assess inhaler technique at every visit; up to 80% of patients use inhalers incorrectly

bronchiectasis-management-np.title: Bronchiectasis Management (NP)
bronchiectasis-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bronchiectasis Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Cystic fibrosis (most common cause in children)
clinicalPearls: Airway clearance is the cornerstone of bronchiectasis management  -  prescribe a regimen and ensure patient adherence
Always rule out NTM (nontuberculous mycobacteria) with sputum AFB culture before starting macrolide prophylaxis
Chronic Pseudomonas colonization worsens prognosis  -  treat aggressively with inhaled antibiotics

bronchiectasis-rpn.title: Bronchiectasis Basics (RPN/LVN)
bronchiectasis-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bronchiectasis Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of severe or recurrent pneumonia
clinicalPearls: Sputum changes (increased volume, darker color, blood-tinged) often indicate an acute exacerbation  -  report these findings promptly
Airway clearance is best performed on a regular schedule, not just when symptomatic
Good oral hygiene reduces bacterial load in the mouth and may decrease risk of respiratory infections

bronchopulmonary-dysplasia-rpn.title: Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (RPN/LVN)
bronchopulmonary-dysplasia-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: BPD infants need more calories (120-150 kcal/kg/day) than healthy neonates because increased work of breathing raises metabolic demands
Cluster care activities to allow rest periods and reduce oxygen consumption
Educate parents early about the possibility of home oxygen therapy and equipment use

chest-tube-basics-rpn.title: Chest Tube Basics (RPN/LVN)
chest-tube-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Chest Tube Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER clamp a chest tube unless specifically ordered  -  clamping can cause tension pneumothorax
If the chest tube becomes dislodged, cover the site immediately with petroleum gauze and occlusive dressing taped on 3 sides, and call for help
Tidaling is NORMAL and indicates a patent system  -  absence of tidaling may indicate tube obstruction or full lung re-expansion

copd-outpatient-np.title: COPD Outpatient Management (NP)
copd-outpatient-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de COPD Outpatient Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Smoking cessation is the ONLY intervention proven to slow FEV1 decline in COPD  -  make it a priority at every visit
ICS in COPD increases pneumonia risk  -  reserve for patients with eosinophils ≥ 300 or asthma-COPD overlap
Refer ALL symptomatic COPD patients to pulmonary rehabilitation  -  it reduces hospitalizations and improves quality of life

cystic-fibrosis-adult-np.title: Cystic Fibrosis Adult Management (NP)
cystic-fibrosis-adult-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cystic Fibrosis Adult Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Autosomal recessive inheritance (carrier frequency 1:25 in Caucasians)
clinicalPearls: Trikafta has transformed CF care  -  check eligibility for ALL CF patients based on CFTR genotype
CF patients need HIGH-calorie, HIGH-fat diets (120-150% of normal caloric intake)  -  do NOT restrict fat
Chronic Pseudomonas infection requires aggressive treatment cycles; eradication protocols for new Pseudomonas acquisition should be initiated promptly

cystic-fibrosis-basics-rpn.title: Cystic Fibrosis Basics (RPN/LVN)
cystic-fibrosis-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cystic Fibrosis Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Pancreatic enzymes must be given with EVERY meal and snack  -  even a small snack needs enzymes
CF patients need MORE calories than normal (high-calorie, high-fat diet)  -  never restrict fat in CF
Salty-tasting skin is a classic sign of CF in infants  -  the sweat chloride test confirms diagnosis

cystic-fibrosis-peds-rpn.title: Cystic Fibrosis Pediatrics (RPN/LVN)
cystic-fibrosis-peds-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Cystic Fibrosis Pediatrics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Failure to thrive despite adequate feeding
clinicalPearls: In infants, mix enzyme beads with applesauce (acidic food), NEVER with formula or milk (alkaline pH destroys the enteric coating)
CF children should NOT share rooms or play areas with other CF children  -  cross-infection with resistant organisms can be fatal
A newborn with meconium ileus (failure to pass meconium within 24-48 hours) should be evaluated for CF with sweat chloride test

hemothorax-management-np.title: Hemothorax Management (NP)
hemothorax-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Hemothorax Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Chest tube output > 200 mL/hr for 2-4 hours or > 1500 mL initially = surgical emergency  -  notify thoracic surgery immediately
Always autotransfuse chest tube blood when possible in massive hemothorax  -  it's the patient's own blood
TXA must be given within 3 hours of injury for mortality benefit  -  later administration may increase mortality

interstitial-lung-disease-np.title: Interstitial Lung Disease (NP)
interstitial-lung-disease-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Interstitial Lung Disease (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: 'Velcro-like' crackles at the lung bases are the hallmark of pulmonary fibrosis  -  always investigate with HRCT
Antifibrotics slow progression but do NOT reverse fibrosis  -  early referral is critical
A 10% decline in FVC over 12 months in IPF is associated with increased mortality  -  this should trigger transplant referral

lung-auscultation.title: Lung Auscultation
lung-auscultation.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lung Auscultation, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always auscultate side-to-side for comparison  -  asymmetry is clinically significant
Crackles that clear with coughing suggest secretions; crackles that persist suggest pulmonary edema or fibrosis
Diminished breath sounds can be just as significant as adventitious sounds  -  always report absent or markedly reduced sounds

lung-cancer-basics-rpn.title: Lung Cancer Basics (RPN/LVN)
lung-cancer-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lung Cancer Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Previous radiation therapy to the chest
clinicalPearls: A new persistent cough or change in chronic cough character in a smoker warrants investigation  -  report promptly
Hemoptysis in a smoker or former smoker is lung cancer until proven otherwise  -  report immediately
Always assess for pain in lung cancer patients  -  undertreated cancer pain significantly impairs quality of life

lung-cancer-staging-np.title: Lung Cancer Staging & Treatment (NP)
lung-cancer-staging-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lung Cancer Staging & Treatment (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: History of COPD or pulmonary fibrosis
clinicalPearls: ALL patients with advanced NSCLC must have molecular profiling (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, KRAS, PD-L1) before starting treatment  -  targeted therapy can dramatically improve outcomes
LDCT screening: annual for adults 50-80 with ≥ 20 pack-year history who currently smoke or quit within 15 years
Early palliative care integration improves survival and quality of life in advanced lung cancer (Temel study)

lung-sounds-assessment-rpn.title: Lung Sounds Assessment (RPN/LVN)
lung-sounds-assessment-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Lung Sounds Assessment (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Absent breath sounds are just as concerning as abnormal sounds  -  ALWAYS report diminished or absent lung sounds
Document the location of adventitious sounds precisely (e.g., 'fine crackles bilateral lower lobes posteriorly')
Compare breath sounds side-to-side  -  unilateral changes suggest a localized problem (pneumothorax, effusion, consolidation)

non-invasive-ventilation-modes-np.title: Non-Invasive Ventilation Modes (NP)
non-invasive-ventilation-modes-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Non-Invasive Ventilation Modes (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Acute COPD exacerbation with respiratory acidosis
clinicalPearls: NIV for acute COPD exacerbation reduces intubation rates by 60% and mortality by 50%  -  use early for pH 7.25-7.35 with hypercapnia
If no improvement in ABG within 1-2 hours of NIV, do not delay intubation  -  NIV failure with delayed intubation worsens outcomes
CPAP for acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema is Class I evidence  -  it works as a 'pneumatic splint' keeping alveoli open and reducing preload

occupational-lung-disease-np.title: Occupational Lung Disease (NP)
occupational-lung-disease-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Occupational Lung Disease (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inadequate respiratory protection in the workplace
clinicalPearls: Occupational history is the MOST important diagnostic tool  -  always ask: 'What type of work have you done and for how long?'
Silicosis increases TB risk 3-fold  -  always screen for latent TB in silicosis patients
Asbestos exposure increases lung cancer risk 5× and mesothelioma risk even decades after exposure  -  ensure ongoing cancer surveillance

oxygen-therapy-basics.title: Oxygen Therapy Basics
oxygen-therapy-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oxygen Therapy Basics, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Nasal cannula rule of thumb: each L/min increases FiO2 by ~4% above room air (21% + 4% per liter)
Pulse oximetry can be falsely elevated in carbon monoxide poisoning  -  order co-oximetry if CO exposure suspected
Non-rebreather mask provides the highest FiO2 of simple devices (~80-95%)  -  if a patient needs this, consider higher-level interventions

oxygen-therapy-setup-rpn.title: Oxygen Therapy Setup (RPN/LVN)
oxygen-therapy-setup-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oxygen Therapy Setup (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Always verify the 5 Rights of oxygen therapy: right patient, right device, right flow rate, right route, right documentation
Humidify oxygen at flow rates > 4 L/min to prevent drying and irritation of nasal and oral mucosa
Oxygen is a medication  -  it requires a prescription, and flow rate changes require a new order

peds-respiratory.title: Pediatric Respiratory: RSV & Croup
peds-respiratory.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pediatric Respiratory: RSV & Croup, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: RSV: Suctioning before feeding is the priority
Croup: Cool mist or cold air can help relieve symptoms
Monitor for dehydration due to poor feeding

pleural-effusion-np.title: Pleural Effusion Management (NP)
pleural-effusion-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pleural Effusion Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Heart failure (most common cause of transudative)
clinicalPearls: Light's criteria are the gold standard for classifying pleural effusions  -  always send protein and LDH with pleural fluid analysis
Limit thoracentesis to 1-1.5 L per session to prevent re-expansion pulmonary edema  -  stop if patient develops chest tightness or cough
A pleural fluid pH < 7.2 or glucose < 60 mg/dL in a parapneumonic effusion indicates complicated effusion requiring tube thoracostomy

pneumonia-management-np.title: Pneumonia Management (NP)
pneumonia-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pneumonia Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Recent hospitalization (> 48 hours in past 90 days)
clinicalPearls: CURB-65 scoring: Confusion, Urea > 7 mmol/L, RR ≥ 30, BP < 90/60, age ≥ 65  -  score ≥ 3 requires ICU consideration
Procalcitonin < 0.25 µg/L suggests viral etiology and can guide antibiotic de-escalation
Duration of therapy for uncomplicated CAP is now 5 days (ATS/IDSA 2019)  -  longer courses increase resistance and C. difficile risk without improving outcomes

pulmonary-fibrosis-basics-rpn.title: Pulmonary Fibrosis Basics (RPN/LVN)
pulmonary-fibrosis-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pulmonary Fibrosis Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Fine 'Velcro-like' crackles at the lung bases that do not clear with coughing are the hallmark of pulmonary fibrosis  -  always report these
Patients may desaturate significantly with activity even if resting SpO2 is normal  -  always check SpO2 with ambulation
This is a progressive disease  -  focus on quality of life, symptom management, and emotional support

respiratory-assessment-rn.title: Respiratory Assessment Protocol (RN)
respiratory-assessment-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratory Assessment Protocol (RN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Use SBAR for all respiratory change communications: Situation (what's happening), Background (relevant history), Assessment (your clinical interpretation), Recommendation (what you think should happen)
NEWS score ≥ 5 = urgent clinical review needed; NEWS ≥ 7 = emergency response needed  -  do not delay escalation
Post-opioid respiratory monitoring: assess RR, sedation level, and SpO2 every 1 hour for the first 24 hours of parenteral opioid initiation

respiratory-failure-np.title: Respiratory Failure Management (NP)
respiratory-failure-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratory Failure Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Elevated A-a gradient distinguishes pulmonary causes of hypoxemia (V/Q mismatch, shunt) from hypoventilation  -  if A-a gradient is normal with Type II failure, the cause is purely hypoventilation
Do NOT delay intubation when it is needed  -  NIV failure with delayed intubation worsens outcomes
Paradoxical abdominal breathing (abdomen moves in during inspiration) indicates diaphragmatic fatigue and is a pre-arrest sign

respiratory-mechanics-np.title: Respiratory Mechanics (NP)
respiratory-mechanics-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratory Mechanics (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Massive obesity restricting chest wall movement
clinicalPearls: Plateau pressure reflects alveolar pressure and lung compliance  -  keep < 30 cmH2O to prevent barotrauma
Peak-plateau gradient reflects airway resistance  -  if elevated, think bronchospasm, secretions, or kinked/obstructed ETT
Auto-PEEP causes hemodynamic instability (impairs venous return)  -  treat by reducing RR, increasing expiratory time, and treating bronchospasm

respiratory-pattern-rpn.title: Respiratory Patterns (RPN/LVN)
respiratory-pattern-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratory Patterns (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Count the respiratory rate without telling the patient (pretend to take pulse while counting breaths)  -  awareness changes the pattern
Kussmaul breathing = deep and rapid = think metabolic acidosis (DKA, renal failure, lactic acidosis)
Cheyne-Stokes with periods of apnea may be a normal finding during sleep in the elderly but is concerning in awake patients (suggests heart failure or neurological injury)

sarcoidosis-pulmonary-np.title: Pulmonary Sarcoidosis (NP)
sarcoidosis-pulmonary-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pulmonary Sarcoidosis (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: African American descent (3× higher incidence, more severe)
Possible environmental triggers (infectious agents, organic dusts)
clinicalPearls: Erythema nodosum + bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy (Löfgren syndrome) has an excellent prognosis with > 90% spontaneous resolution  -  often does NOT require treatment
Cardiac sarcoidosis can cause fatal arrhythmias  -  screen with ECG at baseline and consider advanced cardiac imaging if symptoms
Avoid excess vitamin D supplementation in sarcoidosis  -  granulomas produce 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D, increasing risk of hypercalcemia

tb-basics-rpn.title: Tuberculosis Basics (RPN/LVN)
tb-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tuberculosis Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Close contact with active TB case
clinicalPearls: TB requires AIRBORNE precautions  -  N95 respirator for staff, negative pressure room for patient, door closed at all times
DOT (Directly Observed Therapy) means physically watching the patient swallow EVERY dose  -  this is the gold standard for ensuring treatment adherence
The patient remains in isolation until 3 consecutive sputum specimens are negative for AFB  -  this confirms they are no longer infectious

tb-management-advanced-np.title: TB Management Advanced (NP)
tb-management-advanced-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de TB Management Advanced (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Treatment failure or relapse of previous TB treatment
Immigration from MDR-TB endemic areas (Russia, India, China, South Africa)
Prior TB treatment with poor adherence
clinicalPearls: GeneXpert MTB/RIF can detect rifampin resistance in 2 hours  -  order on ALL new TB cases to rule out MDR-TB early
The BPaL regimen (bedaquiline + pretomanid + linezolid) has revolutionized MDR-TB treatment, reducing duration from 20+ months to 6 months with >90% cure rates
All drug-resistant TB cases must be reported to public health and managed by or in consultation with TB specialists

tb-management-basics-rpn.title: TB Management Basics (RPN/LVN)
tb-management-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de TB Management Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Non-adherence to medication regimen (most important modifiable risk factor)
clinicalPearls: RIPE = Rifampin, Isoniazid, Pyrazinamide, Ethambutol  -  remember this as the standard first-line TB regimen
Hepatotoxicity is the most dangerous shared side effect of INH, Rifampin, and Pyrazinamide  -  report jaundice, dark urine, or severe nausea IMMEDIATELY
Treatment is typically 6 months minimum  -  educate patients that stopping early creates drug-resistant 'super bugs' that are much harder to treat

tb-management-np.title: TB Management (NP)
tb-management-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de TB Management (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Latent TB with positive TST/IGRA and risk factors for reactivation
Recent TB contacts with positive screening
clinicalPearls: The 4-month rifampin regimen for latent TB has better completion rates and fewer hepatotoxicity events than 9-month INH  -  consider as first-line
Rifampin is a potent CYP450 inducer  -  ALWAYS check drug interactions (OCP, warfarin, HIV meds, methadone, azoles)
Hold all hepatotoxic TB drugs if ALT > 5× ULN (asymptomatic) or > 3× ULN with symptoms  -  reintroduce one at a time starting with rifampin

tracheobronchial-injury-np.title: Tracheobronchial Injury (NP)
tracheobronchial-injury-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tracheobronchial Injury (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: A persistent air leak despite a properly functioning chest tube should raise suspicion for tracheobronchial injury
The 'fallen lung' sign on CXR (lung falls laterally rather than collapsing toward the hilum) is pathognomonic for complete bronchial rupture
Avoid positive pressure ventilation if possible until the injury is characterized  -  it can worsen air leak and cause tension pneumothorax

tracheostomy-basics-rpn.title: Tracheostomy Basics (RPN/LVN)
tracheostomy-basics-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tracheostomy Basics (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ALWAYS keep a spare tracheostomy tube (same size AND one size smaller) plus the obturator at the bedside  -  accidental decannulation requires immediate reinsertion
Suction only as NEEDED (not routinely)  -  assess for clinical indications (audible secretions, desaturation, visible secretions in tube)
Tracheoinnominate fistula presents as massive pulsatile arterial bleeding from the stoma  -  hyperinflate the cuff to tamponade and call for emergency surgery

tracheostomy-care-rpn.title: Tracheostomy Care (RPN/LVN)
tracheostomy-care-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tracheostomy Care (RPN/LVN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER cut gauze to use as a trach dressing  -  loose threads can be aspirated into the airway; always use pre-cut trach dressings
Trach tie changes require TWO people  -  one holds the tube securely while the other changes the ties to prevent accidental decannulation
One finger should fit between the trach tie and the patient's neck  -  too tight causes skin breakdown, too loose risks decannulation

ventilator-assessment-rn.title: Ventilator Assessment (RN)
ventilator-assessment-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ventilator Assessment (RN), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER silence a ventilator alarm without assessing the patient first  -  alarms are safety alerts, not annoyances
DOPE mnemonic for acute ventilator deterioration: Displacement (of tube), Obstruction (mucus plug), Pneumothorax, Equipment failure  -  assess systematically
Daily sedation interruption and SBT assessment are evidence-based practices that reduce ventilator days and ICU length of stay

ventilator-associated-pneumonia-np.title: Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia (NP)
ventilator-associated-pneumonia-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ventilator-Associated Pneumonia (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Duration of mechanical ventilation (risk increases 1-3% per day)
clinicalPearls: VAP prevention is far better than treatment  -  strict adherence to the VAP bundle reduces incidence by 50-70%
De-escalate antibiotics within 48-72 hours based on cultures  -  prolonged broad-spectrum therapy increases resistance and C. difficile risk
7 days of antibiotics is sufficient for most VAP  -  longer courses have not shown benefit and increase adverse effects

ventilator-bundle-np.title: Ventilator Bundle: Evidence-Based Practice (NP)
ventilator-bundle-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ventilator Bundle: Evidence-Based Practice (NP), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The ventilator bundle works as a BUNDLE  -  individual elements help, but maximum benefit comes from implementing ALL components together consistently
Daily SBT assessment is the single most impactful intervention for reducing ventilator days  -  every additional day on the ventilator increases complications and mortality
Multidisciplinary rounds (RN, RT, MD/NP, pharmacist) with a daily checklist are the most effective way to ensure bundle compliance

copd-exacerbation.title: Exacerbation de la BPCO
copd-exacerbation.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Exacerbation de la BPCO, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Keep O2 sats between 88-92% for chronic retainers
Avoid high-flow oxygen which can suppress respiratory drive
Position: High-Fowler's or Tripod

asthma-emergency.title: Urgence asthmatique
asthma-emergency.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Urgence asthmatique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Silent chest = imminent respiratory arrest
Peak Flow Meter helps track control
Avoid beta-blockers in asthma patients

epiglottitis-peds.title: Épiglottite pédiatrique
epiglottitis-peds.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Épiglottite pédiatrique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: NEVER examine the throat with a tongue depressor (triggers laryngospasm)
Keep the child calm (avoid crying)
Prepare for emergency intubation/tracheostomy

ards-management.title: Prise en charge du SDRA
ards-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Prise en charge du SDRA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Lung-protective ventilation: Tidal volume 6mL/kg IDEAL body weight, Plateau pressure <30 cmH2O
Prone positioning improves V/Q matching and reduces mortality
Conservative fluid strategy (keep patient dry)
Address the underlying cause (sepsis, aspiration, pneumonia)

active-tb.title: Tuberculose active
active-tb.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tuberculose active, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Close contact with active TB case
clinicalPearls: Airborne precautions: N95 respirator for staff, negative pressure room for patient
3 consecutive negative sputum AFB cultures needed to discontinue airborne isolation
Rifampin turns body fluids orange/red (tears, urine, sweat) - warn patient
Monitor hepatic function regularly (LFTs) - hepatotoxicity is major risk

osa-management.title: Apnée obstructive du sommeil
osa-management.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Apnée obstructive du sommeil, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Weight loss can be curative in obese patients
Polysomnography (sleep study) is the gold standard for diagnosis
CPAP compliance is the key challenge - educate on proper use
Lateral sleeping position reduces airway collapse
Avoid alcohol and sedatives before bed (relax airway muscles)

respiratory-rpn.title: Respiratoire – RPN
respiratory-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratoire – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The right mainstem bronchus is wider, shorter, and more vertical than the left. This means aspirated objects and misplaced endotracheal tubes preferentially go RIGHT. After intubation, listen to BOTH lungs: absent left-sided breath sounds suggests right mainstem intubation.

SpO2 and PaO2 are related but not the same. SpO2 90% ≈ PaO2 60 mmHg (the 'cliff' on the oxyhemoglobin dissociation curve). Above this point, small changes in PaO2 cause minimal SpO2 changes. Below this point, small decreases in PaO2 cause DRAMATIC drops in SpO2.

In COPD patients, be cautious with high-flow oxygen. Some COPD patients rely on hypoxic drive for respiratory stimulation (their chemoreceptors have adapted to chronic hypercapnia and no longer respond to CO2). High-flow O2 may remove their drive to breathe. Target SpO2 88-92% (not 95-100%).

Asthma = REVERSIBLE airway obstruction. COPD = IRREVERSIBLE airway obstruction. This distinction is fundamental. Asthma patients should have normal PFTs between exacerbations. COPD patients have persistent airflow limitation.

Early signs of respiratory distress: Tachypnea, accessory muscle use, nasal flaring, tripod positioning, speaking in short sentences. LATE signs: Bradypnea (ominous: respiratory fatigue), cyanosis, altered mental status, silent chest (no air movement: impending respiratory arrest).

respiratory-rn.title: Respiratoire – IA
respiratory-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratoire – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Lung-protective ventilation saves lives in ARDS: Low tidal volume (4-6 mL/kg IBW), plateau pressure <30 cmH2O, permissive hypercapnia (tolerate PaCO2 up to 55-60 if pH >7.25). The ARDSNet trial showed 22% mortality reduction with low-VT strategy.

Prone positioning in ARDS improves oxygenation by redistributing perfusion to ventral (now non-dependent) regions that remain better aerated, improving V/Q matching. Proning must be done for ≥16 hours/day to show mortality benefit. Complications: facial edema, pressure injuries, accidental extubation, line displacement: requires dedicated prone team.

Chest tube troubleshooting: Continuous bubbling in water-seal = air leak. Check connections first (clamp tubing close to patient: if bubbling stops, leak is at patient's chest; if bubbling continues, leak is in the system). Sudden cessation of tidaling = tube obstruction (kinked, clotted) or lung has fully re-expanded.

Ventilator alarms: HIGH PRESSURE = obstruction (secretions, kinking, biting ETT, bronchospasm, pneumothorax, fighting ventilator). LOW PRESSURE = disconnection or leak (check circuit, cuff leak, chest tube bronchopleural fistula). Always go to the PATIENT first, not the ventilator.

The most dangerous time on a ventilator is extubation. Criteria for extubation readiness: Able to protect airway (gag/cough), adequate mentation (follows commands), FiO2 ≤40%, PEEP ≤5, spontaneous breathing trial passed (RSBI <105: ratio of RR to VT in liters), hemodynamically stable, no active sepsis or pending procedures. Keep reintubation equipment at bedside for 24-48 hours post-extubation.

respiratory-np.title: Respiratoire – IPA
respiratory-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Respiratoire – IPA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with frequent exacerbations
clinicalPearls: PFT interpretation algorithm: 1) Look at FEV1/FVC ratio first. <70% = Obstructive. Normal = check TLC. TLC low = Restrictive. TLC normal = normal spirometry. 2) If obstructive, check bronchodilator response: Reversible = Asthma. Irreversible = COPD. 3) Check DLCO: Low DLCO + obstruction = Emphysema. Normal DLCO + obstruction = Chronic bronchitis or Asthma.

Driving pressure (Plateau - PEEP) is emerging as the most important predictor of outcomes in ARDS. Aim for <15 cmH2O. When adjusting ventilator, consider whether changes improve or worsen driving pressure rather than focusing on individual VT or PEEP targets.

The 'open lung' approach in ARDS: Use enough PEEP to keep recruitable alveoli open (prevent cyclical opening/closing that causes shear injury), but not so much that overdistension occurs. Balance is key: there is no one-size-fits-all PEEP. Consider transpulmonary pressure monitoring for individualized PEEP titration.

ARDS management is primarily SUPPORTIVE: there is no pharmacologic therapy proven to reduce ARDS mortality. What works: lung-protective ventilation, prone positioning, conservative fluid management, treating the underlying cause. What doesn't work (despite theoretical rationale): surfactant, inhaled NO, steroids (controversial: may help in some subgroups), beta-agonists, statins.

VV-ECMO indications for ARDS: PaO2/FiO2 <80 for >6 hours despite optimization, OR pH <7.25 with PaCO2 >60 for >6 hours. Contraindications: Irreversible underlying condition, severe comorbidities, prolonged mechanical ventilation (>7-10 days: lung recovery less likely). ECMO is a BRIDGE: to recovery, to transplant, or to decision-making. It is not a destination therapy.

asthma-copd.title: Asthme et BPCO
asthma-copd.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Asthme et BPCO, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Asthma = REVERSIBLE obstruction. COPD = IRREVERSIBLE obstruction. However, they can coexist (Asthma-COPD Overlap: ACO). ACO features: significant bronchodilator reversibility + persistent airflow limitation + history of asthma and/or atopy.

A 'silent chest' in a severe asthma attack is an OMINOUS sign: it means there is so little air movement that wheezing cannot be generated. This is NOT improvement. It indicates impending respiratory arrest and requires immediate intervention (epinephrine, intubation preparation).

Step-up therapy in asthma: If symptoms are not controlled at the current step for >2-4 weeks, step up. If well-controlled for ≥3 months, consider stepping down. Always assess inhaler technique and adherence before stepping up: poor technique and non-adherence are the most common causes of 'uncontrolled' asthma.

COPD is the only chronic disease where the primary diagnostic test (spirometry) is also the staging tool. GOLD stage is based on post-bronchodilator FEV1 % predicted. But symptoms (CAT score or mMRC dyspnea scale) and exacerbation history determine the treatment GROUP (A, B, E in GOLD 2023).

Never give beta-blockers to asthma patients (even beta-1 selective: selectivity is dose-dependent). Beta-blockers block beta-2 receptors in bronchial smooth muscle, causing bronchospasm. In contrast, beta-blockers are generally safe and beneficial in COPD patients (cardioselective beta-1 blockers like metoprolol/bisoprolol).

pneumonia-basics.title: Pneumonia: Classification and Management
pneumonia-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pneumonia: Classification and Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Aspiration risk (dysphagia, decreased LOC, NG tube)
clinicalPearls: The #1 causative organism for CAP across all age groups is Streptococcus pneumoniae. Prevention: Pneumococcal vaccines (PCV20 for all adults ≥65 or younger with risk factors).

VAP prevention bundle (evidence-based, reduces VAP by 50%+): HOB elevation ≥30-45°, daily sedation vacation + readiness-to-extubate assessment, DVT prophylaxis, PUD prophylaxis, oral care with chlorhexidine (controversial in recent studies but still widely used). The most effective intervention is reducing the duration of intubation.

Elderly patients with pneumonia may NOT present with typical symptoms (fever, cough, pleuritic pain). Instead, they may present with CONFUSION as the primary symptom: acute mental status change in an elderly patient should prompt CXR and urinalysis.

Aspiration pneumonia is NOT the same as aspiration pneumonitis. Pneumonitis = chemical inflammation from gastric acid (sterile initially: antibiotics not needed unless secondary infection develops). Pneumonia = bacterial infection from aspiration of colonized oropharyngeal secretions (antibiotics needed).

De-escalation of antibiotics: Once culture and sensitivity results return (48-72 hours), narrow antibiotic coverage to the identified organism. This reduces antibiotic resistance development, C. difficile risk, cost, and side effects. Broad-spectrum → Narrow-spectrum based on cultures is a quality metric.

abg-basics.title: Gaz du sang artériel
abg-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Gaz du sang artériel, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: ROME: Respiratory = Opposite (pH and PaCO2 go in opposite directions), Metabolic = Equal (pH and HCO3 go in the same direction). This simple mnemonic helps identify the primary disorder.

In a fully compensated ABG (pH is normal), look at which side of 7.40 the pH falls: pH 7.35-7.39 suggests the primary disorder is acidosis (the body is slightly favoring the acid side despite compensation). pH 7.41-7.45 suggests the primary disorder is alkalosis. The body NEVER overcompensates.

A rising PaCO2 in an asthma patient is an OMINOUS sign. Asthma patients normally hyperventilate during exacerbations (respiratory alkalosis from increased work of breathing). A 'normalizing' or rising PaCO2 indicates respiratory muscle fatigue and impending respiratory failure: prepare for intubation.

Metabolic acidosis with a normal anion gap (hyperchloremic) = loss of bicarbonate (diarrhea, renal tubular acidosis, early renal failure). Metabolic acidosis with an elevated anion gap = accumulation of unmeasured acids. Remember MUDPILES: Methanol, Uremia, DKA, Propylene glycol, Isoniazid/Iron, Lactic acidosis, Ethylene glycol, Salicylates.

Compensation is NEVER complete for metabolic acidosis: the lungs cannot blow off enough CO2 to fully normalize pH in severe metabolic acidosis. Kussmaul respirations (deep, rapid breathing) represent maximal respiratory compensation for metabolic acidosis.

abg-interpretation-rn.title: Interprétation des GDS – IA
abg-interpretation-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Interprétation des GDS – IA, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Chronic respiratory disease with baseline CO2 retention
Metabolic emergencies (DKA, lactic acidosis, renal failure)
clinicalPearls: The anion gap is the single most important calculation in metabolic acidosis. It determines whether the acidosis is from acid ACCUMULATION (elevated AG: MUDPILES) or bicarbonate LOSS (normal AG: diarrhea, RTA). This distinction changes the entire diagnostic and treatment approach.

Always calculate the delta-delta ratio when the anion gap is elevated. A 'simple' DKA (AG = 28, HCO3 = 10) has a delta-delta of (28-12)/(24-10) = 16/14 = 1.14: pure AG metabolic acidosis. But if the same AG with HCO3 = 20, the ratio is 16/4 = 4.0: there's a hidden metabolic alkalosis (the patient is vomiting along with DKA).

Winters' Formula is your reality check for respiratory compensation in metabolic acidosis. If the PaCO2 doesn't match what Winters' predicts, there's a second respiratory disorder hiding. Example: HCO3 = 12, Winters' predicts PaCO2 = 1.5(12) + 8 = 26. If actual PaCO2 = 40, the patient also has respiratory acidosis (not compensating adequately: perhaps they have COPD or are too weak to hyperventilate).

In cardiac arrest, the ABG typically shows combined metabolic AND respiratory acidosis: the worst combination. The metabolic acidosis is from lactic acid (no perfusion → anaerobic metabolism). The respiratory acidosis is from no ventilation (no breathing = CO2 accumulation). Treatment: CPR (restores perfusion to clear lactate) + ventilation (eliminates CO2). Bicarbonate is NOT first-line.

Salicylate toxicity produces a classic MIXED disorder: respiratory alkalosis (direct stimulation of medullary respiratory center → hyperventilation) + metabolic acidosis (salicylic acid accumulation + uncoupled oxidative phosphorylation → lactic acidosis). The pH may be near-normal because the two disorders partially offset each other: but the patient is critically ill.

chest-tube-care.title: Soins du drain thoracique
chest-tube-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins du drain thoracique, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Continuous bubbling in the WATER-SEAL chamber = air leak. Continuous bubbling in the SUCTION CONTROL chamber = NORMAL (indicates proper suction level). These are two different chambers: knowing which chamber is bubbling is critical for assessment.

To determine if an air leak is from the patient or the system: Systematically clamp the chest tube at different points starting closest to the patient. If bubbling stops when clamped near the patient, the leak is from the patient (bronchopleural fistula). If bubbling stops when clamped near the drainage system, the leak is in the tubing/connections.

NEVER clamp a chest tube in a patient with a known air leak: clamping prevents air from escaping the pleural space and can cause tension pneumothorax. Clamping is only appropriate for brief assessment purposes when there is NO active air leak.
