If the drainage system is accidentally broken or falls: Immediately submerge the end of the chest tube in a container of sterile water to maintain a water seal. This prevents atmospheric air from entering the pleural space. Then set up a new drainage system.

When a chest tube is pulled out: Cover the site with a sterile petroleum gauze dressing taped on THREE sides only. This creates a flutter-valve effect: air can escape during expiration (through the untaped side) but cannot enter during inspiration (the dressing seals against the chest). Taping on all four sides can cause tension pneumothorax.

oxygen-therapy.title: Oxygen Delivery Devices and Therapy
oxygen-therapy.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oxygen Delivery Devices and Therapy, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: COPD (risk of suppressing hypoxic respiratory drive)
clinicalPearls: Rule of 4's for nasal cannula: Each liter adds approximately 4% FiO2 starting from 21%. 1L = 24%, 2L = 28%, 3L = 32%, 4L = 36%, 5L = 40%, 6L = 44%. This is an approximation: actual FiO2 varies with respiratory pattern.

Venturi mask is the device of choice for COPD patients because it delivers PRECISE, CONTROLLED FiO2 regardless of the patient's breathing pattern. This prevents inadvertent high FiO2 delivery that could suppress hypoxic drive in CO2-retaining COPD patients.

The non-rebreather mask reservoir bag must remain at least 2/3 inflated during inspiration. If it completely deflates, the patient is not receiving adequate oxygen. Check the flow rate and O2 source connection immediately.

HFNC is NOT just 'fancy nasal cannula': it provides physiologic PEEP (2-5 cmH2O), dead space washout, heated humidification, and can deliver up to 100% FiO2. The ROX index (SpO2/FiO2 divided by RR) helps predict HFNC failure: <3.85 at 6 hours predicts need for intubation.

Simple mask MINIMUM flow rate is 5 L/min. Below 5 L/min, the mask becomes a dead space trap where the patient rebreathes their own exhaled CO2: this can cause hypercapnia and respiratory acidosis. If the patient only needs 1-4 L/min, use nasal cannula instead.

tracheostomy-care.title: Soins de trachéostomie
tracheostomy-care.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Soins de trachéostomie, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: The obturator must be kept at the bedside and with the patient AT ALL TIMES. It is needed for emergency reinsertion of the tracheostomy tube. Without it, the rigid outer cannula tip could create a false passage during reinsertion.

Fresh tracheostomy (<7 days): The tract between the skin and trachea is NOT mature. If the tube comes out, DO NOT attempt reinsertion (risk of false passage into subcutaneous tissue → subcutaneous emphysema, loss of airway). Perform oral/nasal intubation instead. Mature tracheostomy (>7 days): The tract is epithelialized and can usually be recannulated.

Cuff pressure must be checked every 8 hours and maintained at 20-25 cmH2O. Think of it as the 'Goldilocks zone': Too low (<20) = aspiration risk and air leak. Too high (>25) = tracheal mucosal ischemia → ulceration → tracheomalacia → tracheal stenosis → tracheoesophageal fistula.

The most feared complication of tracheostomy is tracheo-innominate artery fistula (erosion into the innominate artery). It presents with pulsatile bleeding from the tracheostomy, often preceded by a small 'sentinel bleed.' Emergency management: Overinflate the cuff to tamponade bleeding, or insert a finger through the stoma and compress the artery against the posterior sternum. Mortality exceeds 80% even with emergent surgery.

When communicating with a tracheostomized patient who cannot speak (cuffed tube, no speaking valve), ALWAYS provide alternative communication methods: writing materials, picture/letter boards, electronic communication devices. Not being able to communicate is one of the most distressing experiences for patients. Assess the patient's communication ability and preference at the beginning of each shift.

rhonchi-rpn.title: Ronchi – RPN
rhonchi-rpn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Ronchi – RPN, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Post-operative patients with weak or ineffective cough
Immobility or prolonged bedrest (impaired secretion clearance)
Aspiration risk (dysphagia, decreased level of consciousness)
clinicalPearls: Rhonchi that clear with coughing are a key distinguishing feature - always reassess breath sounds after having the patient cough and document the change.
When reporting rhonchi to the RN, describe the specific location (which lung fields), timing (inspiratory, expiratory, or both), and whether they changed with coughing or repositioning.

Do not confuse rhonchi with stridor. Stridor is a high-pitched sound heard primarily on inspiration and indicates upper airway obstruction - this is a medical emergency requiring immediate intervention.

Positioning is a powerful nursing intervention for rhonchi. Elevating the head of bed and turning the patient to different positions uses gravity to assist with secretion drainage from affected lung segments.
Patients who are post-operative, immobile, or have neuromuscular weakness are at highest risk for developing rhonchi because they cannot cough effectively to clear normal airway secretions.

clinicalPearls: Turn every 2 hours
Nutrition: High protein and Vitamin C
Use pressure-redistribution surfaces

tinea-corporis.title: Tinea Corporis (Ringworm)
tinea-corporis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Tinea Corporis (Ringworm), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Direct contact with infected person or animal
clinicalPearls: Keep area clean and dry
Do not share personal items (towels, combs, clothing)
Complete full course of treatment even if symptoms resolve
Wash clothing and linens in hot water

oral-candidiasis.title: Oral Candidiasis (Thrush)
oral-candidiasis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oral Candidiasis (Thrush), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Rinse mouth after inhaled corticosteroids to prevent thrush
Sterilize pacifiers and bottle nipples for infants
Assess immune status if recurrent
Good oral hygiene is essential

oral-candidiasis-rn.title: Oral Candidiasis - RN Clinical Decision-Making
oral-candidiasis-rn.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oral Candidiasis - RN Clinical Decision-Making, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Inhaled corticosteroid use without mouth rinsing
Broad-spectrum antibiotic therapy disrupting normal flora
clinicalPearls: Always investigate underlying cause of recurrent oral candidiasis  -  may be first presentation of HIV
Esophageal candidiasis is an AIDS-defining illness and requires systemic antifungal therapy
Biofilm on dentures requires physical removal plus chemical disinfection  -  antifungals alone are insufficient
Nystatin contains sucrose  -  use fluconazole in diabetic patients when possible

oral-candidiasis-np.title: Oral Candidiasis - NP Prescriptive & Molecular Management
oral-candidiasis-np.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Oral Candidiasis - NP Prescriptive & Molecular Management, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Solid organ transplant on calcineurin inhibitors
Head and neck irradiation with mucositis
ICU patients with central venous catheters
Patients on biologics targeting IL-17 pathway (secukinumab)
clinicalPearls: Azole resistance mechanisms are cumulative  -  patients with prolonged azole exposure develop progressively higher MICs through sequential acquisition of ERG11 mutations and efflux pump upregulation
C. auris is an emerging global threat with resistance to all three major antifungal classes  -  requires contact precautions and aggressive environmental decontamination
Chronic mucocutaneous candidiasis in children/young adults warrants genetic testing for STAT1 gain-of-function, STAT3 mutations, or IL-17 pathway defects
Echinocandin MIC creep in C. glabrata is an evolving concern  -  always request susceptibility testing for non-albicans isolates

cdiff-basics.title: C. Difficile Infection
cdiff-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de C. Difficile Infection, incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Contact precautions with SOAP AND WATER - alcohol gel does NOT kill spores
Private room required
Bleach-based cleaning solutions required
Do not use antidiarrheals - they trap toxins in the colon

pertussis-basics.title: Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
pertussis-basics.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pertussis (Whooping Cough), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Infants too young for vaccination (< 2 months)
Waning immunity in adolescents and adults
clinicalPearls: Droplet precautions required
Infants may not produce the classic whoop but present with apnea
Tdap booster in pregnancy (27-36 weeks) protects newborn
Most contagious during catarrhal stage

atopic-dermatitis.title: Atopic Dermatitis (Eczema)
atopic-dermatitis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Atopic Dermatitis (Eczema), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Family history of atopy (asthma, allergic rhinitis, eczema)
clinicalPearls: Avoid triggers: harsh soaps, wool, extreme temperatures
Short lukewarm baths (not hot)
Cotton clothing preferred
Keep nails short to prevent excoriation

head-lice.title: Head Lice (Pediculosis Capitis)
head-lice.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Head Lice (Pediculosis Capitis), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Treat ALL household contacts and close contacts simultaneously to prevent re-infestation cycle.
Wash all bedding, clothing, and towels in HOT water (130°F/54°C) and dry on high heat. Items that cannot be washed should be sealed in plastic bags for 2 weeks.
Do NOT share combs, brushes, hats, hair accessories, helmets, or pillows. Educate children about not sharing these items at school.
Nits found >1/4 inch from scalp are likely non-viable (already hatched or dead): focus treatment on nits close to the scalp.

pinworms.title: Pinworms (Enterobiasis)
pinworms.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Pinworms (Enterobiasis), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
clinicalPearls: Tape Test (Scotch Tape Test) is the diagnostic method: apply clear adhesive tape to perianal area FIRST thing in morning before bathing or bowel movement, then examine under microscope for eggs.
Treat ALL family members/household contacts simultaneously, even if asymptomatic, to break the cycle of reinfection.
Strict hand hygiene: wash hands thoroughly with soap and water after toileting and before eating. Keep fingernails trimmed very short.
Wash all bedding, towels, and undergarments in HOT water daily during treatment period. Avoid shaking linens (disperses eggs into air).

diaper-candidiasis.title: Diaper Candidiasis (Diaper Yeast Infection)
diaper-candidiasis.overview: Esta lição abrange os aspectos essenciais de Diaper Candidiasis (Diaper Yeast Infection), incluindo a fisiopatologia, a avaliação clínica, as intervenções de enfermagem prioritárias e o manejo baseado em evidências. Os enfermeiros devem compreender esses conceitos para garantir cuidados seguros e eficazes.
riskFactors: Oral thrush (Candida spreads through GI tract)
clinicalPearls: Key differentiator from regular irritant diaper rash: Satellite lesions (papulopustules beyond the rash border) and involvement of skin FOLDS (irritant rash typically spares skin folds).
Keep diaper area clean and DRY: change diapers frequently (at least every 2 hours and immediately after soiling). Allow diaper-free air exposure time when possible.
Check for concurrent oral thrush (white patches on buccal mucosa/tongue). If present, treat both simultaneously to prevent reinfection cycle via GI tract.
Teach parents: Apply antifungal cream FIRST, then barrier cream (zinc oxide) on top if needed. Do NOT use cornstarch (promotes fungal growth). Avoid baby powder (aspiration risk).
